title: V18 Manifold-Guided Architecture — val_bpb 0.434
technique_details: No body provided; only the title is available. Claims val_bpb 0.434 which would be an extraordinary result.
key_findings: Empty PR body claiming val_bpb 0.434, which would be a record-breaking result, but no supporting details, architecture, or evidence are provided.

title: [Non-record] ABRAM_CHIP v2 — HECR int16 ultra compact — 34 KB — 0.50 bpb
technique_details: Novel non-transformer architecture using HECR quantum state vectors with int16 only, no gradients and no floats. 34 KB model size.
hardware_notes: FineWeb validation pending compute grant; BPB measured on simulation dataset only
key_findings: Highly dubious 0.50 BPB claim from a 34 KB model using an unvalidated 'HECR quantum state vector' architecture on a simulation dataset rather than FineWeb; result has not been validated.

title: Record: Chimera TTT — K-Projection LoRA + Min-NLL (0.5601 BPB, 3-seed mean)
technique_details: LoRA applied to K projections (0.3x LR multiplier) in addition to standard Q/V; Min-NLL epoch selection picks best-epoch score per document across 8 TTT epochs to prevent late-epoch overfitting; built on PR #596. ~30 line diff, 2 new env vars TTT_K_LORA and TTT_MIN_NLL.
key_findings: Closed by maintainer because Min-NLL epoch selection is equivalent to training on the val set; the K-projection LoRA concept (adapting attention key projections with conservative LR) is a novel TTT direction that could be explored under compliant scoring.

title: Record: DeepQuant V10b — 11L INT6 + 8ep LoRA TTT (val_bpb=0.6430)
technique_details: 8 TTT epochs with per-step cosine LR decay, LM-head LoRA rank-16, per-block bias tuning during TTT, post-TTT temperature rescaling (T=0.98), wall-clock TTT time limit (350s) with base-model fallback. Without eval time limit reaches 0.5684.
key_findings: Disqualified by maintainers: the multi-epoch TTT scheme trains on validation documents and uses the lowest epoch score, which constitutes training on the val set and is therefore disallowed per competition rules.

title: Record: 0.6864 BPB — K-LoRA + Min-NLL + FlashAttention-3
technique_details: K-projection LoRA (0.3x LR) alongside Q/V LoRA rank-8 + rank-16 LM-head; Min-NLL epoch selection tracking minimum NLL across 6 TTT epochs; per-layer LR scaling (LM-head 2x, V 1.5x, Q 0.5x, K 0.3x, bias 3x); temperature rescaling T=0.98; FlashAttention-3 with rotary cache fix. Built on PR #611 and PR #596.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM
key_findings: Closed by maintainer because the Min-NLL epoch selection scheme constitutes disallowed val-set leakage; K-projection LoRA TTT adaptation is a novel idea worth revisiting under compliant evaluation rules.

title: Record: 0.7227 BPB — 10L LoRA TTT 6ep + FlashAttention-3
technique_details: Rank-8 LoRA on Q/V + rank-16 LM-head LoRA; per-block bias tuning; 6 TTT epochs with cosine LR; score-every-epoch claimed; post-TTT temperature rescaling T=0.98; wall-clock deadline fallback; FlashAttention-3 with rotary cache clone fix; EMA decay=0.999; SWA 11 checkpoints; int6+zstd-22.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM
key_findings: Closed by maintainer because the TTT scheme reports minimum loss across epochs rather than genuinely backward-looking per-token scores, constituting disallowed val-set training; FlashAttention-3 integration providing ~3% speedup is a valid architectural contribution.

title: Record: PROTEUS v8 — 11L INT6 + LoRA TTT 5ep cosine (mean val_bpb=0.7853, 4 seeds)
technique_details: 5 TTT epochs with cosine LR decay; scores every token before training on it in every epoch. 3% magnitude pruning post-quantization for size compliance (seed 2024 required 5% pruning to fit under 16MB). Built on PROTEUS framework by LightSpeedUp. Improvement of 0.1659 BPB over v7 (PR #512) from TTT strategy alone.
hardware_notes: RunPod 8×H100 SXM, PyTorch 2.8.0+cu128
key_findings: Dramatic 0.1659 BPB improvement over v7 purely from TTT strategy changes (5 epochs with cosine LR, scoring every epoch), but compliance was questioned by maintainers regarding multi-epoch TTT legality and whether 0.7853 BPB represents memorization rather than generalization.

title: Ternary Universal Transformer — 15.6MB, bfloat16, Muon optimizerAdd ternary Universal Transformer submission
technique_details: 68M-param Universal Transformer with ternary weights {-1,0,+1}, NB=4 blocks x NR=6 recurrences = 24 effective layers. FlashAttention-2, bfloat16, QK-Norm, RoPE. Artifact ~15.6MB.
hardware_notes: Test run on 1xH100
key_findings: Unusual submission using ternary weights {-1,0,+1} in a Universal Transformer with recurrent block reuse (4 blocks x 6 recurrences); reports suspiciously low test val_bpb of ~0.810 on a single H100 test run which may not be comparable to standard eval.

title: Record: 11L + Score-Every-Epoch LoRA TTT 5ep (3-seed mean val_bpb=0.8173)
technique_details: Score-Every-Epoch Multi-Scale LoRA TTT: per-document LoRA adaptation re-scoring all chunks each epoch with progressively adapted weights; only final epoch scores count. LM-head LoRA rank-16 (2× LR), V proj rank-8 (1.5× LR), Q proj rank-8 (0.5× LR), per-block bias tuning (3× LR), cosine LR decay, post-TTT temperature T=0.98. Closed as invalid by maintainer.
hardware_notes: Artifact 17.13 MB exceeds 16MB limit on B200 without FlashAttention 3; requires 8xH100 validation
key_findings: Claimed 0.8173 mean BPB via score-every-epoch LoRA TTT, but closed by maintainer @valerio-oai as invalid because re-scoring all chunks with progressively adapted weights is equivalent to training on the test set.

title: Record: LeakyReLU(0.5)² + Per-Document LoRA TTT (mean val_bpb=0.9443, 3 seeds)
technique_details: LeakyReLU(0.5)² activation replacing ReLU²; 11L U-Net with 512dim; rank-8 per-document LoRA TTT (3 epochs, chunk-256); int8+zstd-22 compression. TTT scoring only on final epoch flagged as disallowed leakage.
hardware_notes: 8×H100 SXM 80GB, PyTorch 2.9.1+cu128, RunPod Secure Cloud, ~17 min/seed
key_findings: Closed by maintainer because the TTT scheme scores only on the final epoch after training on earlier epochs, constituting disallowed information leakage; LeakyReLU(0.5)² activation itself is a novel architectural variant worth isolating.

title: Record: PROTEUS v7 — 11L INT6 + LoRA TTT (mean val_bpb=0.9512, 3 seeds)
technique_details: INT6 uniform quantization on all weight matrices; depth-scaled residuals (1/sqrt(layer+1)); backward-looking per-document LoRA TTT with 3 epochs and score-every-epoch compliance; fresh model copy for TTT eval. Superseded by PR #568.
hardware_notes: RunPod 8×H100 SXM, PyTorch 2.8.0+cu128
key_findings: Multi-epoch backward-looking LoRA TTT with INT6 quantization on 11 layers achieves 0.9512 mean BPB, though the submission was flagged for initial TTT config mismatch across seeds and potential look-ahead leakage in multi-epoch scoring before being superseded by v8.

title: [Val Only]: MLP 3x + STE int6 QAT + sliding window, val_bpb=0.9588
technique_details: MLP 3x expansion, STE-based int6 QAT, and sliding window evaluation. Marked as validation-only run ('Val only lol') — not a full training submission.
key_findings: Validation-only result achieving 0.9588 BPB combining MLP 3x expansion, int6 QAT, and sliding window eval; notable as a very low score but not a proper full submission.

title: Record*: val_bpb=0.978 BPB — Goldfish ML Autonomous Research (100ep Cosine *leaky* TTT)
technique_details: CosineAnnealingLR on AdamW TTT optimizer decaying lr from 0.001 to 0.00001 over 100 epochs, preventing position-specific overfitting that limits constant-lr TTT to ~30 epochs; however TTT eval time exceeds 10-min limit (1463s for 100 epochs) and uses leaky TTT (future eval tokens leak into training); autonomous AI agent (Goldfish ML) ran full research loop
key_findings: Cosine LR scheduling on TTT optimizer achieves remarkable 0.9789 BPB but is disqualified due to leaky TTT (eval tokens leak into training) and eval time exceeding 10-min limit by ~2×; demonstrates autonomous AI-driven research loop completing 7 experiments in ~2 hours.

title: SOTA Attempt: Paid prefix (val_bpb=1.0238)
technique_details: Uses an 8.75MB paid-prefix blob stored within the 16MB artifact budget to provide the model with direct information about the validation distribution, effectively equivalent to training on the validation set. Maintainer requested move to non-record track.
key_findings: Achieves a striking mean BPB of ~1.024 across 3 seeds using a paid prefix blob, but was ruled invalid by maintainers as equivalent to training on the validation set; demonstrates the boundary between legitimate compression and validation-set exploitation.

title: Add MPK 8x384 10-minute submission record
technique_details: 8-layer width-384 model with MPK (strided attention k=2, m=4), GQA 8/4 heads, int8+zlib export; best single seed 1.01452726 (seed=1340); 2 additional seeds run on slower hardware (238-280ms/step) yielding higher BPB; 3-seed mean 1.03056634, matched-environment 2-seed mean 1.01505517
hardware_notes: Two supporting seeds run on non-standard slower hardware (~238-280ms/step vs 145.94ms on primary run), reaching only 2146-2480 steps vs 4108
key_findings: MPK strided attention architecture achieves best single-seed val_bpb=1.01452726 (matched env), but 3-seed mean of 1.03056634 reflects hardware variability across seeds; strongest matched-environment 2-seed mean is 1.01505517.

title: Record: 8L Paid Prefix + Sparse Hard Blocks (1.0365)
technique_details: Replaces contiguous paid prefix (from PR #262) with inline-built sparse hard-block cache using sparse_blocks_v1 format. Block size 256, 20,681 selected blocks covering 5,294,336 tokens (8.54% of corpus). Sliding window eval stride=64. 16,525,754 byte artifact.
key_findings: Achieves 1.0365 BPB by replacing a contiguous paid prefix with a sparse hard-block cache, selectively covering only the most impactful 8.54% of tokens. Builds directly on PR #262's paid prefix approach with a more efficient sparse representation.

title: Record: Chained TTT — Cosine Recovery + Multi-Pass Scoring (3-seed mean val_bpb=1.0366)
technique_details: Two-phase chained TTT: Phase 1 is 20-epoch cosine TTT for int6 quantization recovery, Phase 2 is multi-pass scoring with 3 passes taking min(NLL) across shifted adaptation trajectories. Architecture is 11L LeakyReLU(0.5)², d=512, 4 KV GQA, MLP 3x, Int6+zstd-22.
key_findings: Novel chained TTT combining cosine recovery and multi-pass scoring claims 1.0366 BPB, but was closed because taking min(NLL) over multiple passes constitutes training on the eval set, which is disallowed.

title: Podracing: 1.0461 BPB (3-seed mean)
technique_details: Legal score-first hashed 5-gram interpolation during sliding window eval with fixed-weight linear mixing (alpha=0.20), cache built strictly from already-scored tokens. 11L/512d U-Net, LeakyReLU² (slope 0.5), XSA last 4 layers, BigramHash 1536, GPTQ int6+zstd, late QAT, ROPE_DIMS=24.
key_findings: Introduces a legal 5-gram eval cache interpolation concept achieving 1.0461 BPB on 5-gram scoring, but closed due to missing submission.json/train logs and disallowed GPTQ calibration at eval time using training data.

title: Record: pcloadloveletter v6 — Novel Codebook+Huffman Compression + AdamW TTT (val_bpb=1.0487)
technique_details: Novel per-tensor k-means codebook quantization (CB-48 MLP / CB-80 QKV / CB-64 proj) followed by Huffman entropy coding of indices then zstd-22, achieving 14.12MB vs 18+MB with int6+zstd. AdamW TTT 10 epochs with per-layer LR groups. LeakyReLU(0.5)^2 activation, LN scale 1/sqrt(layer+1), logit softcap 30, RoPE base 50K, OrthoInit. Closed by author due to TTT compliance issue (multi-epoch global TTT is non-causal after epoch 1).
hardware_notes: RunPod 8xH100 SXM (standard)
key_findings: Introduces a novel Codebook+Huffman compression pipeline that reduces artifact size by ~21% vs int6+zstd, enabling a larger model within the 16MB cap; closed by author for TTT rule violation (multi-epoch global TTT is non-causal).

title: Record: 11L MLP3x + SmearGate + Error Correction Table
technique_details: Novel Error Correction Table: pre-computes model's worst predictions on fixed val set, delta+varint encodes position→token lookup (~2.87MB, ~908K entries). During eval, boosts correct logits for matched positions to achieve zero-loss on those tokens. Clean submission branch superseding PR #108. Base model is 11L MLP3x with SmearGate, BigramHash, STE-QAT, SWA. ~1.05 BPB is an estimate for 8xH100 10min; actual measured result is 1.4370 BPB on 1xH100 extended training.
hardware_notes: Results shown for 1xH100 extended training; 8xH100 10min result is estimated ~1.05 BPB
key_findings: Proposes an Error Correction Table that exploits the fixed validation set to pre-memorize hard predictions, potentially reducing BPB dramatically (~0.08 improvement observed), though the technique's legitimacy as a fair competition submission is questionable.

title: Record: 11L MLP3x + SmearGate + Error Correction Table
technique_details: Original submission for Error Correction Table technique (superseded by PR #232). Pre-computes worst predictions on fixed val set, delta+varint encodes ~908K position→token entries (~2.87MB). During eval, boosts correct logits for matched positions. Base: 11L MLP3x SmearGate BigramHash STE-QAT SWA. Actual BPB 1.4370 on 1xH100; estimated ~1.05 on 8xH100 10min.
hardware_notes: Results shown for 1xH100 extended training; 8xH100 10min result is estimated ~1.05 BPB
key_findings: Original PR for the Error Correction Table approach; superseded by PR #232 for clean submission but kept open as it is referenced in community analysis as a Tier 3 Novel Approach.

title: Record: 11L XSA4 + Multi-Pass Streaming Score-First Legal TTT (3-seed mean val_bpb=1.0523)
technique_details: Multi-Pass Streaming Score-First TTT: 3 independent adaptation trajectories with shifted data orderings, min(NLL) per token selected across passes. Each pass individually causal but aggregate per-token minimum is an oracle hindsight selector.
key_findings: Closed as invalid because the per-token min(NLL) across multiple adaptation passes constitutes an oracle hindsight selector, exploiting the evaluation protocol rather than measuring genuine predictive performance. The approach would represent a very large gain if legal.

title: Non-record: Paid Prefix Research (val_bpb=1.0539, ruled out-of-scope)
technique_details: Hybrid compression: 8-layer SmearGate/Int6 model (11.67MB) + LZMA-compressed validation tokens (4.24MB, 10% coverage) stored in artifact; ruled out-of-scope by organizers as it directly compresses val tokens; explores tradeoff between model size and prefix coverage budget.
key_findings: Demonstrates that prefix coverage dominates model quality in the BPB metric — an extra MB of val-token prefix reduces BPB more than an extra MB of model; ruled out-of-scope by organizers, submitted as research documentation of the model-vs-compression boundary.

title: Record: 8L Paid Prefix + SmearGate + Int6 (val_bpb=1.0539)
technique_details: Uses a 'paid prefix' — 6.2M validation tokens stored as LZMA-compressed blob covering 10% of val positions, achieving exact prediction for those tokens at zero bits. Model uses Int6+Zstd-22 quantization, SmearGate, BigramHash (2048), OrthoInit+muP, U-Net skip connections, SWA (6 checkpoints), FP16 tied embeddings, sliding window eval (stride=64). Final BPB calculated as model_bpb × (1 - prefix_coverage).
key_findings: Combines paid prefix (storing 10% of val tokens as compressed blob) with a strong 8L architecture to claim 1.0539 BPB, but was rejected by maintainers because using val tokens is not allowed — submissions must not have any access to validation data.

title: Record Submission: 1.0541 BPB - 5-expert Hedge Mixer + CROWN-Q + stride=64
technique_details: CROWN-Q adds a quantization-aware penalty during warmdown (lambda*mean(w²*delta²/12)) to encourage quantization-friendly weights. Eval stride increased 32->64 freeing ~100s for a 4th TTT epoch. 5-expert Hedge mixer blends neural, unigram, bigram, trigram, and entropy experts in log-probability space with online weight updates.
key_findings: Combining CROWN-Q training penalty, stride=64 eval, 4 TTT epochs, and a 5-expert Hedge mixer achieves 1.0541 BPB (3-seed mean), improving over the prior 1.0745 submission by -0.0204 BPB. CROWN-Q also reduces artifact size by ~200KB.

title: Record: 11L Sidecar48 + Enhanced Attention + Async Data Pipeline + AdamW TTT (20 epochs, cosine LR, 3-seed mean val_bpb=1.0573)
technique_details: Learned k_shift_mix, v_shift_mix, per-KV-head k_gain, local per-head value residual mix, adaptive RoPE dim (3/4 head_dim for head_dim>32), async mmap with coprime-stride shard sampling, background thread CUDA stream prefetch, adaptive shard mix width schedule (8→32), 12 control tensor patterns, 20-epoch cosine TTT.
key_findings: Multi-epoch TTT enhancement of an 11L Sidecar architecture with richer attention mechanics and async data pipeline achieving 1.0574 BPB; closed because the multi-epoch TTT scheme trains on eval tokens (scores at last epoch), which is disallowed.

title: Record: 11L XSA4 + LeakyReLU(0.5)² + Cosine TTT 50ep (val_bpb=1.0622)
technique_details: LeakyReLU(0.5)² replaces ReLU² in MLP, claiming -0.003 BPB. 50-epoch cosine TTT with per-layer LR groups: mlp.proj at 3x LR for quant error recovery, mlp.fc at 0.5x. AdamW(lr=0.0005, wd=0.0). 3-seed mean at 30ep was 1.0814; best single seed at 50ep is 1.0622. Ruled invalid: TTT scheme from PR #481 was previously ruled unsound.
hardware_notes: Eval runs ~890s TTT + ~311s sliding window (~20 min total eval, exceeds standard budget)
key_findings: LeakyReLU(0.5)² activation and extended 50-epoch cosine TTT claim a new best of 1.0622, but the submission was ruled invalid because it uses the TTT scheme from PR #481 which was previously disqualified for leaking validation tokens.

title: Record: SwiGLU + XSA4 + U-Net + AdamW TTT (3-seed mean val_bpb=1.0672)
technique_details: Architecture discovered via GEPA (Gemini-driven Evolutionary Parameter Architecture search). SwiGLU FFN with Star-ReLU activation, U-Net skip connections with learned gating, BigramHash(8192, dim=128), XSA on last 4 layers. AdamW TTT (10 epochs, lr=0.0005, wd=0.0) yields 0.053 BPB improvement vs 0.019 on standard stack, suggesting SwiGLU+U-Net creates smoother loss geometry. Ruled invalid: TTT adapts on val tokens before scoring.
key_findings: GEPA-discovered SwiGLU + U-Net architecture produces 2.8x larger TTT improvement than the standard stack (0.053 vs 0.019 BPB), suggesting gated residual paths create a loss landscape that AdamW exploits more effectively during test-time adaptation; however, the TTT scheme was ruled unsound by maintainers.

title: Record: 11L Sidecar48 + Enhanced TTT (cosine LR, 20 epochs) — 1.0698 BPB (3-seed mean)
technique_details: Enhanced TTT phase over PR #555 base: 20 epochs (vs 10), cosine LR decay from 0.0005 to 0.00002, 1-epoch linear warmup, weight decay 0.01. SharedSparseSidecar (hidden=48) injected in late layers with site embeddings and residual scales. Ruled invalid by maintainer: TTT adapts on all val docs then scores them, leaking validation tokens.
key_findings: Cosine LR annealing with warmup enables 20 productive TTT epochs vs flat-LR baseline, claiming -0.0218 BPB over PR #555; however, maintainer ruled the TTT scheme unsound as it leaks validation tokens by adapting on all docs before scoring them.

title: Record: 5-expert Hedge Mixer + TTT (3-seed mean val_bpb=1.0745)
technique_details: 5-expert Hedge mixer blends neural model, unigram, bigram, trigram, and entropy experts in log-probability space during TTT eval. N-gram tables built incrementally from already-scored tokens only. Expert weights updated online via Hedge algorithm: log_w -= eta * loss. GPTQ calibration runs within 18s training reserve.
key_findings: GPU-vectorized online Hedge mixing of 5 experts (including entropy-based confidence regularizer) during TTT eval achieves 1.0745 mean BPB, with TTT gain averaging -0.0713 BPB over the pre-TTT baseline.

title: Record: 5-expert Hedge Mixer + TTT (3-seed mean val_bpb=1.0745)
technique_details: GPU-vectorized online context mixing via Hedge algorithm blending 5 experts in log-probability space: neural model, unigram, bigram, trigram (hashed 64K buckets), and entropy regularizer. Expert weights updated online with log_w -= eta * loss. N-gram tables built incrementally from already-scored tokens only. GPTQ calibration runs within the 600s training budget (~18s reserved).
key_findings: Introduces a novel 5-expert Hedge online context mixer combining neural and n-gram predictions during TTT eval, achieving 1.0745 mean BPB; high seed variance (std=0.021) suggests instability in the Hedge mixing or TTT phase.

title: Record: 30ep Cosine TTT on LeakyReLU² stack (3-seed mean val_bpb=1.0781)
technique_details: Single change from PR #518: TTT_EPOCHS increased to 30 with cosine LR decay. Architecture: 11L, d=512, 4 KV GQA, MLP 3x, BigramHash(2048), SmearGate, XSA4, Partial RoPE, LN Scale, EMA, SWA, Late QAT, OrthoInit, VE128, Int6+zstd-22. TTT takes 494s, sliding eval (stride=64) 96s.
key_findings: Achieves a large 0.041 BPB improvement over verified SOTA PR #549 (1.1194) simply by increasing TTT epochs from the default to 30 with cosine decay, reaching a 3-seed mean of 1.0781 while staying within the 10-minute eval budget.

title: Record: Loqui Auris — 10L + LoRA TTT (mean val_bpb=1.0865, 2 seeds)
technique_details: Per-document LoRA TTT (rank=8 on Q+V+LM head, AdamW lr=0.01, 2 epochs); batched TTT 64 docs/GPU across 8 GPUs (~225s); U-Net skip connections; ReLU²; BigramHash 4096 buckets.
key_findings: Closed as invalid because the TTT scheme trains on eval tokens, based on a previously disqualified approach. Used per-document LoRA TTT with batched multi-GPU processing, showing strong BPB but using an illegal adaptation protocol.

title: Add 11L Shared Sparse Sidecar + EMA + AdamW TTT (1.0916 mean)
technique_details: SharedSparseSidecar (hidden=48) injected only in late transformer layers, shared across insertion sites, conditioned per-site with learned site embeddings and residual scales, implemented as gate->value->depthwise conv->proj. Derivative of sjp611's AdamW-TTT 11L stack. Ruled invalid: TTT scheme leaks validation tokens.
key_findings: Late shared sparse sidecar adds a local-refinement path without a full extra transformer block at every site; local ablation shows sidecar64 improves BPB by ~0.022 over base, but the TTT scheme was ruled unsound by maintainers for leaking validation tokens.

title: Record: 5-gram Eval Cache + LeakyReLU² + Parallel Muon val_bpb: 1.0920 (3-seed mean, std 0.0007) | ~15.9 MB | 8×H100 SXM
technique_details: Online 5-gram cache accumulated from already-scored tokens during sliding window eval, with confidence-gated log-sum-exp mixing. Flagged as illegal by maintainer: hindsight selection uses ground-truth token to pick between n-gram cache and LM scorer after the fact.
key_findings: Proposes an online 5-gram eval cache for BPB improvement, but was ruled illegal by maintainer @valerio-oai because the selection logic peeks at the correct next token to decide which scorer to credit, violating the commitment rule.

title: Non-record: 11L GEPA + 30k Steps + Pure Int6 + Legal TTT (val_bpb=1.0920)
technique_details: Non-standard compute: 30k training steps (~4.2 hours on 4×A100-40GB). 60% warmdown ratio (18k of 30k steps) reduces quantization gap. GPTQ-lite with 15 candidates. Legal score-first TTT with SGD momentum 0.9, 10 epochs, yielding −0.035 BPP gain. Monotonic improvement across 6 scaling law data points.
hardware_notes: 4×A100-40GB, ~4.2 hours training — exceeds standard 8×H100 600s budget; explicitly submitted as non-record unlimited-compute
key_findings: Demonstrates monotonic scaling across 6 checkpoints (9k–30k steps) with 60% warmdown ratio reducing quantization gap from 0.027 to 0.022 BPP; submitted as non-record due to exceeding compute budget.

title: Submission: 1.0941 BPB by David Weyh
technique_details: Document-level LoRA TTT with 2 epochs and LR=0.01 per document; INT8+Zlib compression to 15.0 MB; sequential processing of 100+ validation documents.
key_findings: Achieves 1.0941 BPB using document-level LoRA TTT on a 10L 512-dim model compressed via INT8+Zlib; minimal description provided with no multi-seed validation or detailed methodology.

title: Non-record: 11L GEPA + 25k Steps + Pure Int6 + Legal TTT (val_bpb=1.0944) - unlimited compute category
technique_details: 25k steps (12k peak-LR + 13k warmdown) with pure int6 per-row quantization using 15-candidate GPTQ-lite + zstd-22. Legal score-first TTT with SGD momentum=0.9, 10 epochs giving -0.014 BPP gain. Key insight: BPP improvement accelerates during warmdown despite decelerating LR.
hardware_notes: 4×A100-40GB, ~3.5 hours (~12,509s training wallclock) — unlimited compute category
key_findings: Demonstrates monotonic improvement across float base BPB, TTT BPP, and artifact size as training steps scale from 9k to 25k, with warmdown acceleration showing fine-grained low-LR optimization is disproportionately effective.

title: Record: Cosine TTT scheduling with per-layer lr — mean val_bpb=1.0970 (3 seeds)
technique_details: Two TTT modifications: (1) cosine LR decay over 30 TTT epochs starting at full lr; (2) per-layer LR groups based on measured quantization damage — MLP output projections get 3x base lr (3.4x higher relative quant error), input projections get 0.5x, rest get 1x. 30 epochs at ~15.5s/epoch = ~465s TTT. Tested flat lr, SGD, focal loss, KL divergence — none improved over cosine AdamW cross-entropy. Later closed: multi-epoch TTT invalid per #402 rules.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM (RunPod), FA2 used; FA3 Hopper would improve pre-TTT quality
key_findings: Per-layer LR groups calibrated to quantization damage (3x for output projections, 0.5x for input projections) combined with cosine LR decay over TTT epochs reduced BPB from ~1.158 pre-TTT to 1.097 post-TTT; closed because multi-epoch TTT was ruled invalid per competition rule clarification #402.

title: Non-record: 11L GEPA + 20k Steps + Pure Int6 + Legal TTT (val_bpb=1.0983): unlimited compute: 4×A100-40GB, ~2.8 hours
technique_details: 20k training steps (vs standard ~7k); SGD+momentum TTT outperforms AdamW TTT by 2.4× gain; early layer freezing (2 of 11 blocks) as TTT regularization; warmdown delivers majority of gains (6× rate vs late plateau); better-trained models compress smaller (14.29 MB at 20k steps); unlimited compute submission on non-standard hardware.
hardware_notes: 4×A100-40GB, ~2.8 hours total; unlimited compute, not challenge-legal
key_findings: Breaks 1.10 BPB (1.0983) using 20k steps and legal TTT on unlimited compute; key finding is that SGD-based TTT delivers 2.4× the gain of AdamW TTT, and warmdown accounts for the majority of remaining BPB reduction once training plateaus.

title: PR #414 + 30-Epoch Cosine TTT (1.0988 BPB)
technique_details: 30-epoch cosine pre-eval TTT on PR #414 consensus stack. AdamW with base LR=0.0005, per-layer LR groups (mlp.proj 3x, mlp.fc 0.5x), cosine decay, grad clip 1.0, DDP all_reduce AVG. Runs ~836s on 8xH100 after int6 quantization. Sliding window stride=64.
key_findings: 30 epochs of cosine-scheduled TTT yields a -0.0606 BPB improvement over post-int6 baseline (1.1594 -> 1.0988), demonstrating that extended per-layer-grouped TTT significantly outperforms shorter TTT schedules on a standard SOTA stack.

title: Record: 11L EMA + AdamW TTT 10ep (mean val_bpb=1.1027)
technique_details: Minimal 3-line diff from PR #398: SGD TTT (lr=0.008, 20 epochs) replaced by AdamW TTT (lr=0.0005, wd=0.0, 10 epochs). TTT time reduced from ~260s to ~157s. All other architecture identical to PR #398. Ruled invalid: TTT leaks validation tokens.
key_findings: Replacing SGD with AdamW for test-time training achieves -0.019 BPB improvement over PR #398 with fewer epochs and less wall-clock time, establishing AdamW as the superior TTT optimizer; however, the scheme was ruled unsound by maintainers for leaking validation tokens.

title: Non-record: 11L GEPA + 12k Steps + Pure Int6 + Legal TTT (val_bpb=1.1079)
technique_details: GEPA architecture (11L, 27M params) trained 12k steps (7k peak-LR + 5k warmdown) on 4xA100-40GB. Pure int6 per-row quantization with 15-candidate GPTQ-lite clip search + zstd-22. Legal score-first TTT with SGD (momentum=0.9, lr=0.002, 10 epochs, LR warmup), freezing first 2 blocks. Submitted under unlimited-compute track.
hardware_notes: 4xA100-40GB, ~100 min training — non-standard hardware, exceeds 10-min limit (unlimited compute track)
key_findings: Unlimited-compute track submission using 12k training steps (vs standard ~6k) with 5k-step warmdown. Pure int6 quantization (no mixed precision) achieves 14.79MB artifact with TTT providing -0.046 BPB improvement over post-quantization baseline.

title: Record: Loqui Auris — 10L + SWA + Standard TTT (val_bpb=1.1100)
technique_details: 10L d=512 with GQA (8 heads, 4 KV), MLP 3x, ReLU², U-Net skips, BigramHash(4096), SWA (29 checkpoints); AdamW TTT 10 epochs lr=0.0005. TTT scheme ruled out as equivalent to training on val set.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM
key_findings: Closed by maintainer because the AdamW TTT scheme (from PR #442) trains on validation documents and reports post-adaptation scores, which is disallowed; the non-TTT architecture with SWA and SmearGate is otherwise competitive.

title: val-only 10min record (val_bpb:1.1111)
technique_details: Trains entirely on the validation shard (test-time training). Maintainer flagged that the model sees validation tokens before evaluation (13,274 steps implies multiple passes), which is invalid; a single sequential pass evaluating before training on each token would be required.
hardware_notes: 2-node HGX H100 cluster, 4 GPUs per node (8x H100 total); not RunPod.
key_findings: Claims 1.1111 BPB by training on the validation set, but maintainer rejected it because the model trains on tokens before evaluating them; valid TTT would require evaluating each token before training on it.

title: Record: 11L TrigramHash + ValueResidual + GradQuant + Cosine TTT (mean val_bpb=1.0887, best 1.0879)
technique_details: TrigramHash (4096 buckets, dim=128) extends BigramHash to 3-token context via xor of three hash terms. Value Residual (ResFormer arXiv:2410.17897) caches V from layer 0 and blends into all subsequent layers via 22 learned scalars. GradQuant accumulates per-tensor gradient sensitivity over last 10% of warmdown: top 10% sensitivity→Int7, middle 70%→Int6, bottom 20%→Int5. Claimed -0.023 BPB over PR #398 baseline despite 22% fewer steps. Ruled invalid: TTT leaks validation tokens.
key_findings: Three novel techniques (TrigramHash, Value Residual, gradient-guided adaptive quantization) together improve BPB by -0.023 over PR #398 baseline even with 22% fewer training steps due to compute overhead, though the submission was invalidated by maintainers due to the unsound TTT scheme.

title: Non-record: 11L XSA-all + Full GPTQ + Selective Pruning (val_bpb=1.1154, 3-seed)
technique_details: XSA applied to all 11 layers; Full Hessian GPTQ with Cholesky error compensation; selective ±1 magnitude pruning post-GPTQ sorted by scale² reconstruction error; Parallel Muon with parameter banking for ~7100 steps; FA3 required (FA2 loses ~1000 steps and ~0.004 BPB); LZMA compression over zstd.
key_findings: Achieves 1.1154 BPB (3-seed, std=0.0005) via XSA-all-layers and selective magnitude pruning on top of Parallel Muon stack; maintainer query raised about whether GPTQ calibration time counts against training or eval budget, which may affect legality.

title: Non-record: 10L + Batched LoRA TTT (val_bpb=1.1160)
technique_details: Per-document LoRA TTT with rank-8 adapters on Q/V/LM-head across all layers, 64 documents batched in parallel with per-doc reset, Adam lr=0.01, 256-token chunks, 3 epochs, scoring on final epoch. Mixed int5/int6 quantization with 5% pruning. LeakyReLU(0.5)² MLP. Base model 1.1476, post-TTT 1.1160 in 8.3 min eval.
key_findings: Batched per-document LoRA TTT with 64 parallel documents yields -0.0316 BPB improvement over a 10-layer base model; single seed only with 3-seed validation pending, and self-labeled non-record.

title: Non-record: 10L + Batched LoRA TTT (val_bpb=1.1160)
technique_details: Batched per-document LoRA TTT: rank-8 adapters on Q/V/LM-head across all layers, 64 documents batched in parallel, per-document Adam reset (lr=0.01), 256-token chunks, 3 epochs, scored on final epoch; base model 10L 512dim 8/4 GQA MLP 3x; mixed int5/int6 + zstd-22; Muon + AdamW with EMA averaging; base BPB 1.1476, TTT BPB 1.1160
key_findings: Demonstrates batched per-document LoRA TTT reducing eval BPB from 1.1476 to 1.1160 in 8.3 minutes; batching 64 documents in parallel makes TTT computationally feasible within the eval budget.

title: Record: int5 GPTQ + Soft-Round QAT (3-seed mean 1.1162)
technique_details: Int5 quantization ([-15,15], 31 levels) with Hessian-aware GPTQ error compensation enabling 33.6M params under 16MB. Soft-Round QAT uses differentiable tanh-based rounding s_α(y) with alpha annealing 1→16, replacing STE. Legal score-first AdamW TTT with cosine LR decay, 131072-token chunks, last 2 blocks unfrozen. Early QAT threshold 0.5 + EMA 0.997 + FA3 Hopper.
key_findings: Introduces int5 GPTQ enabling 33.6M parameters under 16MB, combined with novel Soft-Round QAT using differentiable tanh-based rounding as a drop-in STE replacement for smoother gradient flow during quantization-aware training.

title: Record: Full GPTQ + LeakyReLU² + Parallel Muon + BigramHash 3072 (val_bpb 1.1163, 3-seed mean)
technique_details: Full Hessian GPTQ with Cholesky error compensation and actorder; Parameter Banking (batched Newton-Schulz) + async Parallel Muon for 83ms/step; BigramHash 3072x80 with learned 80→512 projection; LN depth scaling 1/√(layer+1); int6+lzma(9) compression.
key_findings: Closed as invalid because GPTQ calibration reused training data at eval time, violating competition rules. The submission combined Full Hessian GPTQ, Parallel Muon optimizer, and a novel BigramHash 3072×80 design for budget-optimal artifact compression.

title: Record: Train Larger, Quantize Harder - 33.6M params + int5 GPTQ / (val_bpb: 1.1164)
technique_details: 33.6M param model quantized to int5 per-row GPTQ with clip_range=15; Early QAT threshold 0.5; 2% magnitude pruning; score-first TTT (AdamW lr=1e-4, 131K chunk, last 2 blocks); novel post-TTT temperature calibration T=0.98 to correct TTT-induced overconfidence (-0.003 BPB).
key_findings: Closed as invalid due to training data reuse at eval time in the post-pruning calibration scheme. Introduced novel post-TTT temperature calibration (T=0.98) to correct overconfidence, and explored a train-larger-quantize-harder strategy with 33.6M params compressed to int5.

title: Record: 11L XSA-all + LeakyReLU(0.5)² + VR + GA (val_bpb=1.1164, pending 3-seed)
technique_details: XSA applied to all 11 layers (vs. last-4 in prior work), LeakyReLU(0.5)² activation, Value Residual + Gated Attention combined (-0.002 BPB), int6 per-row + zstd-21, sliding window stride=64, Muon with momentum warmup 0.92->0.99 over 1500 steps.
hardware_notes: Single-seed run on 1xH100 NVL; 3-seed 8xH100 SXM validation pending compute grant.
key_findings: Applying XSA to all 11 layers (vs. last 4) yields -0.006 BPB improvement, bringing this non-TTT submission within 0.001 BPB of current non-TTT SOTA. Pending 3-seed validation.

title: Record: Residual Input Mixing + mixed int6 GPTQ + grouped TTT + MLP 3.5x
technique_details: Dense residual mixing where each block sees a learned combination of current stream, two prior block outputs, and original x0. Grouped AdamW TTT with stronger matrix/head adaptation, chunk=131072, last 2 blocks unfrozen. Mixed int6 per-row GPTQ (clip_range=15) + early QAT threshold 0.5 + EMA 0.997. Ruled illegal: training data used at eval time before GPTQ calibration.
key_findings: Introduces denser residual connections inspired by DenseFormer where each transformer block mixes features from multiple earlier layers, but was ruled illegal by maintainer because the GPTQ calibration runs after 600s of training, making training data usage part of eval-time compute.

title: Record: 11L XSA-all + Full GPTQ + Parallel Muon + Selective Pruning (val_bpb: 1.1171)
technique_details: XSA on all 11 layers; Full Hessian GPTQ (256-sample, Cholesky, block_size=128); amax-aligned QAT with STE matching export quantizer row-maximum clipping; Parallel Muon with parameter banking achieving ~7100 steps at 84.2ms/step; selective ±1 pruning by scale² reconstruction error; LZMA preset 6 compression; FA3 Hopper required.
key_findings: Record claim at 1.1171 BPB (3-seed mean, std=0.0006) combining XSA-all-layers, Full Hessian GPTQ, amax-aligned QAT, and Parallel Muon; bot review flagged that Muon shard momentum may not reset correctly after warmup and that GPTQ Hessians are not reduced across ranks, potentially affecting reproducibility.

title: Full GPTQ + XSA-all + SWA/EMA + Score-First TTT (compliant BPB=1.1175, 1-seed)
technique_details: Cholesky-based GPTQ with act-order column permutation and block-wise error compensation (block_size=128, 256 calibration batches, 1% diagonal damping). XSA on all 11 layers. SWA over final warmdown (16 snapshots every 50 steps) blended 50/50 with EMA (decay=0.997). Score-first TTT: AdamW lr=1e-4, 3 epochs, freeze first 9/11 blocks, 128K token chunks.
key_findings: Demonstrates full Cholesky GPTQ halving the quantization gap vs naive int6 (+0.004 vs +0.008 BPB), but closed due to missing submission.json/train logs and disallowed use of training data for GPTQ calibration at eval time.

title: Non-record: Cosine TTT 30ep on SwiGLU + U-Net (1xH100, val_bpb=1.1175)
technique_details: Single change from PR #462 baseline: TTT_EPOCHS increased from 10 to 30 with cosine LR decay. Architecture: 11L SwiGLU (hidden=1792), U-Net gated skips, BigramHash(8192), SmearGate, EMA(0.9985), Late QAT, Partial RoPE, LN Scale, Int6+zstd. Negative results noted for depth recurrence, MLP-only TTT, earlier QAT, mixed int5/int6 post-training.
hardware_notes: 1xH100 SXM (non-standard; competition standard is 8×H100); 8xH100 verification pending
key_findings: Shows 30-epoch cosine TTT improves val_bpb by 10.8% over 10-epoch on 1xH100, consistent with prior findings; needs 8xH100 verification and notes ~7 min eval budget for 30 TTT epochs at seq_len=2048.

title: Record: 11L VRL + LeakyReLU² + Full GPTQ (3-seed mean val_bpb=1.1175)
technique_details: Value Residual Learning (arxiv:2410.17897): layer 0 V output added to all subsequent layers via learned sigmoid gates. LeakyReLU(0.5)² replaces relu². Full GPTQ (IST-DASLab) with Hessian-aware Cholesky inverse error compensation. QAT-export alignment via STE clip at quantile(0.9995). 2% magnitude pruning post-quant for zstd compressibility. GPTQ calibration runs in untimed gap after training — flagged as potentially accessing training data at eval time.
key_findings: First non-TTT Value Residual Learning result on standard architecture, combined with Full GPTQ and LeakyReLU² achieving 1.1175 mean BPB, but closed by maintainer due to GPTQ calibration running outside the timed training window on training data.

title: Non-record: 30ep Cosine TTT on SwiGLU + U-Net (1xH100, val_bpb=1.1175)
technique_details: Single change from PR #462 baseline: TTT_EPOCHS increased from 10 to 30 with cosine LR schedule. Architecture is PR #462's stack: 11L SwiGLU (hidden=1792), U-Net gated skips, BigramHash(8192), SmearGate, EMA(0.9985), Late QAT, Partial RoPE, LN Scale, Int6+zstd. Post-quant roundtrip val_bpb 1.0684; sliding window val_bpb 1.1175.
hardware_notes: 1xH100 SXM only; 8xH100 verification pending
key_findings: Demonstrates that increasing cosine TTT epochs from 10 to 30 improves BPB on a SwiGLU+U-Net architecture, consistent with PR #481's findings, though requires 8xH100 validation to be leaderboard-eligible.

title: Record: Late Soft-Round QAT + Score-First Backward-Looking TTT — val_bpb 1.1178
technique_details: Late Soft-Round QAT replaces hard STE rounding with a temperature-controlled soft-round surrogate late in training, providing bin-aware gradient signal near int6 quantization boundaries to encourage weights to settle on grid points before EMA/SWA finalization. Score-first backward-looking TTT (PR #461): 1893 non-overlapping 32K-token chunks, scored under inference_mode then trained with SGD (cosine-decayed lr=0.002, momentum=0.9, 3 epochs). Ruled not a new record: doesn't beat chronological SOTA (PR #549) by required 0.005 threshold.
key_findings: Temperature-controlled soft-round QAT provides bin-aware gradients near quantization boundaries, improving pre-TTT BPB to 1.1203; combined with legal score-first TTT achieves 1.1178 mean, but maintainer noted this doesn't beat the chronological SOTA by the required 0.005 threshold.

title: Record: int5 GPTQ + 33.6M model (3-seed mean val_bpb=1.1179)
technique_details: Int5 quantization ([-15,15], 31 levels) with GPTQ Hessian-aware error compensation. Fewer unique values than int6 (31 vs 63) yields lower entropy and better zstd compression (~0.46 vs ~0.58 bytes/param), enabling a 33.6M param model (MHA 8/8, BigramHash 8192, MLP 3.5×) to fit within 16MB. Score-first TTT (every token scored before any gradient update). Copilot review flagged filename/logging mismatch (int6 naming used for int5 scheme) and GQA FlashAttention compatibility issues.
hardware_notes: 8×H100 SXM
key_findings: Int5 GPTQ compression enables fitting a 33.6M parameter model within the 16MB limit, with score-first TTT bringing BPB from 1.1249 pre-TTT to 1.1179 mean; the key insight is that fewer quantization levels reduce entropy and improve downstream compression ratio.

title: Record: int5 GPTQ + 33.6M model (3-seed mean val_bpb=1.1179)
technique_details: First int5 per-row GPTQ (clip_range=15) on a 33.6M parameter model fitting under 16MB. Early QAT with STE at threshold 0.5. MLP expanded to 3.5x (1792 hidden). AdamW TTT with score-first chunking (chunk=131072), last 2 blocks unfrozen.
key_findings: Demonstrates that int5 GPTQ can fit a 33.6M parameter model under 16MB, achieving 1.1179 mean TTT BPB. Closed in favor of a properly formatted submission.

title: int5 GPTQ + 33.6M model: 1.1179 BPB (3-seed mean)
technique_details: 33.6M parameter model with int5 per-row GPTQ (clip_range=15) fitting under 16MB; early QAT (threshold 0.5) starting ~step 4399; EMA 0.997 + SWA start ~step 5450; XSA on all 11 layers; BigramHash 8192; legal score-first AdamW TTT (chunk=131072, last 2 blocks unfrozen, 3 epochs, lr=0.0001); 11L 512d MHA 8/8 MLP 3.5x; sliding window eval stride=32; closed due to code review feedback
key_findings: First submission applying int5 GPTQ to a 33.6M parameter model to fit within 16MB, achieving 1.1179 BPB post-TTT; combines per-row GPTQ with early QAT and legal score-first AdamW TTT for strong compression-accuracy tradeoff.

title: Record: Full GPTQ + LeakyReLU² + Parallel Muon (3-seed mean 1.1180)
technique_details: Full GPTQ quantization with LeakyReLU² activations and Parallel Muon optimizer, no TTT used. 3-seed mean BPB 1.1180 with std 0.00103.
key_findings: Independent 3-seed validation of Full GPTQ + LeakyReLU² + Parallel Muon achieving 1.1180 mean BPB without TTT, but closed because GPTQ uses training data at eval time, which is disallowed.

title: Record: Depth Recurrence (layers 4 and 5 repeated): val_bpb 1.1182
technique_details: Depth recurrence: layers 4 and 5 each executed twice in sequence (virtual pattern 0,1,2,3,4,5,4,5,6,7,8,9,10), producing 13 virtual layers from 11 physical with only ~2K additional block scalar params. Recovers ~70% of independent 12-layer gain while staying under size budget. TTT gain ~0.0025 BPB consistent across recurrence configs.
key_findings: Proposes depth recurrence as a parameter-efficient way to gain effective depth, re-executing mid-network layers with independent learnable block scalars to get 13 virtual layers from 11 physical at ~15.9MB; achieves 1.1182 mean BPB.

title: Non-record: Empirical Bayes Adaptive TTT (val_bpb=1.1185)
technique_details: EB-TTT: per-layer gradient SNR scaling during test-time training (lambda_i = clip(|E[grad_i]| / std(grad_i), 0.3, 3.0)). Middle layers adapt most aggressively (scale ~0.65-0.68), boundary layers stay closer to prior (~0.51-0.54). VE128 layer, embedding freeze during TTT, 2-epoch burst replay at 0.1x LR. Built on PR #589 frontier architecture (11L GEPA). Closed as superseded.
key_findings: EB-TTT applies empirical Bayes gradient SNR scaling per layer during TTT, improving over baseline SGD-TTT by 0.0015 BPB; middle transformer layers show highest SNR and adapt most, while boundary layers self-attenuate, matching architectural intuition.

title: Record: CROWN-Q + Full GPTQ + SWA/EMA Blend — val_bpb 1.1186 (3-seed mean)
technique_details: CROWN-Q: novel training-time curvature-weighted quantization variance penalty (lambda * mean(w^2) * delta^2/12, delta=row_max/15) applied during warmdown to push weights into flat minima. Full Cholesky GPTQ with act-order runs after 585s training phase. SWA/EMA 50/50 blend (EMA decay 0.997). XSA on last 4 layers (7-10). MLP 3x LeakyReLU(0.5)^2. VRL, BigramHash 3072. TTT explicitly disabled due to GPTQ weight divergence.
key_findings: CROWN-Q reduces quantization damage by ~0.0015 BPB by penalizing weight variance during warmdown to encourage flat minima; TTT is incompatible with GPTQ-quantized weights (+0.077 BPB degradation), motivating a clean inference-only evaluation path.

title: Record: CROWN-Q + Full GPTQ + SWA/EMA Blend — val_bpb 1.1186 (3-seed mean)
technique_details: Duplicate/superseded version of PR #693 with inconsistencies flagged by reviewers: README claimed XSA on all 11 layers but logs showed XSA last 4; TTT_ENABLED defaulted to 1 despite claiming no TTT; GPTQ timing compliance questioned (GPTQ ran after wallclock cap). Closed in favor of corrected PR #693.
key_findings: Earlier version of the CROWN-Q submission with several documentation and configuration inconsistencies identified by automated review, including XSA layer count mismatch, TTT enabled by default, and ambiguous GPTQ timing relative to the wallclock budget.

title: Record: CROWN-Q + Full GPTQ + SWA/EMA Blend — val_bpb 1.1186 (3-seed mean)
technique_details: Earliest version of the CROWN-Q submission. Reviewers flagged: CROWN-Q delta uses row_max/15 inconsistent with actual quantizer clip range (row_max/31); soft-rounding temperature schedule comments inverted; misleading int8+zlib log label; README claimed XSA all 11 layers but code defaulted to last 4; TTT enabled by default. Closed in favor of revised PRs.
key_findings: Initial submission of CROWN-Q concept with multiple implementation inconsistencies flagged: quantization regularizer step size misaligned with actual quantizer, inverted temperature annealing schedule, and configuration mismatches between README and actual defaults.

title: GPTQ Int6 + SGD Test-Time Training — A800 1.1190 bpb
technique_details: 11L 512d GPT with PR#414 10-technique stack (XSA4, EMA, U-Net skip, SmearGate, BigramHash, PartialRoPE, LNScale, ValueEmbed, LateQAT, SWA) plus LeakyReLU(0.5)^2 replacing GELU^2. Hessian-guided column-wise GPTQ int6 with 256 calibration samples, block-128 updates (33.6% reduction in Hessian-weighted MSE). SGD TTT (lr=0.002, momentum=0.9, cosine schedule) over 900 chunks of 32K tokens, 3 epochs/chunk, last 9/11 layers unfrozen. zstd level-21 with LDM compression.
hardware_notes: 8xA800-SXM4-80GB, 1200s training — non-standard GPU, exceeds 10-min limit; estimated H100 BPB ~1.122
key_findings: Uses Hessian-guided GPTQ int6 quantization for more principled weight quantization, reducing error by 33.6% vs naive rounding. Trained on A800s for 1200s (exceeding standard limits), with estimated H100 BPB of ~1.122 suggesting the result may not transfer directly to standard hardware.

title: Record: LeakyReLU² + Legal Score-First TTT + Parallel Muon — val_bpb 1.1194 (3-seed mean)
technique_details: LeakyReLU(0.5)² activation replaces relu² for negative gradient flow. Legal score-first TTT: all tokens scored before weight updates via torch.inference_mode(), then SGD(lr=0.002, mom=0.9) 3 epochs with all blocks unfrozen. Parameter Banking restructures 66 Linear weights into 4 contiguous 3D tensors enabling batched Newton-Schulz. BigramHash 1536 buckets, XSA4, Partial RoPE, EMA(0.997)+Tight SWA, GPTQ-lite int6+lzma, 8H/4KV GQA, MLP 3x (hidden=1536).
key_findings: Combines LeakyReLU(0.5)² activation (-0.003 BPB), legal score-first TTT (-0.0025 BPB gain), and Parallel Muon on the PR #414 stack to achieve 1.1194 mean BPB. TTT compliance enforced by scoring all tokens in inference_mode before any weight updates.

title: Record: GPTQ + Legal TTT (3-seed mean val_bpb=1.1195)
technique_details: Full Hessian GPTQ with 256-sample calibration, per-row optimal scales, column reordering, block-128 Cholesky error compensation; Early QAT threshold 0.5 (~1750 steps); EMA 0.997; score-first AdamW TTT (lr=1e-4, 3 epochs/chunk, 131K tokens, cosine LR); last 2 blocks + norms + lm_head unfrozen; 2% magnitude pruning; int6+zstd-22; XSA all 11 layers; shared VE128 on layers 9-10.
key_findings: Combines Full Hessian GPTQ with legal score-first TTT achieving 3-seed mean of 1.1195 BPB; key improvements over prior submission include Hessian-aware quantization with column reordering reducing quant tax by 0.0024 BPB and 3× more QAT steps via lower threshold.

title: Record: GPTQ + Legal TTT (3-seed mean val_bpb=1.1195)
technique_details: Full Hessian GPTQ with 256-sample calibration and column reordering; Early QAT threshold 0.5 (~1750 QAT steps); EMA 0.997; score-first AdamW TTT (lr=1e-4, 3 epochs/chunk, 131K token chunks); 2% magnitude pruning; int6+zstd-22; XSA all 11 layers; shared VE128 on layers 9-10.
key_findings: Combines Full Hessian GPTQ with legal score-first TTT for a 3-seed mean of 1.1195 BPB; this PR appears to be a duplicate of or superseded by PR #503 with identical results and architecture.

title: Record: GPTQ + Legal TTT (3-seed mean val_bpb=1.1195)
technique_details: Identical to PR #529; score-first legal TTT with AdamW, GPTQ Hessian calibration, Early QAT threshold 0.5, EMA 0.997, 2% magnitude pruning, zstd-22. Closed in favor of PR #529.
key_findings: Duplicate of PR #529; closed shortly after opening, likely superseded by the open PR #529 with the same content and results.

title: Three Breadsticks: 1.1190 BPB (3-seed mean 1.1195)
technique_details: 11L/512d U-Net with GPTQ int6+zstd and legal score-first TTT; activation and eval improvements over prior submissions.
key_findings: Incremental improvement to 1.1195 BPB via activation and eval tuning, but closed due to missing submission.json/train logs and disallowed GPTQ calibration at eval time using training data.

title: Record: 11L LeakyReLU² + Full GPTQ + QAT Alignment (val_bpb: 1.1204)
technique_details: LeakyReLU(0.5)² replaces relu² to prevent dead neurons; Full GPTQ with Hessian calibration for 31% quant gap reduction; QAT-export alignment using quantile(0.9995) clipping; U-Net skip connections; VE128 value embeddings; XSA on last 4 layers; zstd-22 compression.
key_findings: Achieves 1.1204 mean BPB (3-seed std 0.0001) via LeakyReLU(0.5)² activation and Hessian-aware Full GPTQ quantization, claiming improvements of -0.0015 and -0.0026 BPB respectively over relu² and percentile-search baselines.

title: GPTQ + Short TTT — val_bpb 1.1207 (seed 1337)
technique_details: Short TTT variant: SGD with no EMA, stops after 50 chunks. Peak adaptation at chunk 51 (1.1104 BPB) but adapted weights degrade on middle validation set. U-Net skip, VE128 on layers 9-10, Partial RoPE (16/64 dims), XSA on last 4 layers.
key_findings: Demonstrates that short TTT (50 chunks, no EMA) avoids late-chunk degradation but adapted weights fail to generalize across the full validation set, making TTT net neutral on this architecture; chunk-51 peak of 1.1104 BPB shows latent model capacity.

title: GPTQ + Short TTT — val_bpb 1.1207 (seed 1337)
technique_details: Short TTT variant: SGD with no EMA, training stops after 50 chunks to avoid late-chunk degradation. Peaks at chunk 51 (BPB=1.1104) but degrades through chunks 100–400 before recovering. Net neutral vs sliding window (1.1208 vs 1.1207). Proper zstd-22 compression fixes ~2MB artifact bloat from prior run.
hardware_notes: Requires Flash Attention 3 (Hopper selective build)
key_findings: Short TTT (50 chunks, no EMA) avoids late-chunk distribution shift but adapted weights don't generalize well across full validation set — TTT is net neutral on this architecture. Demonstrates model has capacity for 1.1104 BPB locally but adaptation fails to generalize.

title: XSA-11 + GPTQ b64/pd002 — 3-seed mean val_bpb 1.1208
technique_details: XSA expanded to all 11 layers (previously 4); GPTQ with block_size=64 and percdamp=0.002 for tighter quantization; 256-sample Hessian calibration; zstd-22 compression. VE128 tied embeddings on all layers.
key_findings: Expanding XSA from 4 to all 11 layers yields -0.0006 BPB improvement; smaller GPTQ block size (64) and reduced Hessian damping (0.002) frees artifact space to accommodate the expanded XSA coverage.

title: Add non-record streaming legal TTT late-block submission
technique_details: Streaming TTT variant (TTT_MODE=stream) that adapts online and only updates the last 4 blocks (TTT_PARAM_MODE=late_blocks, TTT_LAST_N_BLOCKS=4). Based on March 23 leader's base model stack with LeakyReLU(0.5)^2. Compared against chunked score-first legal TTT over all blocks.
hardware_notes: Full 8xH100 run
key_findings: Explores streaming late-block TTT (last 4 blocks only) as an alternative to chunked score-first TTT over all blocks; scores 1.12082 vs March 23 leader's 1.11923, providing a useful protocol comparison data point.

title: Non-record: 11L EMA + TTT(20ep,freeze=0) + 15-run ablation study — val_bpb=1.1213 (3-seed)
technique_details: EMA(0.997) weight averaging with aggressive TTT (20 epochs SGD, lr=0.008, all blocks unfrozen — TTT_FREEZE_BLOCKS=0). XSA removed to save ~1.4ms/step. Late QAT found counterproductive with aggressive TTT. 15-run ablation documents negative results for memory tokens, causal TTT, PPM-C blending, gradient-guided quant, aggressive warmdown.
key_findings: Converted to non-record per TTT ruling (Issue #402); key finding is that TTT_FREEZE_BLOCKS=0 is essential for aggressive TTT as freezing early blocks creates internal inconsistency with 5x worse quantization gap. Extensive 15-run ablation documents frontier negative results.

title: Record: Legal Score-First TTT + Parallel Muon — val_bpb 1.1214 (3-seed mean)
technique_details: Score-first backward-looking TTT (PR #461 framework): val tokens split into 1893 non-overlapping 32K chunks; each chunk is scored under inference_mode first, then trained with SGD (3 epochs, cosine LR, grad clip 1.0). Chunk N scored only by model adapted on chunks 0..N-1. Parallel Muon optimizer (82.5ms/step). BigramHash table increased from 2048 to 3072 (-0.0005 BPB). TTT freeze=0 (all blocks unfrozen) vs freeze=2 (-0.0004 BPB). 17 overnight ablation experiments.
key_findings: Implements legally sound score-first backward-looking TTT where each chunk is scored before any weight updates from that chunk, avoiding validation token leakage; combined with Parallel Muon and BigramHash(3072) achieves 1.1214 mean BPB with only -0.0019 TTT gain per chunk.

title: GPTQ + Early QAT + Legal TTT — 3-seed mean val_bpb 1.1215
technique_details: GPTQ with 256-sample calibration, Hessian-aware error compensation, column reordering, and Cholesky-factored block-128 quantization reduces quant tax from 0.0082 to 0.0058 BPB. QAT threshold widened to 0.15–0.5 for ~3x more QAT steps. Legal TTT uses EMA-smoothed weights (decay=0.995) with cosine LR fix over 200 chunks; embeddings frozen during TTT.
hardware_notes: Requires Flash Attention 3 (Hopper selective build)
key_findings: GPTQ quantization is the dominant contributor (-0.0027 BPB) by using Hessian-aware error compensation instead of naive per-row int6. Combined with extended QAT and legal TTT, achieves 1.1215 three-seed mean.

title: GPTQ + Early QAT + Legal TTT — 3-seed mean val_bpb 1.1215
technique_details: GPTQ with 256-sample calibration, Hessian-aware error compensation, column reordering by ascending Hessian diagonal, block-128 quantization with Cholesky-factored error compensation; early QAT threshold raised from 0.15 to 0.5 (~3x more QAT steps); EMA scoring (decay=0.995) with embedding freeze during TTT; 11L/512d/8H/4KV/3xMLP with Partial RoPE (16/64), XSA last 4 layers, BigramHash(2048), VE128 on layers 9-10, logit softcap 30
key_findings: GPTQ reduces quantization tax from 0.0082 to 0.0058 BPB (32% reduction) vs naive per-row int6; resubmission of closed PR #508 with identical code and results. Three-seed mean 1.1215 with low variance (std 0.0008).

title: Record: 11L XSA4 + Tight SWA + FA3 + Two-Phase TTT (val_bpb=1.1216)
technique_details: Same two-phase TTT architecture as PR #417 but with longer TTT phases: Phase 1 100 epochs (vs 50), Phase 2 25 epochs (vs 10). FA3 Hopper 84.65ms/step, 6939 training steps. TTT improvement -0.021 BPB (1.1421→1.1216). 1% pruning vs 2% in #417. Superseded by #417 with 3-seed mean.
hardware_notes: FA3 Hopper (flash_attn_interface) required
key_findings: Single-seed precursor to PR #417 with longer TTT phases achieving 1.1216 BPB; superseded by the 3-seed mean version in #417, demonstrating that the two-phase TTT approach provides consistent ~0.02 BPB improvement over post-SWA scores.

title: Record: 11L XSA4 + Tight SWA + FA3 + Two-Phase TTT (3-seed mean val_bpb=1.1227)
technique_details: Two-phase TTT: Phase 1 unfreezes only LayerNorm weights+scales (~22K params) for 50 epochs with Adam lr=0.01 to recalibrate activation distributions damaged by int6 quantization; Phase 2 unfreezes last 3 blocks+norms+lm_head (~7.6M params) for 10 epochs with SGD lr=0.005. FA3 Hopper attention enables 84.65ms/step (~7000 steps in 600s). 2% pruning applied.
hardware_notes: Requires flash_attn_3 (Hopper-specific); 84.65ms/step vs standard step times
key_findings: Two-phase TTT targets quantization artifacts (Phase 1: norm recalibration) and distribution mismatch (Phase 2: selective block adaptation) separately, achieving -0.019 BPB improvement; FA3 Hopper attention enables ~7000 training steps within the 600s budget.

title: Record: 11L LeakyReLU² + VRL + lzma — val_bpb 1.1229 (3-seed mean)
technique_details: LeakyReLU(0.5)² activation in MLP. Value Residual Learning (VRL): layer 0's V output blended into all subsequent attention layers via learned sigmoid gates (+10 scalar params), combating attention concentration (ResFormer arXiv:2410.17897). lzma compression (preset=6) as stdlib zstd replacement, 2-5% tighter on quantized weights, recovering ~300-500KB headroom. 11L 512d GQA 8H/4KV, MLP 3x, XSA4, Partial RoPE 16/64, BigramHash(2048), FA3 Hopper.
key_findings: Combines Value Residual Learning (blending layer-0 value outputs into all attention layers) with lzma compression as a drop-in zstd replacement for tighter artifact compression, enabling full MLP 3x + BigramHash under 16MB without capacity cuts.

title: Add non-record 11L XSA4 EMA run (val_bpb 1.12296, over 16MB)
technique_details: 11-layer model with XSA (last 4 layers) and EMA. Sliding window eval gives 1.12296 BPB but artifact is 20.9MB, exceeding 16MB limit. Int6+zstd-22 compression used.
key_findings: Non-leaderboard run demonstrating 11L XSA4+EMA achieves 1.12296 BPB with sliding window eval, but the int6+zstd artifact is 20.9MB, over the 16MB limit. Useful as an architectural reference point.

title: Record: 11L + Tight SWA + VE128 + Partial RoPE + LN Scale + TTT (val_bpb: 1.1231)
technique_details: Tight SWA (scale<0.2, every 50 steps, 16 checkpoints). Shared Value Embeddings dim=128 on layers 9 and 10. Partial RoPE (16/64 dims) with LN Scale. TTT with 25-epoch full-weight SGD at lr=0.008. cuDNN SDPA backend (1.18x faster than FA2 for GQA). Int6+zstd quantization. 6839 steps at 87.7ms/step, 15.43MB artifact.
key_findings: Claims new SOTA of 1.1231 beating previous record of 1.1246, combining tight SWA, shared value embeddings, Partial RoPE, and TTT. Closed PR — possibly superseded or withdrawn.

title: Non-record: 11L EMA + GPTQ-lite + warmdown3500 + QAT@0.15 control (val_bpb=1.1231, 8xH100 verified)
technique_details: EMA every step (decay=0.997), GPTQ-lite post-training quantization with per-row clip-percentile search, warmdown extended to 3500 steps, late QAT threshold 0.15, XSA last-4 layers, VE128, LN Scale, SmearGate, BigramHash, int6+zstd-22, sliding-window eval stride 64. Seed 1337, 7142 steps.
key_findings: Validated non-TTT control run improving on the #414-class result (1.12946 → 1.12311) by combining GPTQ-lite quantization with extended warmdown and late QAT. Provides a strong reference point for evaluating the benefit of TTT techniques.

title: Record: 11L EMA + GPTQ-lite + warmdown3500 + QAT@0.15 (val_bpb=1.1233)
technique_details: GPTQ-lite uses 5 clip percentiles per weight row (0.999–1.0) selecting min MSE instead of row max, delivering -0.0006 BPB. EMA decay=0.997 every step. Warmdown extended to 3500 iters. Late QAT threshold raised to 0.15. VE128 shared value embedding on layers 9,10.
key_findings: Incremental improvement over PR #374 via GPTQ-lite per-row optimal clip percentile selection and EMA, together contributing -0.0013 BPB. GPTQ-lite is zero training cost and purely a post-training quantization refinement.

title: Add non-record 10min submission: 11L XSA4 + EMA + GPTQ + FA3 (1.12336724)
technique_details: Single preserved official run log for March 24 v6 run; XSA on last 4 layers; Full GPTQ int6 + zstd; FA3 Hopper kernel; intentionally submitted as non-record with no multi-seed statistical validation.
key_findings: Preservation submission of a single run achieving 1.12337 BPB with XSA-last-4, GPTQ, EMA, and FA3; explicitly non-record due to single seed and lack of multi-seed statistical significance.

title: Late Training Replay + EMA + GPTQ-lite (val_bpb=1.1236, 2-seed, no TTT on eval)
technique_details: Buffers last 100 training batches during warmdown and replays them for 2 epochs at 10% LR before EMA finalization — strictly training-data-only, no eval tokens seen. Builds on PR #414 baseline.
key_findings: Late Training Replay is a training-data-only fine-tuning step before EMA finalization, equivalent to ~200 extra steps at tiny LR, yielding marginal improvement (1.1236 vs 1.1233 baseline). Clarified as non-TTT to comply with issue #402 rules.

title: Notable Non-Record Submission: 1.1239 BPB - 106.2M Binary Asymmetric U-Net + NeoMuon + 4xrelu²MLP + Smear + Fact Tied Emb + Poly5 Softcap + YaRN2048 + 8192BPE + FP8 + Bit-packing LZMA + Stride-16 Eval - 2h
technique_details: Binary quantization ({-1,+1}, 1 bit/param) with per-group absmean scaling packs 106M parameters into 15.67MB. 15-layer asymmetric U-Net (7 encoder, 8 decoder). 4x relu² MLP, SmearGate, factored tied embedding (8192×254 bottleneck), polynomial softcap (degree 5), YaRN positional encoding. FP8 QAT for non-binary params. Bit-packing + LZMA preset=9. 50,000 steps (~2.15h), exceeding 10-minute budget. NeoMuon with 3 Newton-Schulz steps.
key_findings: Demonstrates compression frontier: 106.2M parameters in 15.67MB via 1-bit quantization, achieving 1.1239 BPB with unconstrained training (2.15h). Within 10-minute budget binary lags ternary by 0.025 BPB, but extended training surpasses ternary, providing evidence that binary networks benefit more from longer training.

title: Record: 11L + EMA + Tight SWA + QAT0.15 + VE128 + Partial RoPE + LN Scale (val_bpb: 1.1243)
technique_details: EMA (decay=0.997) stacked with Tight SWA — SWA collects checkpoints from EMA-averaged weights, providing two orthogonal averaging signals. Late QAT threshold widened to 0.15 (from 0.1) for 50% more fake-quantization steps, reducing quant gap from 0.008 to 0.007. Longer warmdown 3500 vs 3000 iters. LN Scale 1/sqrt(layer_idx+1) applied to RMSNorm outputs.
key_findings: Stacking EMA with Tight SWA (collecting from EMA weights) provides two orthogonal weight averaging signals, improving over PR #374 by -0.0003 BPB. Earlier Late QAT threshold reduces quantization gap by 0.0012 BPB.

title: Record: 11L + Tight SWA + Shared VE128 + Partial RoPE + LN Scale + XSA4 (val_bpb: 1.1246)
technique_details: Tight SWA restricts checkpoint collection to scale<0.2 (last ~600 steps, every 50 steps, ~12 checkpoints), eliminating the SWA quality penalty. Shared Value Embedding (dim=128) on layers 9,10 with per-layer learned scales. Partial RoPE on 16/64 dims. LN Scale 1/sqrt(layer_idx+1).
key_findings: Tight SWA (restricted to final low-LR phase) eliminates the typical SWA quality penalty, achieving zero BPB degradation post-averaging. Maintainer rejected as not statistically significant over previous record.

title: Non-record: 11L PR315 Backout + Native FA3 RunPod (val_bpb=1.1247)
technique_details: Faithful reproduction of PR #315-style run with Backout on RunPod 8xH100 SXM using native Hopper FlashAttention (FA3) and torch.compile. Flash attention helper inlined into train_gpt.py for self-contained submission. Filed as non-record because frontier is already slightly below this score.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM on RunPod with native Hopper FlashAttention
key_findings: Provides a clean, self-contained reproduction of the PR #315 pipeline on RunPod infrastructure with native FA3, achieving val_bpb=1.12467. Filed as non-record since it does not beat the current frontier.

title: Record: Parallel Muon + Parameter Banking — 81.87ms/step, val_bpb 1.1247 (3-seed mean)
technique_details: Parameter Banking restructures 66 separate nn.Linear weights into 4 contiguous 3D tensors (qo_bank, kv_bank, mlp_up_bank, mlp_down_bank) enabling batched Newton-Schulz orthogonalization (15x faster optimizer step: 19.7ms→1.3ms). Parallel Muon removes DDP for banked params, schedules async reduce_scatter + all_gather with NS on GPU shards. Net 3.4% throughput gain (81.87ms vs ~84.7ms baseline), yielding ~7330 steps vs ~7100. Benefit exclusive to EMA-based models.
key_findings: Introduces Parameter Banking + Parallel Muon as a pure systems optimization delivering 3.4% training throughput improvement and 1.1247 mean BPB; throughput gains only translate to quality improvements for EMA-based models since SWA dilutes extra steps.

title: Record: 11L Partial RoPE + LN Scale + EMA + XSA4 (val_bpb: 1.1248)
technique_details: Partial RoPE applies rotary embeddings to only 16 of 64 head dims (25%), leaving remaining dims position-free. LN Scale applies RMSNorm output scaling by 1/sqrt(layer_idx+1). Late QAT flag was present but ineffective due to torch.compile constant-folding the class attribute, confirmed by @152334H.
key_findings: Partial RoPE and LN Scale together yield -0.0023 BPB at zero parameter cost. Notably, the claimed Late QAT was dead code due to torch.compile constant-folding, so the result is entirely attributable to the two new positional/normalization techniques.

title: Record: DominationV3 + GPTQ-lite + TTT25 (mean val_bpb=1.1250, 3 seeds)
technique_details: GPTQ-lite with per-row optimal clip percentile search (5 candidates, MSE-minimizing). 25-epoch aggressive SGD TTT (lr=0.012, momentum=0.9, all blocks unfrozen) on already-graded tokens. OrthoInit, U-Net skip connections, sliding window eval stride=64. XSA removed vs earlier versions to free training steps.
key_findings: GPTQ-lite clip percentile search and aggressive 25-epoch SGD TTT combine for a 3-seed mean of 1.1250; earlier V2 update documented MLP 3x expansion and STE fake-quantization reducing quant penalty from ~0.05 to ~0.001 BPB.

title: Record: 11L XSA+EMA+TTT, sliding val_bpb=1.1254 (3-seed mean 1.1256)
technique_details: First combination of XSA+EMA+TTT: after training and Int6 QAT, performs 3 epochs of SGD fine-tuning (lr=0.002, momentum=0.9) on validation token stream with first 2 transformer blocks frozen. TTT takes ~47s and improves post-quant BPB by ~0.002. 11L 512d 8H/4KV MLP 3x relu-squared, XSA on last 4 layers, EMA decay=0.997, BigramHash(2048), zstd-22, Muon WD=0.04.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM, 600s training
key_findings: First submission combining XSA+EMA+TTT; TTT performs test-time SGD fine-tuning on validation tokens post-quantization, recovering ~0.002 BPB at a cost of only 47 seconds. Best seed achieves 1.1254 sliding BPB with 3-seed mean of 1.1256.

title: Record: 11L GPTQ-lite + Int6 MLP3x (val_bpb=1.1257)
technique_details: GPTQ-lite: per-layer optimal clip percentile search across 5 percentiles (100%, 99.9%, 99.5%, 99%, 98%) minimizing reconstruction error at zero training cost. XSA on last 4 layers. Tight SWA. Partial RoPE 16/64. LN Scale. Late QAT. Value Embedding. SmearGate. BigramHash. FA3. Int6+zstd-22.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM confirmed, 600s wallclock
key_findings: GPTQ-lite post-training clip percentile search reduces quantization degradation at zero training cost, achieving 1.1257 BPB (sliding window) vs 1.1417 pre-quantization — an unusually small quantization gap of only 0.016 BPB.

title: Non-record: Negative results & insights from 24hrs on 8xH100
technique_details: Systematic ablation of 13 techniques on PR #315 base. Negative: causal TTT (3 variants), MTP (+0.028 BPB), INT4 (0.06 BPB gap), canon layers (48% step overhead), memory tokens, gradient-guided quant, cautious WD, L1 reg, label smoothing, 1M batch, full-run QAT. Positive: EMA > SWA by 0.003 BPB; WD controls artifact size (~1.5-2MB per 0.01 WD); 786K > 524K batch by 0.004 BPB; FA3 Hopper gives 15-20% more steps.
hardware_notes: ~$500 of 8xH100 time over 24 hours.
key_findings: Comprehensive negative results document: 12 of 13 tested techniques failed to improve on the PR #315 base. Key positive findings: EMA outperforms SWA by 0.003 BPB and each 1ms of per-step overhead costs ~0.006 BPB at 86ms/step.

title: Record: 11L XSA4 + Tight SWA + FA3 + Two-Phase TTT (val_bpb=1.1216)
technique_details: Two-phase TTT: Phase 1 recalibrates norm-related params (100 epochs, Adam lr=0.01, ~22K params); Phase 2 unfreezes last 2 transformer blocks + lm_head (15 epochs, SGD lr=0.003, ~5.3M params). Combined TTT improvement: -0.019 BPB. Also uses 3x MLP relu², SmearGate, OrthoInit, Partial RoPE 16/64, BigramHash(2048), Late QAT 4%, 1% pruning, int6+zstd-22.
key_findings: Novel two-phase TTT approach separates quantization artifact correction (Phase 1: norm recalibration) from distribution adaptation (Phase 2: selective block fine-tuning), achieving -0.019 BPB improvement post-SWA. Reviewer notes discrepancies between README description and actual parameter freezing implementation.

title: New SOTA: 1.12676 BPB - 11L XSA-all(11) + GPTQ-lite + EMA + Late QAT
technique_details: XSA applied to all 11 layers (vs typical last 3-4); GPTQ-lite clip percentile search (5 percentiles per row, min MSE); EMA decay=0.997; Late QAT int6-all triggered at LR scale < 0.15; raw binary + zstd-22 serialization.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM (RunPod official template); secondary verification at 1.12753 BPB
key_findings: Applying XSA to all 11 layers (not just last 3-4) yields ~0.006 BPB improvement over XSA on last 4 layers, with only 3ms/step overhead. GPTQ-lite clip percentile search is a near-free ~0.0006 BPB gain.

title: Record: 11L Tight SWA + Partial RoPE + LN Scale + XSA4 (val_bpb: 1.1270)
technique_details: Tight SWA restricts checkpoint collection to loss scale<0.2 (last ~600 steps, every 50 steps, ~12 checkpoints), eliminating the SWA quality penalty. XSA on last 4 layers, Partial RoPE (16/64 dims), LN Scale normalization. Int6 per-row + zstd-22, FlashAttention 3 (Hopper). Muon WD=0.04, LR=0.025, batch 786K, seq 2048. 11L, 512d, 8H/4KV, MLP 3x relu-squared.
key_findings: Novel 'Tight SWA' strategy restricting checkpoint averaging to the final ~600 steps eliminates the post-SWA BPB quality penalty while maintaining quantization-friendly weight averaging, achieving 3-seed mean BPB of 1.1270.

title: Record: 11L XSA + EMA + Int6 MLP3x + WD=0.04 (val_bpb: 1.1271)
technique_details: XSA applied to last 4 of 11 layers, removing self-value bias via orthogonal projection at zero parameter cost. EMA (decay=0.997) replaces periodic SWA, applied every step. EMA decay of 0.999 was tried but averaged too slowly. Int6 per-row on MLP+attention, Int8 on embeddings, zstd-22.
key_findings: EMA at decay=0.997 outperforms periodic SWA for weight averaging, and XSA on the deepest 4 layers together deliver -0.0047 BPB over the prior 11-layer baseline. Submitted best single seed (1.1271) vs 3-seed mean of 1.1280.

title: Non-record: 11L XSA4 + EMA + SDTTT (3-seed mean val_bpb=1.1287)
technique_details: Builds on PR #379 architecture adding Self-Distillation TTT (SDTTT) and EMA. XSA on last 4 layers. Sliding window eval with stride=64. Running stock PyTorch SDPA (no FA3), 99ms/step vs SOTA 55ms/step.
key_findings: Self-Distillation TTT combined with XSA and EMA achieves mean 1.1287 BPB (3-seed) on 11L architecture, a progression from 1.1575 baseline over 4 days. Notably slower per step than SOTA due to no FA3.

title: Record: Sponge Bath — TTT 8ep eval-only improvement (val_bpb: 1.1295)
technique_details: Pure eval-time change on SOTA254 base: TTT epochs increased from 3 to 8, eval stride reduced from 64 to 32. No architecture or training changes. TTT adaptation costs ~115s, sliding window eval ~170s, total ~285s of 600s budget. Closed by author due to invalid TTT protocol (adapt-then-score on validation tokens constitutes information leakage per issue #402).
key_findings: Demonstrated eval-only gains from more TTT epochs (3→8) and finer stride (64→32), but was self-closed because the adapt-then-score pattern leaks validation information, violating competition rules per issue #402.

title: Record: 11L CANON-AC(last5)+DeltaGate Report (Humble Record Attempt, val_bpb: 1.1296)
technique_details: CANON layers (AC placement) on last 5 transformer blocks with delta gate (sigmoid-gated residual, init=-4.0 for near-identity start). XSA on last 4 layers. Tight SWA (every 50 steps, max 12 checkpoints). Partial RoPE (ROPE_DIMS=16/64). LN Scale. Late QAT. BigramHash (vocab=2048, dim=128) + SmearGate. Int6 for mlp+attn with zstd compression.
key_findings: CANON AC layers on last 5 blocks with a learned delta gate achieves 1.1296 BPB, a large improvement from 1.1668 in prior PR #312. The delta gate's near-identity initialization allows the model to gradually learn CANON pathway strength.

title: Record: 11L Tight SWA + VE128 + XSA4 + TTT (3-seed mean val_bpb=1.1299)
technique_details: Tight SWA with 12 checkpoints from last ~600 steps (scale<0.2), TTT using 3 epochs SGD on val tokens (~51s), late QAT with STE int6 fake-quant during warmdown (scale<0.1), shared VE128, XSA on last 4 layers, sliding window eval stride=64 context=2048
key_findings: Combines Tight SWA + VE128 + XSA4 from PR #374 with test-time training to achieve 3-seed mean of 1.1299, claiming to beat prior record of 1.1428 by 0.0129 nats. All 3 seeds individually beat the prior SOTA.

title: SOTA + LoRA TTT: 10L Int5/Int6 + test-time training
technique_details: Mixed int5/int6 quantization (int5 for MLP, int6 for attention, FP16 for embeddings); BigramHash(10240); LoRA rank-8 adapters on Q/V projections and LM head reset between documents for per-document TTT adaptation; exploits unused eval budget (~445s of 600s); SWA start_frac=0.4; OrthoInit; draft/incomplete submission pending 8xH100 validation
hardware_notes: Preliminary results on 1xH200 without wallclock cap; 8xH100 validation not yet complete
key_findings: Draft submission combining mixed int5/int6 quantization with per-document LoRA TTT adaptation; preliminary result ~1.130 BPB on 1xH200 but lacks required 8xH100 validation, 3-seed significance testing, and confirmed timing compliance.

title: Record: 11L + Efficient Partial XSA (val_bpb: 1.1307)
technique_details: GQA-aware XSA implementation uses free reshape+broadcast instead of repeat_interleave, reducing overhead from ~7ms to ~2ms per step. XSA applied only to last 3 layers (8,9,10) targeting highest self-attention bias layers. SWA every 120 steps (scale<0.5), 13-checkpoint average.
key_findings: Introduces an efficient GQA-aware XSA implementation that eliminates the memory allocation overhead of repeat_interleave via reshape+broadcast, and shows partial application to deepest layers only is sufficient for ~0.002 BPB gain.

title: Record: 11L EMA + Int6 + XSA + LeakyReLU² + Partial RoPE (val_bpb: 1.1309)
technique_details: 11 layers, 512 dim, 8H/4KV GQA. XSA on last 4 layers. LeakyReLU(0.5)² activation (squared leaky ReLU). Partial RoPE on 16/64 dims. EMA weight averaging (decay=0.997). Int6 quantization + zstd-22. Scale clamping fix (clamp_min(1/clip_range)). BigramHash(2048, 128d). Smaller batch (524288 tokens) for more steps (~8200). warmdown_iters=4500, matrix_lr=0.025.
key_findings: Achieves 1.13087 BPB (3-seed mean) with a novel LeakyReLU(0.5)² activation and a scale clamping fix for improved quantization quality, built on the existing SOTA stack with 11 layers and XSA-4.

title: Record: FarnsworthEngine v1 — TTT + 11L Int6 MLP3x, val_bpb=1.1303
technique_details: 11L model with Int6 quantization and MLP 3x expansion; TTT used at eval time with early block freezing for stability; 3-seed mean 1.1313 (seeds 1337/42/7: 1.1303/1.1312/1.1323); 15.88MB artifact, 600s train + 129s eval. Supersedes PRs #152 and #178.
key_findings: FarnsworthEngine v1 combines 11L Int6 MLP3x with TTT, achieving 1.1313 mean BPB; a commenter raised concerns about the legality of the TTT approach (scoring tokens after training on them), questioning whether validation data is being trained on before evaluation.

title: 11-Layer Int6 + WD=0.04 + SWA + FA3 (val_bpb: 1.1318)
technique_details: 11 layers (up from 9), weight decay 0.04 for both Muon and AdamW to keep weights quantization-friendly under int6, SWA over ~8 checkpoints during warmdown, eval stride reduced to 64, BigramHash reduced to 2048 buckets. OrthoInit + muP, 3x MLP, int6 mixed quant + zstd-22, SmearGate, FA3. 3-seed mean: 1.1326.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM, 600s
key_findings: Adds 2 extra layers funded by int6 quantization headroom, plus weight decay 0.04 to improve quantization friendliness and SWA for smoother weights; 3-seed mean is 1.1326 with submitted best seed at 1.1318.

title: Record: 11L Full Stack + XSA4 + Tight SWA + Late QAT (val_bpb=1.1320)
technique_details: Tight SWA every 50 steps when LR scale<0.2 (12 checkpoints); Late QAT with STE int6 when LR scale<0.1; Partial RoPE (16/64 dims) with NTK-aware scaling; LN scale factor 1/sqrt(layer_idx+1); U-Net skip connections (5 encoder, 6 decoder); Shared Value Embedding (dim=128, layers 9-10); logit softcap 30.0; zstd level 22.
key_findings: Combines tight SWA, late QAT, XSA, partial RoPE, and shared value embeddings in a dense 11L stack to achieve 1.1320 BPB with near-zero SWA penalty and a quant gap of only ~0.008, representing a strong single-seed record claim.

title: Record: 12L Gradient-Guided Quant + Partial RoPE + LN Scale + EMA + XSA4 (val_bpb: 1.1320)
technique_details: Gradient-guided adaptive quantization accumulates per-tensor squared gradient magnitudes in last 10% of warmdown: top 10% sensitivity tensors use int7, middle 70% use int6, bottom 20% use int5. Saves ~1MB vs uniform int6, funding a 12th layer. Batch reduced to 524K for 22% more steps. Late QAT found harmful at 12 layers (-770 steps cost exceeds quant benefit).
