key_findings: Gradient-guided adaptive quantization (int5/6/7 by sensitivity) saves ~1MB enabling a 12th transformer layer, achieving 1.1320 BPB. Negative finding: Late QAT hurts at 12 layers because the per-step overhead reduces total training steps enough to negate quantization benefits.

title: Record: 11L + XSA4 + EMA + Late QAT + GPTQ-lite (1.1325 BPB)
technique_details: XSA4 on last 4 layers for ~800K parameter savings enabling 11L in 16MB. EMA decay 0.997 full training duration. Late QAT activates when LR < 15% peak. GPTQ-lite: 5-percentile MSE search across clip levels for attention layers. fullgraph=True compilation. However, automated review found EMA and QAT were not actually implemented in the submitted code despite being claimed.
key_findings: Claims 11L + XSA4 + EMA + Late QAT combination achieving 1.1325 BPB, but code review revealed EMA and QAT were not actually implemented in the submitted train_gpt.py, creating a significant discrepancy between claimed and actual methodology.

title: The Frugendorff: Recursive Weight Sharing Research — Cadence Laws + Challenges (1.1325 BPB)
technique_details: Recursive weight sharing: 4 unique flat blocks + 2 shared crawler blocks (Micro Crawler, 4f+2cx2), effective depth 8; Persistent Deliberation gate with bidirectional gradient flow; cadence ablation (C/N ratio of double-fire vs single-fire steps); GPTQ Hessian-aware quantization essential for shared weights; GQA 10H/5KV, XSA last 2, MLP 4x enabled by parameter savings; sliding window eval (stride 64).
key_findings: Asymmetric recursive weight sharing (4 flat + 2 shared crawler blocks) beats uniform sharing by 0.015 BPB, and GPTQ reduces quantization gap 5x for shared weights; however, double-firing C-steps create EMA instability and reduce total steps by 10-20%, making cad0 (no double-firing) the best config at 1.1325 BPB.

title: Record: 7L MLP3x + BigramHash + SmearGate + TTT 5ep (mean val_bpb=1.1327)
technique_details: AdamW TTT for 5 epochs on validation tokens (lr=0.0005, wd=0.0), DDP-synced. BigramHash(2048, dim=128) additive before RMSNorm. SmearGate per-dimension learned blending. Partial RoPE 16/64 dims. LN scale depth damping 1/sqrt(layer_idx+1). Muon optimizer with momentum 0.99, warmdown 6000 iters. Int8+zlib compression.
key_findings: Achieves strong BPB with only 7 layers by combining AdamW TTT (5 epochs on validation data) with BigramHash and SmearGate; claims to beat prior SOTA 1.1428 by -0.010 BPB using a shallower architecture than competitors.

title: Submission: 11L NTK-RoPE + FA3 + Batch524K + XSA4 + EMA (val_bpb=1.1328)
technique_details: NTK-aware RoPE auto-scaling (4x base at seq=2048). FA3 on Hopper (58ms/step vs 99ms SDPA, 71% more steps). XSA on last 4 layers. EMA decay=0.997. Adaptive pruning auto-finds minimal prune % per seed to hit 16MB. Mixed int5 MLP / int6 attn / int8 embed + zstd-22. 11L 512d 8H/4KV GQA, MLP 3x relu², U-Net skips, BigramHash(4096).
key_findings: Demonstrates FA3 on Hopper enabling 71% more training steps (10,300+ vs 6,030) in the same 600s budget, combined with NTK-aware RoPE scaling and per-seed adaptive pruning to reliably fit under the 16MB artifact limit.

title: Non-record: Negative findings on codebook quantization, magnitude pruning, multi-token prediction, embedding factorization
technique_details: 25 experiments documented in FINDINGS.md. Key negatives: codebook quantization (K-means K=256) gives 87% lower reconstruction MSE than int6 but 25% larger artifact under zstd-22; magnitude pruning at 3% increased artifact by 728KB; multi-token prediction auxiliary t+2 head at 0.5x loss weight: +0.0018 BPP, 3% slower; embedding SVD rank-64 explains only 41.9% of variance. Later closed due to non-causal TTT in reproduction command (invalid per #402).
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM (RunPod Parameter Golf template)
key_findings: Comprehensive negative-findings submission documenting 25+ experiments; notably codebook quantization achieves lower MSE than int6 but compresses worse under zstd-22 due to higher byte entropy in codebook indices, and magnitude pruning non-monotonically affects artifact size.

title: Non-record: 11L MLP3.5x LeakyReLU(0.5)^2 + Full SOTA Stack (mean val_bpb=1.1330, 8xH100)
technique_details: Identical content to PR #635: MLP 3.5x expansion with LeakyReLU(0.5)²; XSA all 11 layers; Value Residual + Gated Attention + SmearGate + BigramHash(10240) + TrigramHash(4096); late QAT via STE (final 15%); GPTQ-lite with 5-percentile clip search; logit softcap 30.0; 30-experiment autoresearch loop on 1xH100.
key_findings: Appears to be an earlier duplicate of PR #635 with identical results (1.1330 BPB, 3-seed, std=0.0007); title says 'Record' in the body but 'Non-record' in the PR title, suggesting a later correction to the title when resubmitted as PR #635.

title: Non-record: 11L MLP3.5x LeakyReLU(0.5)^2 + Full SOTA Stack (mean val_bpb=1.1330, 8xH100 SXM)
technique_details: MLP expanded to 3.5x (hidden=1792) with LeakyReLU(0.5)², full SOTA stack including Value Residual, Gated Attention, XSA all 11 layers, TrigramHash(4096), late QAT via STE (final 15% of warmdown), GPTQ-lite clip search, sliding window stride=64. Developed via 30-experiment autoresearch loop on 1xH100.
hardware_notes: Development on 1xH100 (~8 hours); validated on 8xH100 SXM (RunPod).
key_findings: Systematic ablation confirms Value Residual (-0.017 BPB) and SmearGate (-0.010 BPB) as the largest contributors; MLP 3.5x + full SOTA stack reaches 1.1330 mean BPB without TTT.

title: Non-Record: BPB 1.1334 — 7000-Step Training + Mixed Int6/Int8 Quantization + Legal TTT
technique_details: Extended 7000-step training with longer warmdown cosine anneal (step 3500→7000); mixed int6/int8 quantization (int6 per-row for attention+MLP, int8 per-tensor for LN/VE/biases); GPTQ-lite clip search (5 candidates/row) late QAT at step 6476; score-first TTT with SGD+momentum (0.9), lr=0.002, 10 epochs per 32K-token chunk; U-Net skip connections; LN depth scaling.
hardware_notes: 4×A100-40GB (non-standard; standard is 8×H100)
key_findings: Non-record exploration on 4×A100 showing that extended 7000-step training with longer warmdown improves pre-TTT BPB by 0.0133 vs prior baseline; mixed int6/int8 quantization scheme keeps 27M-param GEPA model under 16 MB while fewer TTT epochs (10 vs 30) reduce eval time by 40%.

title: Track 10min_16mb: PR #287 family rerun at 585s wallclock (mean val_bpb=1.1346)
technique_details: 3-seed rerun of the PR #287 family (XSA 4-layer, EMA) with FA3 under a 585s wallclock cap to keep all seed artifacts within the 16MB limit. No novel techniques beyond the PR #287 baseline.
hardware_notes: 8×H100 SXM
key_findings: Numerically strong rerun (mean 1.1346, p=0.0032 vs 1.1428 baseline) but closed by the author for lacking novelty beyond prior PR #287 family work.

title: SOTA Submission (1.1349 BPB) by weywey [10min_16mb track]
technique_details: 11-layer INT6 quantized model with OLR-FW TTT, rank 128, LR=0.001, beta2=0.95. Built via Weyhe Framework. Artifact size 16,590,005 bytes (over 16MB limit).
key_findings: Straightforward INT6 + TTT submission claiming 1.1349 BPB but artifact size of 16,590,005 bytes exceeds the 16MB limit, likely rendering it non-compliant.

title: Non-record: Fixed Bank QAT + XSA5 + Label Smoothing (1.1352)
technique_details: Fixed dead-code Bank QAT by implementing STE int6 fake-quantization directly in GPT.forward() for bank parameters (original SOTA's QAT was constant-folded away by torch.compile). Expanded XSA to 5 layers. Added label smoothing 0.05. Tuned TTT LR (0.003) and momentum (0.95). QAT fix caused ~50s recompilation + 5ms/step overhead → 460 fewer training steps.
key_findings: Identifies that SOTA's bank parameter QAT is dead code due to torch.compile constant-folding; fixing it costs training steps due to recompilation overhead, label smoothing is counterproductive for compute-limited models, and XSA5 is neutral vs XSA4.

title: Record: 11L EMA + BigramHash(12288) + Mixed Int5 + FA3 (1.1354)
technique_details: EMA decay 0.997, BigramHash(12288, dim=128) — larger than typical 10240 bucket size, mixed low-bit quantization with int5. Uses FA3 kernel fetched at runtime from kernels-community/flash-attn3 (disclosed). Stride-64 sliding window evaluation.
key_findings: Pushes BigramHash vocabulary to 12288 buckets (up from common 10240), finding it fits within 16MB artifact budget while improving BPB. FA3 runtime kernel acquisition is explicitly disclosed as a potential compliance concern.

title: Record: 11L + Partial XSA + TTT + BatchOpt (val_bpb=1.1354)
technique_details: Partial XSA applied to last 3 layers; TTT via 3-epoch full-model SGD with first 2 blocks frozen (50s); batch size 524K tokens; RoPE base 50K; int6 per-row + zstd-22 compression; sliding window eval stride=64; PyTorch SDPA fallback instead of FA3 due to RunPod image constraints.
hardware_notes: PyTorch SDPA fallback used instead of FA3 (FA3 not available in RunPod image); estimated ~600 additional steps possible with FA3
key_findings: Stacks Partial XSA (last 3 layers), eval-time TTT (SGD), and larger batch size on an 11-layer base, achieving 1.1354 BPB sliding window; single-seed only due to budget constraints, with FA3 expected to improve results further.

title: Non-record: 1.1354 BPB — 10L TTT 22ep AdamW Cosine + LeakyReLU(0.5)² + TrigramHash
technique_details: LeakyReLU(0.5)² activation preserving negative gradient flow; TrigramHash extends BigramHash to 3-token context via XOR hashing with zero extra parameters; batched TTT (32 seqs/GPU) ~500x faster than chunk-based; per-step cosine LR decay for TTT prevents overfitting; per-layer LR groups (3x output proj, 0.5x input proj); gradient sync via all_reduce per step critical for multi-GPU TTT; XSA on last 4 layers; mixed int5 (MLP)/int6 (attention) + zstd-22; 3% magnitude pruning; 27-checkpoint SWA.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM (standard)
key_findings: Batched TTT (32 seqs/GPU) is ~500x faster than chunk-based approaches, and per-step cosine LR decay prevents overfitting at high epoch counts. TrigramHash extends BigramHash to 3-token context at zero parameter cost via XOR hashing into the shared embedding table.

title: Record: 11L XSA4 + EMA + Batch524K + zstd fallback (val_bpb: 1.1357)
technique_details: XSA on last 4 layers, EMA decay 0.997, batch size 524288 tokens. SDPA fallback for flash_attn_interface when FA3 bindings unavailable. torch.compile behind env flag. zstd Python-or-CLI fallback for portability. BigramHash(2048, dim=128). GQA 8 heads / 4 KV heads. Pre-quant BPB 1.1529, int6 roundtrip 1.1580, sliding window s64 1.1357.
key_findings: Demonstrates that batch size 524288 tokens is a better fixed-budget choice for 11L+XSA+EMA than larger batch sizes, while adding robust FA3/zstd fallbacks for portability. Single-seed result below then-current merged SOTA of 1.1428.

title: Record: 11L XSA6 + Warmdown3000 + QAT@0.30 (val_bpb=1.1352, 2-seed mean)
technique_details: XSA applied to last 6 layers (vs SOTA's 4); warmdown shortened to 3000 iters; QAT threshold raised to 0.30 (earlier activation). 37 local ablation experiments on RTX 4060 Ti identified transferable hyperparams. PyTorch SDPA fallback used (no FA3), resulting in fewer steps (~5400 vs ~7100).
hardware_notes: FlashAttention 3 unavailable; PyTorch SDPA fallback used, ~5400 steps at 110ms/step vs expected ~7000 steps with FA3
key_findings: Ablation study across 8 hyperparameter dimensions on consumer GPU identified that warmdown ratio and QAT threshold transfer from local to H100, while EMA decay and LR do not. Extending XSA to last 6 layers and earlier QAT activation yield measurable gains over SOTA baseline.

title: 11L + XSA4 + EMA(0.997) + seq2048 + Int5-MLP + MuonWD=0.04 + LateK-FP16 | val_bpb=1.1361
technique_details: Late-K FP16 keeps the final layer's key projection in fp16 instead of int6 (~131KB cost). BigramHash reduced to 2048 buckets (dim=64) to fit artifact budget. EMA replaces SWA. MuonWD tuned to 0.04. RoPE base 500K. Seq length 2048 with warmdown tuned to 2000 steps.
key_findings: Closed by author due to artifact size exceeding 16MB decimal limit (16,564,943 bytes). Stacks 8 techniques including Late-K FP16 and EMA over prior submissions, achieving 1.1361 mean BPB across 3 seeds.

title: Record: Dynamic Eval + TTT on SOTA Pipeline (val_bpb=1.1364)
technique_details: Dynamic evaluation applies SGD gradient steps (lr=0.001) during sliding window scoring (stride=64), adapting to local text distribution. Scored in batches of 32 windows, gradient step every 4 batches. Complementary to TTT which adapts before scoring. Built on PR #315 (XSA, EMA, Partial RoPE, LN Scale, Late QAT) and PR #338 (TTT integration).
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM, SDPA backend
key_findings: Combines TTT (pre-scoring adaptation) with dynamic evaluation (during-scoring adaptation) for a claimed 2.0% BPB improvement over the SOTA pipeline at zero artifact cost. 3-seed mean is 1.1371, best seed is 1.1364.

title: 11L MLP3x + Int6 QAT + XSA + EMA + BigramHash + FA3 (val_bpb 1.1345)
technique_details: 11L 512d, GQA (8/4), 3x MLP SwiGLU, Int6 STE QAT, Zstd-22, XSA on last 4 layers, EMA(0.997), BigramHash(2048), Muon (momentum=0.99, WD=0.04)+Adam, sliding window eval stride=64, FlashAttention 3 (Hopper build required). Best seed 1.1345, mean 1.1364.
hardware_notes: Requires FlashAttention 3 Hopper build; implies H100 SXM dependency beyond standard setup
key_findings: Achieves mean val_bpb=1.1364 with a strong SOTA stack, but author self-closed as a reproduction of PR #287 without novel ML contributions; notable for requiring FA3 Hopper build as a hard dependency.

title: Record: 11L Backout + Int6 + SWA (val_bpb: 1.1364)
technique_details: 11L, 512d, 8 heads / 4 KV heads, MLP 3x, relu-squared, SmearGate, BigramHash(4096), OrthoInit, Muon+AdamW WD=0.04, SWA (6 checkpoints), int6 mixed quant + zstd, FA3, seq 2048, sliding window eval stride=64. Novel 'Backout Connection': learned scalar residual subtraction of h_mid from final representation (lambda init=0.2, zero new matrix params), improving val_bpb by -0.0071 vs baseline. Artifact 16.17MB — 170KB over 16MB cap; INT5_MLP=1 planned for fix.
hardware_notes: RunPod EUR-IS-3 community cloud (vs dedicated); hardware differences caused baseline replication discrepancy vs PR #198
key_findings: Introduces 'Backout Connection' — a zero-matrix-parameter learned negative residual subtracting mid-network hidden state from final representation — yielding -0.0071 BPB improvement, but the artifact is 170KB over the 16MB cap and requires INT5_MLP to become valid.

title: 10L XSA + EMA + Partial RoPE + LN Scale (val_bpb: 1.1365)
technique_details: Partial RoPE applies positional encoding to only 16 of 64 head dims (25%), leaving 48 dims position-free for content-based attention. EMA decay=0.997. XSA on last 4 layers. Int5 for MLP weights, Int6 for attention. 3.2% magnitude pruning. zstd-22 compression. 92ms/step on 8xH100.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM, 600s, PyTorch 2.7.0 + FlashAttention 2.8.3.
key_findings: Achieves 1.1365 bpb (2-seed) without TTT by combining Partial RoPE (16/64 dims) with XSA, EMA, and LN Scale; ablations suggest 16 positional dims outperforms 0, 32, and 64.

title: 11L + Hadamard Rotation + VE128 + cuDNN SDPA (val_bpb: 1.1365, 3-seed mean)
technique_details: Data-free Walsh-Hadamard rotation applied to weight matrices before int6 per-row quantization, spreading outliers to improve compression from 1.70x to 1.76x and reduce quantization gap from 0.0093 to 0.0084 BPB. Compression headroom enables Shared Value Embeddings (VE128) on layers 9-10. Full GPTQ found redundant when Hadamard rotation is present. CPU parameter probe across 9.5M configs reduced GPU compute by ~84%.
key_findings: Novel data-free Hadamard rotation for INT6 quantization improves compression and enables additional architecture features within the 16MB budget; notably Hadamard rotation and GPTQ are found to be substitutes at int6 precision.

title: 10L XSA + EMA + Partial RoPE + LN Scale (val_bpb: 1.1366)
technique_details: XSA on last 4 layers, EMA decay=0.997, Partial RoPE (16/64 dims), LN Scale, int5 MLP / int6 attention quantization, FP16 embeddings, 3.2% weight pruning, zstd-22 compression, BigramHash(10240), SmearGate, orthogonal init, Muon optimizer. Sliding window eval stride=64. Superseded by updated submission per author comment.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM, ~92ms/step, PyTorch 2.7.0 + FlashAttention 2.8.3
key_findings: 10-layer model combining XSA, EMA, Partial RoPE, and LN Scale achieves mean 1.1366 BPB across two seeds; subsequently closed as superseded by an updated submission from the same author.

title: 10L XSA + LeakyReLU² + Partial RoPE (val_bpb=1.1370)
technique_details: 10L, d=512, 8 heads / 4 KV heads, tied embeddings. XSA (removes self-value projection) on last 4 layers, LeakyReLU(0.5)² replacing ReLU², Partial RoPE on 25% of head dims (16/64), increased learning rates (matrix_lr 0.025, scalar_lr 0.025, tied_embed_lr 0.035), 8% magnitude pruning. Int5/Int6+zstd quantization, sliding window stride=64. Built on thwu1 SOTA (PR #180).
key_findings: Combines XSA (no self-value projection on last 4 layers), LeakyReLU(0.5)², and Partial RoPE to achieve 1.1370 mean val_bpb across 3 seeds, representing an improvement over the SOTA baseline. All three seeds fall under the 16MB artifact limit.

title: Non-record: val_bpb=1.1374, FA2+SWA adaptation of Farnsworth
technique_details: 11L, 3x MLP expansion, ReLU^2. Int6 for MLP+attention, Int8 for embeddings, zstd-22. SmearGate sigmoid blending, BigramHash 2048 buckets. U-Net skip connections. TTT full-weight SGD on val data (lr=0.002, momentum=0.9, 3 epochs). SWA 7 checkpoints every 200 steps during warmdown. FlashAttention 2.8.3. NTK-RoPE base=10000. Key finding: cuDNN SDP 40% faster per-op than Flash SDP but 0.004 worse BPB; WD=0.042 optimally targets 15.5MB artifact; QAT hurts at this scale.
key_findings: Systematic study of FA2 vs cuDNN SDP (cuDNN faster but worse quality), weight decay as artifact size control (WD=0.042 → 15.5MB), and QAT being counterproductive at this scale. Closed because non-causal TTT was used; superseded by PR #375.

title: Non-Record: BPB 1.13872 — LeakyReLU(0.5)² + Per-Layer LR Legal TTT (3 seeds)
technique_details: Builds on PR #526 with LeakyReLU(0.5)² replacing ReLU² (−0.0035 mean pre-TTT improvement, source of all final BPB gain). Per-layer LR for TTT (mlp.proj 3x, mlp.fc 0.5x) and intra-chunk cosine decay added but showed no measurable TTT benefit. Mean BPB across 3 seeds: 1.13936±0.0008. SGD 30ep/chunk, freeze first 2 blocks.
hardware_notes: 4xA100-40GB training (~42 min); eval ~3690s on 1xA100 (non-standard)
key_findings: 3-seed validation confirming LeakyReLU(0.5)² drives the BPB improvement over PR #526; per-layer TTT learning rates showed no measurable benefit in this setting, highlighting that activation function choice dominates over TTT optimizer tuning here.

title: Record: 11L Gradient-Guided Adaptive Quant + EMA + Sliding Eval (val_bpb=1.1396)
technique_details: Gradient-guided adaptive quantization assigns int5/int6/int7 per tensor based on gradient sensitivity (top 45% int7, middle 40% int6, bottom 15% int5). EMA from init (alpha=0.997) loaded at eval. Adaptive warmdown auto-caps at 55% of estimated steps. XSA on last 4 layers. Muon momentum=0.99, WD=0.04, grad_clip=0.3. 11L, 512 dim, 8 heads, 4 KV heads, MLP 3x, relu^2 activation.
key_findings: Gradient-guided per-tensor adaptive quantization (int5/6/7 mixed) combined with EMA from initialization achieves 1.1396 BPB at 15.9MB. The adaptive bit assignment based on gradient sensitivity is a novel approach to quantization in this competition.

title: Record: 11L XSA + EMA + Int5-MLP (val_bpb=1.1399)
technique_details: XSA (Exclusive Self-Attention) applied on last 4 of 11 layers. Continuous GPU float32 EMA (decay=0.997) every step with no CPU transfers. Mixed int5 MLP / int6 attention / int8 embedding quantization plus 8% magnitude pruning before zstd-22 compression. SmearGate, BigramHash(2048), U-Net skip connections, GQA (8H/4KV), Muon WD=0.04, cosine warmdown 3000 iters.
hardware_notes: 8xH100, <600s per seed
key_findings: Applies XSA only to the last 4 layers rather than all layers, combined with continuous on-GPU EMA and mixed int5/int6/int8 quantization plus magnitude pruning; 3-seed mean is 1.1399.

title: Record: 11L Int6 + SmearGate + Batch Optimization (val_bpb=1.1400)
technique_details: Key finding: reducing batch from 786K to 524K gives 22% more gradient updates at cost of 17% fewer total tokens, net positive. Int8 tok_emb (vs fp16) frees space for wider MLP at 11 layers. Int6-all beats int5-MLP (quant penalty 0.010 vs 0.029 BPB). WD=0.04 keeps artifact at 15.7MB. Batched sliding eval (32 windows simultaneous) makes stride=64 feasible in 172s.
key_findings: Demonstrates that smaller batch size (524K vs 786K) wins in fixed-time training by maximizing gradient update count. Systematic sweep of batch size, weight decay, and quantization precision provides actionable negative findings for the community.

title: feat: Ultimate SOTA submission - 10L Model, Mixed Int6 QAT, and TTT/LoRA Evaluation
technique_details: 10L model with 3x MLP, mixed Int6 QAT with STE and per-row scaling, SmearGate, BigramHash (4096 buckets), U-Net skip connections, Muon+WD, SWA over final 50% of training, sliding window eval stride=64, TTT with batched LoRA adapters rank=8, magnitude pruning (3% zeroed), Zstd-22. Score is approximate (~1.14 BPB), no actual measured result reported.
hardware_notes: Notes single-GPU H100/A100 optimization
key_findings: Comprehensive kitchen-sink submission combining many SOTA techniques including TTT with LoRA adapters for validation specialization, but reports only an approximate target BPB (~1.14) rather than a measured result, raising questions about actual validation.

title: Record: 11L Next-Gen Stack + Custom Kernels, val_bpb=1.1399
technique_details: 9-layer transformer, 512d, 8H/4KV GQA, MLP 3x (hidden=1536). SmearGate + BigramHash 2048 buckets, Int6+zstd-22. SWA over 14-15 checkpoint average during warmdown. Muon optimizer (WD=0.04, momentum=0.99, warmup 0.92→0.99 over 1500 steps). NTK-RoPE base=50000. Custom Triton/CUDA kernels in dev: fused RMSNorm+QKV (1.47x), fused ReLU² MLP (1.26x), fused softcap+CE (1.70x). Sequence length 2048, batch 786K tokens, ~125ms/step, 4782 steps.
key_findings: 9-layer competitive stack with custom Triton/CUDA kernels achieving 1.1401 BPB; kernel pipeline (fused RMSNorm+QKV, MLP, softcap+CE) is in development and expected to enable more training steps in future submissions.

title: Record: 11L XSA + EMA + TTT + Partial RoPE + LN Scale — val_bpb=1.1401
technique_details: TTT uses 3-epoch SGD. SmearGate corrected to use interpolation (torch.lerp) instead of additive blending. BigramHash uses XOR hashing with large primes and a learned output scalar (init 0.05). Eval stride=32 rather than the more common 64.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM via RunPod, PyTorch 2.9.1+cu128, Flash Attention 2
key_findings: Closed by author in favor of earlier PR #212 (same code). Notes that SmearGate's additive formula inflates magnitude and should use lerp interpolation instead.

title: Record: val_bpb: 1.14020 [tested 3x on 8xh100]
technique_details: Test-time training applied during validation: each chunk is evaluated first (loss recorded), then trained on, ensuring no look-ahead. Single sequential pass through 32 validation chunks. Compliant TTT: tokens scored exactly once before any training on that chunk.
key_findings: Demonstrates compliant TTT during validation by scoring each chunk before training on it, achieving 1.1402 BPB across 3 seeds. The approach is explicitly argued to be rule-compliant based on no re-evaluation and single-pass sequential processing.

title: [Record] Stride-32 + Warmdown/Muon Tuning on SOTA #1: mean val_bpb=1.1403
technique_details: Built on 10L Int5MLP BigramHash SWA base with env-var-only changes: EVAL_STRIDE=32 (from 64) for 2x context overlap, WARMDOWN_ITERS=5000 (from 3000), MUON_MOMENTUM=0.95 (from 0.99), TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS=524288 (from 786432). All artifacts under 16MB via int6+zstd-22.
hardware_notes: 8x NVIDIA H100 80GB HBM3 SXM on RunPod
key_findings: Achieves mean val_bpb=1.1403 across 3 seeds via stride-32 sliding window eval and warmdown/Muon tuning, improving over SOTA by 0.017 nats (3.4x the 0.005 significance threshold) with no code changes to train_gpt.py.

title: Non Record: Add PPM heuristic for test time learning
technique_details: Delayed outside-context-only PPM at evaluation time on top of 10L Int5-MLP + BigramHash(10240) model. 2048-token delay ensures PPM bank only sees tokens outside transformer's sliding-window context. Backoff n-gram model with k=[16,12,8,6], min_confidence=[1.0,1.0,1.0,0.95], K=15.
key_findings: Delayed PPM (2048-token delay) provides consistent ~0.00126 BPB improvement across all 3 seeds by capturing long-range repeated-sequence signal outside the transformer's local context window.

title: Non-record: 27M params at Int5 QAT / train larger, quantize harder (val_bpb=1.1418)
technique_details: 27M param model at d=576 with int5 QAT (32 levels); early QAT activation at 50% training threshold (~1700 steps) vs standard 4%; GQA 9/3; XSA on last 4 layers; Partial RoPE 16/64; U-Net skips; built on PR #315.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM, 600s
key_findings: Validates 'train larger, quantize harder' principle: a 27M param model compressed to int5 achieves 1.1418 BPB, with early QAT activation at 50% threshold being critical for int5's 32-level adaptation compared to standard 4% QAT window.

title: Non-record: VR + GA + Late QAT + Full GPTQ — 1.1418 BPB, 15.7 MB
technique_details: Value Residual uses layer-0 V vector shortcut for deep attention signal flow (−0.015 BPB). Gated Attention adds per-head learned sigmoid gate after SDPA (−0.003 BPB). Full GPTQ + Int5 MLP post-training quantization (−0.028 BPB, −3.6 MB). Late QAT triggered by LR threshold (~5% of training). Key negative finding: SGD TTT hurts by +0.030 BPB on GPTQ-quantized models.
hardware_notes: 1x NVIDIA RTX A6000, ~14 hours (non-standard hardware, non-standard duration)
key_findings: Value Residual and Gated Attention provide modest BPB gains; notably TTT degrades performance (+0.030 BPB) on GPTQ-quantized models, suggesting gradient-based test-time adaptation is incompatible with quantized weight spaces.

title: Add non-record 4xH100 10L Int5-MLP submission
technique_details: Standard 10L Int5-MLP recipe rerun on 4xH100 with 1200s wallclock cap and built-in gradient accumulation. zstd compression for artifact.
hardware_notes: 4xH100 instead of standard 8xH100; 1200s wallclock instead of 600s
key_findings: Demonstrates that the 10L Int5-MLP recipe achieves competitive BPB (1.1422) on 4xH100 with doubled training time, useful for resource-constrained reproducibility.

title: Non-record: 11L + 30-Epoch Legal TTT (BPB 1.14252)
technique_details: Builds on PR #461 by increasing SGD TTT epochs from 3 to 30 per 32K chunk with frozen first 2 blocks and cosine LR across chunks. Epoch sweep: 3ep→1.14458, 10ep→1.14295, 30ep→1.14252 (non-monotonic at 15ep). SGD outperforms AdamW by 0.027 BPB for legal TTT. Architecture unchanged from PR #461.
hardware_notes: 4xA100-40GB training (~41 min); eval 3662s on 1xA100 (non-standard)
key_findings: Systematic epoch sweep showing 30-epoch SGD legal TTT yields −0.0184 BPB gain (2.7x vs single-epoch AdamW), with non-monotonic behavior at 15 epochs suggesting complex optimization dynamics in per-chunk TTT.

title: Non-record: QAT & EMA negative results on SOTA stack (val_bpb=1.1426)
technique_details: Ablation on PR #180 SOTA stack. QAT (STE fake-quantization, warmup=500) costs 8% of training steps; EMA via .cpu().clone() every step costs 32% throughput. QAT+EMA combined reduces steps from 6684 to 4546 and worsens BPB by 0.018. EMA artifact exceeds 16MB.
key_findings: Demonstrates that QAT and EMA both hurt performance under the 10-minute budget because their throughput costs (8% and 32% respectively) outweigh their compression/averaging benefits; EMA implementation also causes artifact size to exceed 16MB limit.

title: Record: 11L NonTTT VR+GA MixedInt5/6: val_bpb=1.1428 (3-seed, 8xH100)
technique_details: Value Residual caches layer-0 V vectors mixed into later layers via learnable λ (22 params/block). Gated Attention uses per-head sigmoid gate (~37K params total). Mixed quantization: Int5 [-16,15] for MLP middle layers, Int6 [-32,31] for edge layers and attention. DDP bug (find_unused_parameters=True) caused +53% step time, reducing steps to ~4600 vs expected ~7000. All seeds exceeded 16MB artifact limit.
hardware_notes: 8x H100 SXM 80GB, 600s wallclock, ~27 GiB/GPU peak. DDP regression caused 130ms/step vs expected ~85ms.
key_findings: Combines Value Residual and Gated Attention on an 11L base with mixed Int5/Int6 quantization, but submission is invalid as all artifacts exceed 16MB and a DDP bug significantly reduced training steps; authors plan a corrected resubmission.

title: Bigram-Aware Context Modeling with Mixed-Precision Quantization (val_bpb: 1.1431)
technique_details: BigramHash 10,240-bucket XOR hash table. Mixed quantization: int5 for MLP (smooth distributions), int6 for attention (precision-sensitive). 10 layers funded by int5 MLP savings (~1.86MB recovered). SWA 24 checkpoints over last 40% of warmdown. Orthogonal init with muP output scaling. Muon WD=0.04. Sliding window eval stride=64. zstd-22.
key_findings: Int5/int6 mixed-precision quantization (MLP vs. attention) recovers ~1.86MB artifact budget to fund an extra layer, achieving 1.1431 BPB. The rationale of matching quantization precision to weight distribution smoothness (MLP vs. attention) is clearly articulated.

title: Bigram-Aware Context Modeling with Mixed-Precision Quantization (val_bpb: 1.1431)
technique_details: Int5 for MLP layers and Int6 for attention layers (mixed precision). BigramHash with 10,240-bucket XOR hash table. SmearGate blends adjacent token embeddings. 3x MLP expansion. 24-checkpoint SWA over last 40% of warmdown. Orthogonal init with muP output scaling. Muon with WD=0.04. Sliding window stride=64, seq_len=2048. zstd-22 compression.
key_findings: Demonstrates mixed-precision quantization (Int5 MLP + Int6 attention) combined with BigramHash and SmearGate, achieving 1.1431 BPB. Int5 on MLP recovers ~1.86MB to fund an extra layer.

title: 12L Int5-MLP + SmearGate + BigramHash + SWA (val_bpb 1.1433)
technique_details: Mixed int5 per-row for MLP weights (3 zero high bits for better zstd-22 ~3.8x compression) and int6 per-row for attention, fp16 embedding passthrough. 12 layers funded by ~1MB saved from int5 compression. U-Net encoder-decoder split with learned per-dim skip weights. Orthogonal init with 1/sqrt(2*num_layers) output projection scaling. SWA every 50 steps during warmdown. PyTorch 2.8 SDPA.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM
key_findings: Demonstrates that int5 quantization for MLP weights with high-bit zeroing enables better zstd compression, funding an extra layer within the 16MB cap; 3-seed mean BPB is 1.14327.

title: [Non-record] XSA + EMA + TTT: Negative interaction study (val_bpb=1.1436)
technique_details: Stacks TTT on top of XSA+EMA base (from PR #287); TTT uses SGD updates; FA2 (not FA3); 6001 steps at ~100ms/step; TTT adds 67s eval overhead; artifact 15.3 MB
key_findings: Demonstrates a negative interaction: adding TTT to XSA+EMA worsens BPB by +0.016 because both techniques target local context modeling, causing signal double-counting and TTT's SGD updates disrupting the smooth EMA weight landscape.

title: Record: 11L XSA4 + EMA + TTT + Int6 MLP3x (val_bpb=1.1442)
technique_details: Full-model SGD TTT at eval time (3 epochs, lr=0.002, momentum=0.9, first 2 blocks frozen) on dequantized checkpoint before scoring; XSA on last 4 layers; EMA instead of SWA; eval_stride 256→64; MLP 3x expansion; zstd-22 compression; manual KV head repeat for GQA compatibility; RoPE cache cleared before TTT backward pass.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM community cloud; two seeds ran on different nodes causing step time variance; SDPA fallback used (no FA3 installed)
key_findings: TTT at eval time (zero training budget cost) drives mean BPB from ~1.16 to 1.1442, but a maintainer comment flags TTT as potentially outside the spirit of the challenge, putting the submission's validity in question.

title: Submission/qat bigram12k stride32
technique_details: QAT with STE using int5 for MLP and int6 for attention weights. BigramHash increased from 10240 to 12288. Eval stride reduced from 64 to 32. Magnitude pruning 5%. SWA cadence claimed as 25 steps in README but code defaults show 50 steps (Copilot flagged discrepancy). Built on 10L_Int5MLP_MuonWD04_SWA50 baseline. 15.90MB artifact, 6549 steps in 600s.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM
key_findings: Extends the 10L Int5 MLP pipeline with larger BigramHash (12288) and tighter eval stride (32 vs 64), achieving 1.1444 BPB. Copilot flagged a discrepancy between README claiming SWA every 25 steps and code defaulting to 50 steps.

title: Non-record: 11L Depth Recurrence + High-Yield Legal TTT (1.14458 BPB)
technique_details: 11 logical layers from 10 shared BlockCores (depth recurrence). SGD+momentum(0.9) TTT for 3 epochs per 32K chunk with first 2 blocks frozen — yields 2.4x more TTT gain than single-epoch AdamW (−0.0165 vs −0.0068). Partial RoPE 16/64 dims with NTK scaling. Value Embeddings 128d on layers 9-10. LN scale 1/sqrt(layer+1).
hardware_notes: 4xA100-40GB (non-standard; standard is 8xH100)
key_findings: Shows SGD with momentum and 3 epochs per chunk with early-layer freezing yields 2.4x more legal TTT gain than AdamW 1-epoch; depth recurrence provides 11-layer capacity at 10-layer parameter cost.

title: Non-record: QAT Int5/Int6 on #1 architecture (1.14476 BPB)
technique_details: STE fake-quantization during training with int5/int6 export; built on thwu1's #1 entry (Int5-MLP + BigramHash + SWA); key finding that post-training quantization + SWA outperforms QAT by ~0.002 BPB
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM, 600s wallclock
key_findings: Finds that QAT slightly underperforms post-training quantization + SWA on this architecture, suggesting quantization noise from int5/int6 post-training acts as beneficial regularization that QAT inadvertently removes.

title: Submission TrigramHash + PartialRoPE + HeadTemp + stride32 (val_bpb: 1.1450)
technique_details: TrigramHash: 8192-bucket embeddings for token triplets (dim=64). Partial RoPE on 50% of head dims. Per-head temperature scaling (learnable per-head softmax temperature). BigramHash(10240). Int5 MLP/Int6 attention quant. SWA frac=0.4 every 50 steps. Stride-32 sliding eval. LoRA TTT infrastructure added but disabled and later removed from artifact to fit size cap.
key_findings: Introduces TrigramHash (3-gram token hash embeddings) and per-head temperature scaling on a 10L base; artifact trimmed by removing disabled LoRA TTT infrastructure to stay under 16MB, achieving 1.1450 mean BPB over 2 seeds.

title: Record: 10L Int5-MLP + BigramHash(10240) + SWA(0.4) + WD=0.04 (val_bpb=1.1428, mean 3 seeds)
technique_details: Mixed int5/int6 quantization: int5 [-16,15] for MLP weights (1.88x zstd compression vs int6's 1.51x), int6 [-32,31] for attention. Saves 1.86MB enabling a 10th transformer layer. MuonWD=0.04, SWA every 50 steps (~29 checkpoint average). Built on PR #162 with SmearGate, BigramHash, OrthoInit.
key_findings: Novel mixed int5/int6 quantization scheme where int5 MLP weights compress more efficiently with zstd, freeing enough artifact space to add a 10th transformer layer while staying under 16MB.

title: feat: parameter golf v15b - SWA tuned (1.1453 val_bpb)
technique_details: SWA-tuned variant, minimal details available in PR body
key_findings: SWA-tuned submission achieving 1.1453 val_bpb; PR closed with no body content available for further analysis.

title: Non-record: Shared-weight transformer with extended warmdown (1.1454 val_bpb)
technique_details: Single transformer block reused across 9 passes (recurrent-style), per-layer scaling on attention/MLP/residual to break symmetry, U-Net skip connections, step-based warmdown control via WARMDOWN_START_STEP. Training ran ~2.3 hours, exceeding the 10-minute budget.
hardware_notes: Training ran ~2.3 hours on 8×H100, well beyond the 10-minute competition constraint
key_findings: Explores shared-weight (weight-tied depth) transformers with U-Net skips, finding that most improvement occurs during extended warmdown with no plateau observed. Intentionally exceeds the 10-minute budget to study long-horizon convergence dynamics.

title: 11L Int5-MLP + TTT-SGD + SmearGate + SWA (1.1455 BPB)
technique_details: Int5 MLP [-16,15] + Int6 attention [-32,31] saves ~1.9MB to fund 11th layer; full-model SGD TTT for 2 epochs at eval time (~0.005 BPB gain); SmearGate + BigramHash for bigram context injection; 3x MLP expansion with relu^2; SWA over 30 checkpoints; muP scaling with OrthoInit; 3-seed validation pending.
key_findings: Combining Int5 MLP quantization with full-model SGD TTT yields 1.1455 BPB on a single seed; the int5/TTT combination is novel and the eval pipeline (TTT 422s + sliding eval 273s) fits within the 10-min eval budget.

title: Non-record: MoE exploration + multi-bit quantization analysis
technique_details: 2-expert soft-routing MoE (2×1.5x MLP) compared against dense control; MoE run terminated at step 2000 due to pod failure (preliminary). Multi-bit quantization sweep on dense control: int6/int6 baseline 1.1456, int6/int5 +0.0068, int6/int4 +0.0655, int5/int5 +0.0103, int5/int4 +0.0727 BPB. Dense control reached 1.1456 BPB.
hardware_notes: RunPod pod — run terminated early at step 2000
key_findings: Provides quantification evidence that aggressive MLP quantization below int5 is costly at this scale (int4 costs +0.0655 BPB), and presents preliminary (incomplete) evidence that soft-routing MoE underperforms dense at equivalent parameter budget.

title: Record: Int6 MLP3x + SmearGate + BigramHash + MuonWD + SWA (mean val_bpb=1.1483)
technique_details: Per-row int6 [-32,31] quantization with zstd-22 on MLP+attention, fp16 passthrough for tied embeddings and last-layer key projection. 3x MLP expansion (hidden=1536). SmearGate blends token embedding with previous token. BigramHash 4096-bucket table (dim=128->512). Orthogonal init with muP scaling. Muon WD tuned to 0.04, SWA every 50 steps. Updated from initial 1.1483 to 1.1458 via WD/SWA tuning.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM
key_findings: Comprehensive technique stack combining int6 quantization, 3x MLP expansion, SmearGate, BigramHash, and tuned SWA achieving mean 1.1458 BPB across 3 seeds; forms the basis for several subsequent submissions.

title: Exploratory: PR315-derived candidate and looped-depth gate
technique_details: PR315-derived 11-layer candidate with experimental codec and TTT branches behind flags, plus a separate looped-depth gate exploration. Reports both standard quant roundtrip (1.16892776) and sliding-window (1.14586586) BPB. Does not beat PR315 frontier reference of 1.1248 sliding-window BPB.
hardware_notes: 8xH100, stopped at step 4625 on 600s wallclock cap, peak memory 26152 MiB allocated
key_findings: Exploratory PR315-derived submission reaching 1.1459 BPB (sliding window) with experimental TTT and looped-depth gate variants; explicitly does not beat the PR315 reference of 1.1248 and is framed as investigation work only.

title: Add Looped Transformer Design non-record submission (non tuned)
technique_details: PR315-derived looped transformer with a shared recurrent core. Uses Partial RoPE, LN scaling, late QAT, XSA4 (exclusive self-attention on 4 layers), and bigram features. Submitted as an untuned architectural reference point for recurrent-depth design.
key_findings: Untuned looped transformer with shared recurrent core achieves 1.14620 sliding window BPB, submitted as an architectural reference rather than an optimized result. Suggests recurrent-depth / weight-sharing approaches are competitive and warrant further hyperparameter sweeps.

title: Add LLMAdvisor submission: 1.14638 BPB (track_10min_16mb)
technique_details: Mixed int5 MLP / int6 attention quantization with FP16 embeddings and zstd-22 compression. Reduced batch (622,592 tokens) for ~7,370 steps in 600s. SWA boost: every 30 steps, start_frac=0.50, 49 averaged checkpoints. BigramHash size 10,240.
key_findings: 10-layer model with int5/int6 mixed quantization and aggressive SWA (49 checkpoints) achieves 1.14638 BPB at 15.7MB. Reduced batch size trades per-step efficiency for more steps within the time budget.

title: Add LLMAdvisor submission: 1.14638 BPB (track_10min_16mb)
technique_details: 10L with mixed int5 MLP / int6 attention quantization, FP16 embeddings, zstd-22. BigramHash(10240). SWA boost every 30 steps starting at 50% of training, averaging 49 checkpoints. Reduced batch (622592 tokens) for ~7370 steps in 600s.
key_findings: Explores SWA boost with 49 averaged checkpoints starting at 50% training fraction and larger BigramHash(10240), achieving 1.14638 BPB but not beating current SOTA by the required 0.005 margin.

title: Non-record: 11L mixed int5/int6 + working QAT + TTT (val_bpb=1.1466)
technique_details: Fixes dead QAT code in PR #315 (was broken by torch.compile). Mixed int5 MLP / int6 attention quantization with 3% magnitude pruning. TTT: 3 epochs SGD post-quant (83s on 8xH100). BigramHash(10240), 64 learnable memory tokens, backout connection (1 scalar), per-head temperature (88 params), eval stride 32. Used PyTorch SDPA instead of FA3 resulting in 110ms/step.
key_findings: Demonstrates that QAT in PR #315 was dead code due to torch.compile interaction, and provides a working fix. Stacks 8 techniques on top of the #315 baseline but achieves slightly worse BPB (1.1466 vs 1.1248) due to using SDPA instead of FA3 and other tradeoffs.

title: Record: 11L Int5-All + XSA5 + EMA + 10% Pruning (val_bpb=1.1466)
technique_details: Uniform int5 quantization for both MLP and attention weights (prior SOTA used int5 MLP + int6 attention). 10% magnitude pruning after EMA averaging but before quantization, zeroing smallest weights for better zstd compression. Combined artifact reduction from ~15.6MB to 14.8MB. Built on PR #315 XSA+EMA stack. 5,987 steps, pre-quant val_bpb=1.1504.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM
key_findings: Extends the XSA+EMA pipeline with uniform int5 quantization across all weight types and magnitude pruning, reducing artifact size to 14.8MB. However, the 1.1466 BPB score is notably worse than the current frontier (~1.1231-1.1247), and a comment from ElliotSlusky notes this is not a record.

title: Record: 12L + Catalytic Residuals + BigramHash(10240) + SWA + Late QAT (val_bpb=1.1466, mean 3 seeds)
technique_details: Novel catalytic residual connections replace x+f(x) with x+c*f(x) where c is a learned per-dimension vector (~11K extra params, -0.024 BPB). BigramHash(10240). Late QAT with STE int6 quantization at threshold=0.25. SWA from last 20% of warmdown. 12 layers identified as depth sweet spot (-0.023 BPB vs 11L).
key_findings: Introduces catalytic residual connections (learned per-dimension scale on residual branch) for near-zero parameter cost with meaningful BPB reduction. 12-layer depth found to outperform 11-layer despite tighter parameter budget.

title: Record: 11L, int6+zstd, decoupled WD (val_bpb = 1.1472)
technique_details: 11-layer GPT with GQA (NUM_KV_HEADS=4); int6 per-row quantization; fp16 embeddings; zstd-22 compression; sliding window eval stride=64; decoupled weight decay (0.038) on Muon optimizer reduces quantization gap enabling 11L to fit under 16MB; higher LR (0.025); also includes a val-only non-record run achieving 0.92737 mean BPB. Seed 7 experienced Modal preemption/restart.
hardware_notes: Seed 7 experienced Modal preemption and automatic restart, resulting in slower ms/step (~86 vs ~73) but completed within time cap
key_findings: Decoupled weight decay on Muon optimizer reduces the quantization gap sufficiently to fit an 11-layer model under 16MB with int6+zstd; achieves mean 1.1472 BPB across 3 seeds with p=0.0008, well below the 0.01 significance threshold.

title: Submission: 12L Int5-MLP BigramHash10K EMA (1.1476 BPB)
technique_details: Mixed Int5/Int6 quantization (MLP=Int5, Attn=Int6), BigramHash(10240) expanded embeddings, EMA(0.997)+SWA, GPTQ-lite clip search, late QAT, SmearGate, OrthoInit, XSA on last 4 layers, Partial RoPE (16 dims), sliding window eval stride=64. Single seed (1337).
hardware_notes: 8×H100 SXM
key_findings: 12-layer model using mixed Int5 (MLP) / Int6 (attention) quantization with BigramHash(10240) achieves 1.1476 BPB; single-seed result, not claimed as record, built on PR #414 stack.

title: Non-record submission: BigramDim160 + 10% Prune + SWA (1.14767 bpb, 2 seeds)
technique_details: BigramHash dim reduced to 160 (from 192) for reliable sub-16MB artifacts; 10% post-SWA weight pruning of non-embedding linear weights for improved compressibility; zstd-22 compression; 2 of 3 seeds completed due to compute budget exhaustion.
hardware_notes: All runs on 8xH100 SXM; third seed (2024) not completed due to exhausted compute budget on RunPod and Vast.ai
key_findings: Documents artifact size variance as a primary constraint: seed-dependent compression variance can exceed 600KB at BigramHash dim=192, making dim=160 + pruning the reliable solution; submitted with only 2 of 3 seeds due to compute limits.

title: [Record Submission] QAT Int5/Int6 + Backout + U-Net Skips + BigramHash(10240) + SWA50 — val_bpb=1.1477
technique_details: QAT with STE (int5 MLP / int6 attention) reduces quant gap from ~0.016 to ~0.005 BPB; Backout learned residual subtraction (λ·x_mid) from final output; U-Net skip connections (5+5 encoder-decoder) with learned per-dim skip weights; SVD embedding init with spectral decay 1/√k; BigramHash(10240); 8% magnitude pruning; single seed (42), 6457 steps at 92.9ms/step.
key_findings: Combines QAT with novel Backout residual subtraction and learned U-Net skip weights, achieving 1.1477 BPB; the Backout mechanism and per-dim learned skip weights are novel contributions not seen in prior competition submissions.

title: The Frugendorff: Recursive Weight Sharing + MLP 4x (1.1478 BPB, 15.19MB)
technique_details: 6 unique transformer blocks each applied twice in sequence (12 effective layers from 6 stored parameter sets). Parameter savings fund 4x MLP expansion (hidden=2560). Orthogonal loop position embeddings (QR-initialized). Self-distillation with EMA teacher for 50 steps. Alternating full/reduced-depth forward passes during training acts as regularization.
key_findings: Recursive weight sharing (each block applied twice) frees parameter budget for 4x MLP expansion while maintaining 12 effective layers. ~0.8MB artifact headroom remains unused; approach enables variable-depth inference at test time without retraining.

title: The Frugendorff: Recursive Weight Sharing + MLP 4x (1.1478 BPB, 15.19MB)
technique_details: Duplicate of PR #499 — closed by author to resubmit with only submission files. 6 unique blocks x 2 loops = 12 effective depth, MLP 4x, late QAT, training data replay, self-distillation with EMA teacher.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM, 600s
key_findings: Closed duplicate of PR #499; author self-closed to clean up and resubmit with only required submission files.

title: Record: 11L Int6 QAT + SmearGate + OrthoInit + SWA + TTT (val_bpb=1.1478)
technique_details: Full-stack 12-technique submission: 11L MLP-3x, STE Int6 QAT, SmearGate, BigramHash(2048 buckets, dim=128), OrthoInit+muP scaling, SWA (8 checkpoint average during warmdown), TTT (full-weight SGD on val data, lr=0.002, 3 epochs, freeze first 2 blocks), NTK-RoPE base=50000, Muon WD=0.04 momentum=0.99 LR=0.025, zstd-22, sliding window stride=64. Draft PR.
key_findings: Draft submission stacking 12 techniques including TTT (full-weight SGD on validation data) and SWA over 8 checkpoints, achieving 1.1478 BPB with a single seed; notable for TTT phase completing in 73s within the eval budget.

title: Record: 11L Int6 QAT + SmearGate + SWA + SAM: 1.1480 BPB (3-seed mean)
technique_details: First introduction of SAM (Sharpness-Aware Minimization) to the competition. Fractional SAM (frac=0.03, rho=0.05) flattens loss landscape for quantization robustness. Per-dimension SmearGate (512 learnable params). SWA every 50 steps over last 50% of training (~29-30 checkpoints). Int6 QAT with STE, FP16 tied embeddings. Muon momentum=0.99 (warmup 0.92→0.99), WD=0.038. 11L, 512 dim, 8 heads, 4 KV heads, MLP 3x. Best single seed: 1.1453.
key_findings: First use of SAM in this competition; SAM frac=0.20 is the sweet spot giving 0.0007 BPB improvement by encouraging flatter minima that survive int6 quantization. 3-seed mean of 1.1480 with very low variance (std=0.0024), statistically significant improvement over prior SOTA by 0.0268 BPB.

title: Record: SwiGLU+VE128+NoTTT val_bpb=1.1181 (3-seed mean)
technique_details: Original claim of 1.1181 BPB (3-seed mean) invalidated due to artifact size ~20MB exceeding 16MB limit. Corrected submission: 1.14802544 val_bpb, 13.88MB artifact. 11-layer GEPA architecture with SwiGLU FFN (hidden=1792), U-Net skip gates (5 encoder + 6 decoder), XSA on all 11 layers, Value Embeddings VE128 (128-dim, layers 9-10), BigramHash (8192 buckets, 128-dim), EMA decay=0.997, Partial RoPE (16 dims), Late QAT@0.15, Int6+GPTQ-lite+zstd-22. matrix_lr corrected to 0.025, warmdown 6000 steps, LeakyReLU(0.5)^2 activation.
hardware_notes: 8×H100 SXM (Modal), ~18 min/seed for original run
key_findings: Original record claim of 1.1181 BPB was invalidated due to artifact exceeding 16MB; corrected submission achieves 1.14802544 BPB at 13.88MB. Key finding from original work: seq_len=2048 provides -0.008 BPB improvement over seq_len=1024.

title: Add non-record 10min/16MB submission: Wavelet-Lite PR549 Parallel Muon (1.1483)
technique_details: PR #549-derived Parallel Muon stack with a tiny causal wavelet-lite mixer inside each residual block. TTT removed from final run. Bigram table trimmed to fit byte budget. Artifact recovered from persisted full-precision checkpoint after training pod exited. Post-EMA diagnostic val_bpb=1.1400 at wallclock cap.
key_findings: Adds a causal wavelet-lite mixer to each residual block on top of the PR #549 Parallel Muon stack, achieving 1.1483 BPB as a non-record submission. TTT was removed from the final run to fit within the byte budget.

title: 10L MLP3x + BigramHash(2048) + SWA + Stride-32: 1.1487 BPB
technique_details: 10-layer relu² MLP 3x with BigramHash(2048), SmearGate, OrthoInit, mixed int5/int6 quantization, zstd-22, SWA averaging, stride-32 sliding window eval. 14.9 MB artifact.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM, 600s
key_findings: Solid 10L baseline combining MLP 3x, BigramHash, SWA, and stride-32 eval achieving 1.1487 mean BPB; uses mixed int5/int6 quantization and remains under 15MB artifact size.

title: Non-record: 11L Int6 QAT + SmearGate + SWA(0.4) + WD=0.04 (3-seed mean val_bpb=1.1488)
technique_details: SWA_START_FRAC lowered from 0.5 to 0.4 to capture ~33% more checkpoints for smoother weight averaging; Muon WD increased from 0.038 to 0.04 to improve int6 quantization quality. Builds on PR #194 technique stack.
key_findings: Two small hyperparameter tweaks (SWA_START_FRAC=0.4, WD=0.04) reduce the quantization gap and yield mean BPB 1.1488 with notably tight std=0.0006 across 3 seeds, compared to SOTA's std=0.0024.

title: Record: 10L Int5-MLP3x BigramHash4096 SlidingEval — mean val_bpb 1.1489
technique_details: 10-layer transformer with MLP 3x expansion. Int5 quantization with full-run Int6 QAT. BigramHash with 4096 buckets and 128-dim embeddings. Sliding window eval with stride=64. Hyperparameter schedule optimized via Optuna TPE sweep. All 3 seeds under 16MB cap, trained 600s on 8xH100 SXM.
key_findings: Uses Int5 quantization (below the more common Int6) combined with Optuna TPE hyperparameter search for schedule optimization, achieving mean 1.1489 BPB over 3 seeds with tight variance (±0.0003) on a 10-layer model.

title: Weight Entropy Regularization: Improved SWA Averaging (+0.028 BPB)
technique_details: Adds weight entropy regularization loss term (loss += λ * weight_entropy) computed via torch.histc over all weight tensors every step; zero effect during normal training but improves SWA averaging by 0.028 BPB; ~12% training overhead. Run incomplete — interrupted at step 8500.
hardware_notes: Run on 1xH100 with 20 shards; seeking 8xH100 for full run
key_findings: Weight entropy regularization has no impact during normal training but significantly improves SWA checkpoint averaging, with a growing gap of 0.028 BPB at step 8500; mechanism is that entropy-regularized weights are more consistent across checkpoints. Run incomplete and not yet at SOTA.

title: Record: Batch-Optimized 524K + Warmdown 4000 (val_bpb 1.1497)
technique_details: Two hyperparameter changes only: TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS reduced to 524288 (more optimizer steps per wall-clock minute) and WARMDOWN_ITERS increased to 4000 to match the longer effective training horizon. No architecture or code changes from the #1 baseline.
key_findings: Reducing batch size to 524K tokens and extending warmdown to 4000 steps yields mean BPB 1.14970 across 3 seeds, demonstrating that optimizer step frequency and warmdown tuning are meaningful levers even without architectural changes.

title: Record: 11L Int6+Zstd MLP3x SmearGate BigramHash OrthoInit MuonWD EMA (mean val_bpb=1.1497)
technique_details: 11L 512d with MLP 3x expansion, INT6 quantization + Zstd compression level 22, SmearGate, BigramHash, orthogonal initialization, Muon optimizer with WD=0.02, EMA decay 0.997, FP16 tied embeddings, seq_len=2048, sliding window eval stride=256. Artifact ~14.8MB. Ablations of AttnRes, depth recurrence, seq-len curriculum, TTT documented in README.
key_findings: Combines a broad stack of complementary techniques (quantization+compression, architectural gating, optimizer improvements, EMA) achieving mean 1.1497 BPB with very low variance (std=0.0004) across 3 seeds.

title: Update: 11L MLP3x + WD=0.04 + zstd-22 (val_bpb 1.1502)
technique_details: 11 layers, MLP 3x expansion, Muon WD=0.04, zstd-22 compression. 5-seed results with t-stat reported. Details in README.
key_findings: 11-layer model with MLP3x expansion and WD=0.04 achieves 1.1502 BPB (mean 5 seeds), demonstrating that adding depth via int6/zstd savings combined with tuned weight decay yields strong results.

title: Record: 11L Int6 QAT + SmearGate + WD 0.038 (val_bpb=1.1502)
technique_details: 11-layer GPT (512 dim, 8 heads, 4 KV heads, MLP 3x) with Int6 STE QAT stored in int8 containers + zstd-22 compression (26.5M params → 15.5MB). SmearGate learned gate blending current + previous token embedding (~513 params). Decoupled Muon WD=0.038. Sliding window eval stride=64, batch=32.
key_findings: Extends the Int6 QAT + SmearGate recipe to 11 layers with MLP 3x expansion and higher Muon weight decay (0.038), achieving 1.1502 BPB on a single seed — the lowest reported score in this batch. Additional seeds not yet run.

title: [10min/16MB] AWQ + Cyclic Momentum + ReLU² + 11L Shared — 1.1507 bpb
technique_details: AWQ (activation-aware weight scaling, α=0.5) before int5/int6 quantization closed 63% of quant gap (0.027→0.010 bpb). Cyclic Muon momentum as triangle wave 0.85–0.95 (period=50) after warmup to escape sharp minima. ReLU² activation. 11-layer with last block weight-shared (10 unique weight sets). Developed with 21+ experiments on 1×H100 before promoting to 8×H100. Tested and rejected: MTP, curriculum learning, TTT, GPTQ-lite, layer-aware quant, EMA, QAT, AdaptiveRMSNorm, partial RoPE, value embeddings.
hardware_notes: 8×H100 SXM, 600s; development on 1×H100
key_findings: AWQ (activation-aware quantization scaling) and cyclic Muon momentum are the clear winners, with AWQ alone closing 63% of the quantization gap. 11-layer weight sharing (last block reused) provides free depth at no parameter cost.

title: 10L Int5-MLP + BigramHash(4096) + SWA (1.1507 BPB)
technique_details: Mixed int5 MLP / int6 attention with zstd-22 compression. BigramHash reduced to 4096 buckets (dim=128) for reliable size margin across seeds. SWA with frac=0.4, Muon WD=0.04, warmdown=3000.
key_findings: Builds on thwu1's 10L Int5-MLP submission with reduced BigramHash dimensionality to ensure artifact size stays reliably under 16MB across all seeds; demonstrates stable 3-seed results with mixed int5/int6 quantization.

title: Record: Int6 STE + SmearGate + Seq2048 + OrthoInit + RoPE50K + SWA/100 (mean val_bpb=1.1507)
technique_details: Builds on PR #156 stack; adds SmearGate (learned gate blending token embeddings with predecessors, ~512 params), orthogonal init on all non-zero-init linear layers, seq len doubled to 2048 with RoPE base 50K, SWA every 100 steps (more frequent than prior). Evolved over 31 AIDE2 optimization steps. 9L, 512 dim, 8 heads, 4 KV heads (GQA), relu^2, logit softcapping 30.0, sliding window stride=64 giving ~1984 token context.
key_findings: Claims SOTA at mean 1.15072 BPB (3-seed, p<0.001), beating merged SOTA (1.1748) by 0.024; key additions over PR #156 are SmearGate, orthogonal init, doubled sequence length (2048), and more frequent SWA checkpointing, evolved via AIDE2 automated optimization.

title: Record: 10L d=512 Int5-MLP Int6-Attn sp1024 (val_bpb=1.1508)
technique_details: 10L, d=512, 8 attention heads, 4 KV heads (GQA 2:1), sp1024 tokenizer, Int5-MLP + Int6-Attn quantization, BigramHash, SmearGate, Muon + SWA stack from PR #162. Key finding: Int6 embedding quantization enables large-vocab models with negligible quality cost (+0.0005 bpb), and sp1024 with 10L at d=512 outperforms sp8192 configs due to layer count advantage. Local testing on Apple Silicon (MPS) used for architecture search at 500 steps.
hardware_notes: Local architecture search on Apple Silicon (MPS); final runs on 8×H100 SXM (RunPod)
key_findings: Systematic architecture search across tokenizer sizes (sp1024–sp8192) and widths finds sp1024 with 10L d=512 is optimal, as more layers at full width outweigh tokenizer efficiency gains. Also demonstrates Int6 embedding quantization as a near-free technique to enable larger vocabularies within the artifact budget.

title: FP8 + Arithmetic Coding + SWA (1.1511 BPB)
technique_details: FP8 training via TransformerEngine (E4M3 fwd / E5M2 bwd) for 1.3-1.5x throughput; custom arithmetic coder replacing zstd-22 using per-tensor empirical histograms; early SWA start at step 4500; TF32 matmul for non-FP8 ops; 10L, 512dim, MLP 3x, SmearGate, BigramHash(10240)
key_findings: Replaces zstd-22 with a custom arithmetic coder approaching Shannon entropy, combined with FP8 training for throughput gains. Achieves 1.1511 BPB building on the SOTA Int5-MLP + BigramHash + SWA baseline.

title: SmearGate + BigramHash + Int6 + SWA + U-Net Skips (1.1518 BPB)
technique_details: 11-layer GPT with seq_len=1024; 3x MLP expansion (hidden=1536) with relu² activation; SmearGate for learned token-predecessor blending at input; BigramHash 2048-bucket hash table (dim=128); U-Net skip connections with learned per-dimension weights between encoder/decoder layers; Muon optimizer WD=0.04 momentum warmup 0.92→0.99; SWA 7 snapshots every 200 steps during warmdown; int6 per-row + zstd-22; TTT LoRA eval also available (1.1535 BPB).
key_findings: Independently developed stack featuring novel U-Net skip connections with learned per-dimension weights and seq_len=1024 tradeoff enabling more layers and training steps; BigramHash and SmearGate provide cheap token-pair context; single-seed result modestly above current #3.

title: Non-record: 11L int5/int6 + XSA + online TTT w/ decay prior (single-run val_bpb=1.1520)
technique_details: Pre-Q/K RMSNorm on attention input before Q and K projections (V gets raw input) for stability under int5/int6; online causal TTT with Krause-style decay prior (p += λ(p₀-p)) on MLP weights in last 3 blocks; Reptile meta-learning in last 10% of training (K=1 inner step); XSA in last 3 layers; int5-MLP/int6-attn; BigramHash(10240); 15.1 MB artifact
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM
key_findings: Introduces online TTT with a decay prior to prevent weight drift and Reptile meta-learning to improve TTT adaptation quality; Pre-Q/K RMSNorm proposed as a stabilization technique for lower-precision attention projections.

title: Record: 10L CountInitBigram + XSA + PartialRoPE (val_bpb=1.1522)
technique_details: Novel count-initialized exact bigram logit head: 1024x1024 lookup table initialized from corpus bigram transition probabilities before training; int4 nibble packing for bigram table (~524KB vs ~1MB); XSA on last 4 layers; Partial RoPE 16/64 dims; LN Scale 1/sqrt(layer+1); zstd-22; sliding window eval stride=64.
key_findings: Introduces a corpus-statistics-initialized bigram logit head as a strong Markov prior from step 0, allowing the neural network to only refine beyond bigram predictions; int4 nibble packing halves the table's storage cost.

title: Record: 10L CountInitBigram + XSA + PartialRoPE (val_bpb=1.1522)
technique_details: Count-initialized exact bigram logit head (1024x1024 LUT from corpus bigram transition probabilities, B[a,b]=log p(b|a)-log p(b)), applied before logit softcap. Int4 nibble packing halves bigram table storage to ~524KB. XSA on last 4 layers, Partial RoPE 16/64 dims, LN scale 1/sqrt(layer+1), higher LR 0.025, U-Net skips. Reproducibility issue flagged by Copilot: BIGRAM_LOGIT_HEAD defaults to 0 but submission claims it active by default.
key_findings: Introduces a count-initialized bigram logit head providing a strong Markov prior from step 0, combined with Int4 nibble packing for compact storage; reproducibility concerns raised by automated review regarding default hyperparameter not matching documented run command.

title: Record: 10L CountInitBigram + XSA + PartialRoPE (val_bpb=1.1522)
technique_details: Novel count-initialized exact bigram logit head: 1024x1024 lookup table initialized from corpus bigram transition probabilities, Int4 nibble-packed (524KB). XSA on last 4 layers. Partial RoPE 16/64 dims. LN scale 1/sqrt(layer+1). Leaky ReLU squared activation (negative_slope=0.5). Post-SWA int5/int6+zstd-22 quantization.
key_findings: Introduces a count-initialized bigram logit head using corpus transition probabilities as a strong Markov prior from step 0, stored as an Int4 nibble-packed 1024x1024 table. Reproducibility concerns noted: BIGRAM_LOGIT_HEAD defaults to disabled and warmdown iters mismatch between README and code.

title: Submission: OrthoInit + Int6 MLP3x + SmearGate + BigramHash (val_bpb: 1.1524)
technique_details: Orthogonal init with muP scaling (1/√(2·layers)), 3x MLP hidden=1536, per-row Int6 on MLP+attention with Int8 on embeddings and zstd-22, SmearGate learned sigmoid blending token with previous token embedding, 4096-bucket bigram hash embedding, Muon LR=0.02 momentum=0.99, seq_len=2048 with NTK-aware RoPE, FlashAttention 3. Best of 3 seeds submitted (seed 42); mean across seeds is 1.1538.
key_findings: Stacking 8 orthogonal techniques yields a 0.0135 BPB improvement over prior submission (PR #70), with the combination of Int6 quantization freeing parameter budget for a 3x MLP expansion being particularly notable. Training at seq_len=2048 with sliding window eval at stride=256 contributes meaningfully despite fewer total steps.

title: PROTEUS v9 — 11L INT6 + single-epoch LoRA TTT (mean val_bpb=1.1526, 3 seeds)
technique_details: 11L/512d, 8/4 GQA heads, 26.8M params, INT6 GPTQ-lite, zstd-22, ~15.4MB artifact; single-epoch score-first TTT (TTT_EPOCHS=1) — each 256-token chunk scored before LoRA trains on it; TTT_MIN_DOC_LEN=512; addresses legality ruling from PR #568 by reducing from multi-epoch to single-epoch to avoid training on eval data.
key_findings: Resolves multi-epoch TTT legality concern by reducing to exactly one epoch of score-first LoRA TTT, achieving 1.1526 BPB mean (3 seeds) and improving from post-quant ~1.177 BPB; demonstrates that single-epoch backward-looking TTT is both legal and provides meaningful gains.

title: Non-record submission: Depth Recurrence + Legal Score-First TTT (10L, 1.1532 BPB)
technique_details: Competition-legal score-first full-model TTT: chunked 32K-token sliding window, scores each chunk first (inference_mode), then one AdamW step per chunk with cosine LR decay across chunks. No LoRA, no resets between documents — persistent adaptation. 3x ReLU² MLP, BigramHash(10240), SmearGate, XSA last 3 layers, U-Net skips, GQA 2:1. Depth recurrence infrastructure present but inactive.
hardware_notes: 4xA100-40GB (non-standard; standard is 8xH100)
key_findings: First competition submission with full-model (non-LoRA) legal score-first TTT yielding −0.0068 BPB improvement; persistent adaptation across entire validation set without document resets is the novel contribution over prior LoRA-based TTT.

title: Add ContextFuse-2048-BigramSmear submission
technique_details: Builds on ContextFuse-2048 (PR #143) adding BigramHash token-pair features, SmearGate input smoothing, mixed int6 export, SWA checkpoint averaging, Muon weight decay, and corrected bigram control-tensor handling. Fixes sliding-window evaluator bug that double-counted tail tokens in truncated windows when EVAL_STRIDE < TRAIN_SEQ_LEN. Withdraws multi-seed mean claim due to eval fix.
key_findings: Corrects a sliding-window evaluation bug that caused double-counting of tail tokens, improving reported val_bpb from 1.17792945 to 1.15369565 for this submission; stacks BigramHash and SmearGate on top of the ContextFuse-2048 baseline.

title: Record: OrthoInit + Int6 MLP3x + BigramHash + SmearGate (val_bpb: 1.1539)
technique_details: Orthogonal init with muP scaling for output projections (1/sqrt(2*num_layers)); Int6 per-row on MLP and attention weights with FP16 passthrough for embeddings and last-2-layer Key projections; zstd-22; MLP hidden=1536 (3x); SmearGate (~512 params learned gate blending current and previous token embeddings); BigramHash 4096-bucket hash table (dim=128 projected to 512); sliding window stride=64 seq_len=2048; warmdown=3000, muon_momentum=0.99 with warmup from 0.92 over 1500 steps.
key_findings: Achieves 1.1539 BPB by adding SmearGate and BigramHash token-context embeddings on top of the Int6+MLP3x+sliding window stack, with orthogonal muP-scaled initialization; SmearGate and BigramHash are novel architectural additions for this competition.

title: Non-record: 12L Int5-MLP + Int6-Attn mixed quantization, val_bpb=1.1541
technique_details: Int5 [-16,15] for MLP weights (stored in int8 container, compresses ~1.88x with zstd vs ~1.51x for int6), Int6 [-32,31] for attention weights, FP16 for tied embeddings. Savings from int5 fund a 12th transformer layer. 3x MLP expansion (hidden=1536), SmearGate learned token blending, BigramHash (2048 buckets, dim=128), U-Net skip connections, orthogonal+muP-scaled init, sliding window stride=64.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM
key_findings: Deepest model submitted (12 layers) enabled by novel int5 MLP quantization which achieves better compression than int6, freeing space for an extra layer; however the slower per-step time (107ms vs 81ms for 11L) reduces training steps and does not beat current SOTA.

title: Record: Int6 + MLP 3x + NorMuon + SmearGate + BigramHash + OrthoInit + Sliding Window, val_bpb=1.1541
technique_details: Seven techniques stacked: Int6 per-row quantization with FP16 scales and FP16 tied embeddings, 3x MLP expansion (hidden=1536), NorMuon with per-row normalized Newton-Schulz and decoupled WD=0.02, SmearGate (learned gate blending with previous token, ~512 params), BigramHash (4096-bucket hash embedding for token pairs, dim=64), orthogonal+muP init with 1/sqrt(2L) output scaling, sliding window eval stride=256 with 2048-token windows. TRAIN_SEQ_LEN=2048, MUON_MOMENTUM=0.99, WARMDOWN_ITERS=3000. 7332 steps at 81.84ms/step.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM (RunPod)
key_findings: Stacks seven complementary techniques reaching 1.1541 BPB, with SmearGate and BigramHash as novel additions on top of the established Int6+MLP3x+sliding-window meta. Pre-quant BPB is 1.1644, sliding window eval contributes an additional ~0.021 BPB improvement over the int6 roundtrip score.

title: Record submission : Int6 + MLP 3x + Flash Attention 3 + NorMuon, val_bpb = 1.1532
technique_details: Int6 post-training quantization with per-row scaling, MLP hidden size 1024->1536 (3x expansion), fp16 tied embeddings and final c_k.weight in fp16, train seq_len=2048, sliding window eval stride=256, GRAD_CLIP_NORM=0.3. NorMuon replaces Muon; FlashAttention 3 kernel on H100s. Best single seed: 1.1532 (seed 7), mean 1.1546.
hardware_notes: Trained on Modal with 8xH100
key_findings: NorMuon optimizer and FlashAttention 3 kernel replace Muon and standard attention yielding a repeatable improvement; mean val_bpb of 1.1546 over 3 seeds with best seed reaching 1.1532, building on Int6+MLP3x baseline from PR #114.

title: LAWA-EMA frontier fork (pr198 base, SWA -> LAWA val_bpb=1.1551)
technique_details: Replaces SWA with LAWA-EMA (decay=0.995, float32 shadow, every-step update). Adds Overtone init (SVD power-law embedding spectrum for smoother int6 quantization). Fixes bigram proj zero-init override and sliding window partial-window overlap. Built on PR #198 (11L, int6, MLP3x, relu², FA3, SmearGate, BigramHash, OrthoInit, U-Net skips, WD=0.04).
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM
key_findings: Replaces SWA with LAWA-EMA and adds Overtone init, achieving a smaller 12.7MB artifact but regressing BPB to 1.1551 vs PR #198's 1.1318. Single seed only; multi-seed validation pending.

title: feat(arch): Mish² Activation & PyTorch Native SDPA GQA Core (1.155 BPB) 8xH100
technique_details: 11L with Mish² activation (F.mish(x).square()), MLP 3x, Partial RoPE (16/64 dims), layer-scaled LayerNorm (1/√(layer+1)), BigramHash(1536); replaced flash_attn with PyTorch native F.scaled_dot_product_attention with GQA via repeat_interleave; legal score-first TTT; Mac MLX compatibility layer also developed.
hardware_notes: Flash-attn dependency issue on RunPod required workaround using PyTorch native SDPA
key_findings: Introduces Mish² activation (smooth non-monotonic alternative to LeakyReLU²) and replaces flash_attn with PyTorch native SDPA for GQA compatibility; achieves 1.1552 BPB at step 5470 but does not claim SOTA and scores above current leaderboard leaders.

title: Add PR114 RunPod H100 SXM non-record submission
technique_details: Long-context selective-precision recipe from PR114, rerun on RunPod 8x H100 SXM. Uses int8+zlib compression. Three same-provider reruns (seeds 1337-1339) give one-sided p=0.00306 against older 1.1698 threshold, but intentionally non-record as leaderboard moved to 1.1428.
hardware_notes: RunPod 8x H100 SXM
key_findings: Packages PR114 long-context selective-precision recipe with verified RunPod reruns achieving p=0.00306 significance over an older threshold, but explicitly deferred as non-record after the leaderboard advanced to 1.1428.

title: Record: Mixed Quant Int6/FP16 + SmearGate + OrthoInit + MLP 3x + Sliding Window, val_bpb=1.1556
technique_details: SmearGate blends current and previous token embeddings via learned per-dim gate (init~0.95). 4096-bucket bigram hash embedding (dim=128 projected to 512). MLP 3x expansion (1536 hidden for 512-dim). U-Net skip connections between encoder (4L) and decoder (5L) halves. Int6 STE QAT on all 2D weights, fp16 passthrough for tied embeddings. Sliding window eval stride=64. Zstd-22 compression. Muon with decoupled WD=0.01 and momentum warmup 0.92->0.99. Orthogonal weight init.
key_findings: Stacks SmearGate bigram embedding blending, orthogonal init, 3x MLP expansion, U-Net skip connections, and int6 STE QAT to achieve 1.1556 BPB (sliding window) / 1.1891 BPB (standard eval), with near-zero quantization gap.

title: Non-Record: 11L Low-Rank on Q192 (val_bpb=1.1548) 14.7MB in decimal
technique_details: Low-rank Q factorization r=192 (512→192→512, saving 25% params vs full Q); Q matrices have extreme condition numbers (100M+) indicating naturally low-dimensional subspace; only 89-114 effective dims used out of 192; reduces step time from 108ms to 77ms (+28% steps); K/V/O are full-rank and not factored; U-Net encoder-decoder skips (5+6 layers); Int6 per-row + zstd-22; FP16 tied embeddings; Late-K passthrough last 2 layers; sliding window stride=64.
key_findings: Weight analysis reveals Q projection matrices operate in a low-dimensional subspace (extreme condition numbers), enabling r=192 low-rank factorization that cuts step time 22% and fits 2 extra layers in budget; K, V, O matrices are full-rank and do not benefit from the same treatment.

title: Record: 1.1558 BPB — 11L U-Net + Catalytic + SwiGLU + SW64
technique_details: Gated U-Net skip connections (sigmoid-gated encoder→decoder blending), catalytic residuals (learned per-dim gates on attn/MLP outputs init=1.0), SwiGLU with 3x expansion, value residual blending first-layer V into all subsequent layers, LN scale dampening 1/sqrt(layer_idx+1), decoder LR multiplier 2x for Muon and Adam, Int5/Int6 mixed quantization + zstd-22, BigramHash 4096 buckets, partial RoPE 25%, XSA last 4 layers, EMA decay=0.9985.
key_findings: Combines U-Net skip connections, catalytic residuals, SwiGLU, and value residual in a single 11-layer model achieving 1.1558 BPB; uses Int5/Int6 mixed quantization and a decoder LR multiplier to boost the U-Net decoder pathway.

title: 11L XSA4 + SmearGate + BigramHash + SWA + RoPE50K (mean val_bpb=1.1565, 3 seeds)
technique_details: 11L XSA on last 4 layers, SmearGate+BigramHash(2048)+OrthoInit, INT6 per-row quant+zstd-22, FP16 tied embedding, Late-K FP16, SWA every 50 steps in fp32 (bf16 causes catastrophic loss), Muon WD=0.04, RoPE base 50K, Overtone SVD init, phase-transition residual mixing, MLP 2.75x (3x exceeds 16MB with SmearGate at 11L). Updated version of PR #186 with improved results.
hardware_notes: 23 GPU runs on 8xH100 SXM5
key_findings: Systematic 23-run exploration finding SWA far superior to EMA for quantization gap (EMA causes 0.14 BPB gap), and that MLP 2.75x is the sweet spot when SmearGate+BigramHash are present at 11L. Fresh PR superseding closed PR #186.

title: 11L XSA + SmearGate + BigramHash + SWA (mean val_bpb=1.1565, 3 seeds)
technique_details: 11L XSA on last 4 layers, SmearGate+BigramHash(2048)+OrthoInit, INT6 per-row quant+zstd-22, FP16 tied embedding, Late-K FP16, SWA every 50 steps fp32, Muon WD=0.04, RoPE base 50K, Overtone SVD init, phase-transition residual mixing, MLP 2.75x. Updated from initial 1.1754 to 1.1538 best seed over 23 runs.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM5
key_findings: Closed by author in favor of fresh PR #333 with identical final results but cleaner history; key finding is EMA causes large quantization gap while SWA does not.

title: Staging: Int6 MLP3x 11L + SmearGate + BigramHash4096x128 + MuonWD038 + SWA50 + DocSliding (single-run val_bpb=1.1568)
technique_details: 11-layer 512-dim model with Int6 quantization, 3x MLP expansion, SmearGate gating, BigramHash(4096x128), MUON_WEIGHT_DECAY=0.038, SWA_EVERY=50 starting at 50% training, doc-level sliding window eval (2048/256 stride), int6_zstd_core export path. Built-in export exceeded 16MB cap so score comes from re-export path.
key_findings: Single-run staging submission showing 0.018 BPB improvement over the then-current leaderboard leader, but more runs needed for p<0.01 significance before making a formal SOTA claim. Notable that the default integrated export exceeded the 16MB cap, requiring a separate re-export path.

title: Add 2026-03-20 11L dense-lexical submission candidate
technique_details: int6_zstd_core export with doc_sliding eval (2048/256 stride); SmearGate activations; BigramHash with 4096x128 vocab; Muon WD=0.038; SWA 50 snapshots; integrated export path was slightly over 16MB cap so score comes from legal re-export path
key_findings: Dense-lexical 11L model combining Int6, SmearGate, BigramHash, and SWA achieves val_bpb=1.1568 as a single-run candidate; artifact was trimmed from over-cap integrated export to a legal re-export path.

title: Record Submission: 1.1570 BPB - 73.7M Ternary U-Net + NeoMuon + 4x relu²MLP + Factored Tied Emb + Poly5 Softcap + YaRN2048 + 8192BPE + FP8QAT + Bitmask-LZMA + Stride-16 Sliding
technique_details: 73.7M param ternary (BitNet b1.58, {-1,0,+1}) 10-layer transformer with dim=768, 8 heads, 4 KV heads (GQA), head_dim=96. 4x MLP expansion with relu² activation. U-Net encoder/decoder with learned skip weights. Factored tied embedding (8192×254 bottleneck). Polynomial softcap degree-5 cap=10 with Z-loss (1e-4). YaRN positional encoding (max_len=2048, ROPE_BASE=5000). FP8 QAT (e4m3) halves fp_params. Base-3 LZMA preset=9 compression (39% reduction vs int8+zlib). Stride-16 sliding window eval. Temperature scaling T=0.90. NeoMuon with 3 Newton-Schulz steps. 8192 BPE vocab. Shrinkage fix for ternary zero-fraction scale mismatch.
key_findings: Achieves 1.1570 BPB (3-seed mean) using BitNet b1.58 ternary quantization combined with U-Net skip connections, relu² MLP, FP8 QAT, and base-3 LZMA compression. Width-over-depth (768d/10L) preferred over 512d/25L due to faster steps enabling more training iterations in the 600s budget.

title: Record: val_bpb=1.1574 — Int6 + MLP 3x + selective precision + optimized long-context training
technique_details: Int6 per-row quantization (±31 range) compresses weights ~25% smaller than int8, enabling MLP hidden 1024→1536 (3x). FP16 tied embedding preserves dual-role embedding/output matrix. Late-K passthrough keeps last 2 layers' key projections in fp16. Train@2048 found optimal for fixed-time budget; batch=786K optimal after sweep; GRAD_CLIP_NORM=0.3 stabilizes long-sequence training.
key_findings: Int6 quantization frees artifact space for 3x MLP expansion while selective FP16 preservation (tied embedding + last-2-layer keys) mitigates quantization damage to sensitive tensors. Train@2048 with batch=786K identified as optimal configuration for fixed 10-minute budget.

title: submission: 10L Int5-MLP + Aggressive Warmdown (WD=20000) — targeting <1.14 bpb
technique_details: Aggressive warmdown_iters=20000 makes the entire training run a cosine decay phase from step 0. Ablation shows this reduces post-quantization BPB penalty from 0.014 (WD=1200) to 0.005 (WD=20000). Validated on RTX 4090; 8xH100 run requested to confirm.
hardware_notes: Local validation only on RTX 4090; 8xH100 CI run requested but not yet completed
key_findings: Proposes that an extremely aggressive warmdown schedule (warmdown_iters=20000, spanning the entire run) produces weights that quantize significantly better under Int5/Int6, reducing the post-quantization BPB penalty by ~64%. Full 8xH100 validation pending.

title: Non-record: 10L Int6 QAT + SmearGate + SWA (val_bpb=1.1575)
technique_details: 10 layers × 512 dim, 8 heads, 4 KV heads, 3x MLP. Int6 QAT with STE, per-dim SmearGate, SWA with window 50, Muon WD=0.038, sliding window eval stride=64, zstd-22. 14.73MB artifact, 9,156 steps at 65ms/step. Single seed 1337, self-verified.
key_findings: Demonstrates that 10L outperforms 11L under the 10-minute wall-clock constraint: 65ms/step vs 80ms/step yields 22% more training steps (9,156 vs 7,472), more than compensating for reduced model capacity. Informed by systematic analysis across 17 experiments.

title: record: 1.158
technique_details: Credits PR #88 as source; no implementation details provided.
key_findings: Claims a record BPB of 1.158 with no technical details, crediting PR #88 for inspiration.

title: Record: 11L XSA4 + EMA + Partial RoPE + Rank-8 TTT Hooks (1.1591 bpb)
