technique_details: 11 layers with XSA on last 4 layers, EMA, Partial RoPE, layerwise LN scale, mixed int6/int8 export with zstd, integrated long-document LoRA TTT hooks (rank-8). Single seed (42) exact roundtrip result only; sliding-window and adaptive-TTT scores not recovered due to container loss.
hardware_notes: RunPod container disappeared after exact roundtrip metric; longer post-train eval variants not recovered
key_findings: Achieves 1.1591 BPB (single seed, exact roundtrip) with a PR315-derived stack including long-document LoRA TTT hooks, but is self-described as non-SOTA and limited to one seed due to infrastructure issues.

title: Record: Int6 MLP3x + STE QAT + Sliding Window (val_bpb=1.1594)
technique_details: Int6 per-row quantization with zstd-22 compression; MLP hidden=1536 (3x); STE fake-int6 QAT throughout training; FP16 tied embedding passthrough; sliding window eval stride=64 seq_len=4096; matrix_lr=0.02, muon_momentum=0.99, warmdown=3000. Built with Claude Code as autonomous research agent.
key_findings: Six-technique stack achieving 1.1594 BPB post-quantization; bot reviewer flagged potential issues with warmup not counted in training budget and validation set truncation.

title: Add SP4096 11L432 MLP3x Int6+Zstd Momentum99 record (val_bpb=1.1596)
technique_details: SP-4096 tokenizer, 11 layers × dim 432, 3x MLP expansion (relu²), Muon momentum 0.99, decoupled WD 0.02, warmdown 3000 iters. Int6 PTQ (levels=31) + zstd-22, FP16 embedding passthrough. Sliding window eval stride=64. 20.3M params → 15.3MB artifact. 9237 steps in 600s.
key_findings: Claims new SOTA at 1.1596 BPB (Δ=−0.0157 from previous 1.1753), achieved by switching from 10L×496d with 2x MLP to 11L×432d with 3x MLP and boosting Muon momentum from 0.95 to 0.99, trading width for depth and larger MLP expansion.

title: Record: 10L Int6 QAT + Zstd MLP2.6x Muon0.99 Sliding Window (val_bpb 1.1598)
technique_details: STE int6 QAT eliminates quantization gap entirely (pre-quant = post-quant). MLP hidden 1344 (2.625x model_dim). FP16 tied embedding passthrough. Seq len 2048. Muon momentum 0.99 with warmup from 0.92 over 1500 steps. Grad clip 0.3. Warmdown 3600 steps. Sliding window eval stride=64. Zstd-22 compression. 3-seed mean: seeds 1337, 42, 3.
key_findings: Achieves 1.1598 mean BPB (3 seeds) by combining STE int6 QAT (completely eliminating quant gap), 2.625x MLP expansion, fp16 tied embeddings, and sliding window evaluation, stacking 11 techniques on the naive baseline for a large cumulative gain.

title: Non-record: WiderMLP + FP16 Embed + Stride-32 (val_bpb=1.1601)
technique_details: MLP_MULT=3 (hidden=1536) exceeds 16MB cap at 18.97MB. Mixed int6 (layers 2-8, step=4) and int8 (layers 0/1/9) per-row quantization. FP16 tied embedding passthrough. Stride-32 sliding window eval with 4064 token context takes ~17min exceeding eval limit. Muon momentum=0.99, warmup from 0.92 over 1500 steps, warmdown_iters=3000.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 80GB HBM3 (RunPod), PyTorch 2.4.1+cu124
key_findings: Non-record submission exceeding both artifact size (18.97MB vs 16MB) and eval time (~17min vs 10min) limits; demonstrates that wider MLP (3x) with mixed int6/int8 quantization and stride-32 sliding window eval achieves 1.1601 BPB.

title: feat(record): Int6 STE + NorMuon + SWA + Sliding Window (val_bpb=1.16019)
technique_details: Int6 STE: fake int6 per-row quantization [-31,31] on every forward pass with straight-through gradient bypass. NorMuon: row-normalized Newton-Schulz updates with adaptive step sizes. 3x MLP width (hidden=1536). Sliding window stride=64 giving ~960 token context. SWA with 7 checkpoint average during warmdown. Zstd-22 compression. U-Net encoder-decoder skip connections with learnable weights. 9L, 512 dim, 8 heads, 4 KV heads (GQA), relu^2, logit softcapping 30.0.
key_findings: Claims SOTA at mean 1.16019 BPB across 3 seeds, beating prior merged SOTA (1.17475) by 0.01456; key innovations are Int6 STE (quantization-aware training) and NorMuon optimizer combined with wider MLP enabled by int6 compression savings.

title: Record: Sliding Window Eval, 2048 Vocab Size, fp16 embeddings, SWA, NorMuon, FA3; mean_val_bpb:1.160
technique_details: SP2048 tokenizer; row-wise int6 QAT with STE on weights, embeddings kept in FP16; sliding window eval (stride=64, seqlen=1024); SWA averaging 7 checkpoints during warmdown; NorMuon optimizer; FlashAttention 3; 3× MLP expansion (hidden 1536); zstd-22 compression; 3-seed mean reported.
hardware_notes: Hyperbolic 8×H100 SXM5
key_findings: Combines high vocab size (2048), FP16 embeddings, int6 QAT, SWA, NorMuon, FA3, and sliding window eval into a single stack achieving a 3-seed mean sliding-window BPB of 1.160. Notes a tradeoff between vocab diversity and representation resolution requiring one fewer layer to accommodate larger vocab.

title: submission: Int6 MLP3x + Late-K Passthrough + SlidingWindow (val_bpb: 1.1605)
technique_details: Int6 per-row quantization on MLP, c_q, c_v, proj; late-layer K passthrough (blocks.7 and blocks.8 c_k kept in fp16); remaining c_k in grouped int8 (group_size=64); fp16 tied embedding; zstd compression; 3x MLP (hidden=1536); sliding window eval stride=64; 9 layers, 512-dim, GQA (8 heads, 4 KV). Submitted code is minified to save bytes within 16MB cap.
key_findings: Demonstrates selective K-projection preservation strategy where only the two deepest layers' key projections are kept in fp16 while others use grouped int8, yielding 1.1605 BPB within the 16MB cap; provides 3-seed validation (seeds 2025, 42, 4242) showing consistent results.

title: Non-record: 11L Int6 + Online Logit Bias (val_bpb=1.1609)
technique_details: 11L model with Int6 quantization and an online logit bias technique. No further details provided in PR body.
key_findings: Exploratory submission testing online logit bias on an 11L Int6 baseline; self-described as non-record at 1.1609 BPB with no additional description in the PR body.

title: Int6 MLP3x + Tuned LR + SmearGate + SlidingWindow (val_bpb: 1.1618)
technique_details: Int6 per-row quantization on MLP and attention 2D weights, fp16 tied embedding passthrough, zstd-22; 3x MLP expansion (hidden=1536); SmearGate blends token embedding with previous token embedding via learned sigmoid gate (512 params); halved learning rates (matrix_lr=0.02), Muon momentum=0.99 with warmup from 0.92 over 1500 steps, warmdown=3000 iters, grad clip norm=1.0; sliding window eval stride=64. Documents rejected approaches including MoE, depth recurrence, int7, multi-token prediction.
key_findings: Combines SmearGate (providing bigram context at ~512 param cost), int6 quantization enabling 3x MLP expansion, and tuned Muon hyperparameters to achieve 1.1618 BPB; provides thorough ablation of rejected approaches including MoE (3.8x slower) and depth recurrence (0.13 BPB quant gap).

title: record: val_bpb=1.1622, NorMuon + int6 STE + SWA + sliding window
technique_details: Int6 STE (straight-through estimator fake quantization during training, quant gap only +0.002 bpb). FP16 tied embedding passthrough. MLP 3x (hidden=1536). NorMuon (row-normalized Newton-Schulz updates from modded-nanogpt). SWA over 7 checkpoints during warmdown. Sliding window eval stride=64 (~0.033 bpb improvement). ~50ms/step, 11917 steps in 600s.
key_findings: Introduces int6 STE (quantization-aware training with straight-through estimator) achieving only +0.002 bpb quant gap, combined with NorMuon optimizer and stride-64 sliding window eval for mean 1.1622 BPB.

title: Record: MLP3x + Int8 Tok Emb + Grouped LZMA + Sliding Window (val_bpb=1.1623)
technique_details: MLP expanded to 3x (MLP_MULT=3), token embedding kept at Int8 while rest of model quantized to Int6, grouped QGv3 serialization with LZMA compression, sliding window eval at seq_len=2048 stride=256. QAT was configured but did not activate before wallclock cap (stopped at step 7534/20000). U-Net-style skip structure with GQA (8 heads, 4 KV heads). FP16 tied embeddings used in intermediate export but not in final submission artifact.
key_findings: Keeping token embedding at Int8 while quantizing remaining tensors to Int6 was the largest quality-preserving compression change; grouped LZMA serialization enabled fitting under the 16MB cap while preserving most of the sliding-window evaluation gain.

title: Add non-record 11L int6 challenger 8xH100 attempt
technique_details: 11-layer Int6 quantization challenger; pre-roundtrip BPB only as RunPod terminated pod during export before post-roundtrip metric could be recorded; multiple vendored helper modules (lowbit_utils, optimizer_variants, validation_utils)
hardware_notes: RunPod pod terminated during export; pre-roundtrip score only, no post-roundtrip artifact validated
key_findings: Reached val_bpb=1.1624 (pre-roundtrip) on 11-layer Int6 architecture but pod was terminated during export, preventing a valid artifact submission; submitted as candid non-record.

title: Record: Int6 MLP3x + MTP + Sliding Window Eval (val_bpb=1.1605)
technique_details: Int6 per-row quantization [-31,31] with zstd-22. 3x MLP expansion (hidden=1536). seq4096 training context. MTP auxiliary head (training-only, excluded from artifact). FP16 tied embedding passthrough. Sliding window eval stride=512. MATRIX_LR=0.02, MUON_MOMENTUM=0.99, WARMDOWN_ITERS=3000. Best single seed 1.1605, mean 1.1625 across 3 seeds.
key_findings: Adds MTP auxiliary head (training-only) and seq4096 training context on top of int6+MLP3x stack, achieving mean 1.1625 BPB across 3 seeds with best seed at 1.1605.

title: Record: 10L Int5-MLP + SmearGate + BigramHash + Late QAT (val_bpb=1.1628)
technique_details: Mixed-precision export: MLP weights int5, attention weights int6, buying artifact budget for 10th layer; SmearGate gated residual smearing; BigramHash 4096-bucket bigram embedding (dim=128); orthogonal init + muP-style output projection scaling; Muon with decoupled WD=0.04; SWA averaging 15 checkpoints every 50 steps starting at 50% of training; late QAT kicking in at 85% wallclock to avoid always-on STE instability; sliding window eval stride=64.
key_findings: Introduces mixed int5/int6 precision (MLP vs attention) to free artifact budget for an additional layer, and late QAT (activating only at 85% wallclock) to avoid training instability; single-seed result does not yet beat current best MLP3x submission (1.1598) and requires multi-seed validation.

title: Record: Pre-Enrichment + Encoder Recurrence + XSA + SmearGate + BigramHash (val_bpb=1.1629)
technique_details: GELU pre-enrichment bottleneck (512→768→512) before residual stream. 2x encoder recurrence on first 5 layers (15 effective layers from 10 physical blocks, zero extra params) with RMS norm stabilization between passes. XSA on last 4 layers (subtracts self-value component from attention output). EMA decay=0.997 replacing SWA. Int6 QAT with STE. BigramHash (4096x64). SmearGate. MLP 3x, seq 2048, sliding window eval stride=64.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM, 600s, seed 1337, 5636 steps at ~106ms/step
key_findings: Achieves 1.1629 sliding BPB through novel combination of GELU pre-enrichment embedding transformation and 2x encoder recurrence (giving 15 effective layers from 10 physical blocks at zero parameter cost), plus XSA on last 4 decoder layers which drove a -0.004 BPB gain in v3→v4. EMA (decay=0.997) reduced quantization gap from 0.020 to 0.004 BPB.

title: Late STE QAT + Int6 MLP3x + SmearGate + BigramHash + OrthoInit + Overtone + SWA + SGD TTT (int6+zstd-22)
technique_details: Late STE QAT activates at ~85% wallclock with 0.5x LR. Full-model SGD TTT (not LoRA) after sliding-window eval on dequantized checkpoint. 9x512 stack, MLP 3x (hidden 1536). zstd level 22 compression to fit under 16MB cap. Muon+AdamW training.
key_findings: Late STE QAT avoids Muon momentum corruption while closing quantization gap. Full-model SGD TTT outperforms LoRA TTT when combined with SmearGate; zstd-22 needed to fit int6 artifact under 16MB.

title: Record/smaller batch sota, val_bpb 1.16314679 (post-quant, int6+zlib, sliding eval)
technique_details: Int6+zlib post-quantization with sliding window eval. No further details available from PR body.
key_findings: Minimal PR body; claims val_bpb=1.16314679 with Int6+zlib and sliding window eval, framed as a record on a smaller-batch configuration.

title: ArjunAutoResearch: MLP 3x + STE int6 QAT + seq4096 + sliding window. val_bpb 1.1632
technique_details: Autonomous agent (ArjunAutoResearch) composed techniques from existing PRs: 3x MLP expansion (hidden=1536, 64-aligned for H100 tile utilization), STE fake-int6 QAT on all CastedLinear weights, TRAIN_SEQ_LEN=4096, sliding window eval stride=64 with compiled forward_logits, fp16 tied embedding passthrough, int6 per-row on MLP+attention, zstd-22, Muon momentum=0.99 with halved LRs, warmdown=3000 steps. Mean BPB 1.16323 ± 0.00042 across seeds 1337/1338/1339.
key_findings: Demonstrates an autonomous LLM agent that reads open PRs, ranks techniques by expected BPB impact, and composes them; the resulting 3-seed mean of 1.16323 BPB with p<<0.001 validates the approach, combining STE int6 QAT, seq_len=4096, and sliding window eval as the highest-impact techniques.

title: Record: SwiGLU + BigramHash + SWA, val_bpb=1.1634 (8xH100 verified)
technique_details: SwiGLU FFN activation discovered via GEPA automated evolutionary search. BigramHash with 4096 buckets, 128 dim. SWA every 50 steps from 50% of training. Int6 + zstd-22 compression. Muon optimizer with WD=0.02. 10 layers, dim=512, 8 heads / 4 KV heads.
key_findings: SwiGLU activation (discovered via automated evolutionary search GEPA) combined with BigramHash and SWA achieves 1.1634 BPB, superseding prior unverified PR #113. Demonstrates automated search as a viable technique discovery method for this competition.

title: Add MergedTop3_v3 clean 8xH100 record-track submission
technique_details: Merged top-stack recipe: XSA last 4 layers, EMA only (no SWA), 3x MLP, SmearGate, BigramHash(2048), mixed int6 + zstd, seq_len 2048, GPTQ-lite clip search, sliding window stride=64, Partial RoPE (ROPE_DIMS=16), layerwise LN scaling, WARMDOWN_ITERS=3500. Strict runtime gates for uninterrupted 8xH100 execution.
hardware_notes: Fresh uninterrupted single-seed run on 8xH100.
key_findings: Clean single-seed rerun of merged leaderboard top stack achieving 1.1642 BPB; primary contribution is robust reproducibility infrastructure including strict preflight checks and one-shot run scripts, not a new SOTA claim.

title: Record: Vocab 4096 + MLP 3x + Sliding Window Eval (mean val_bpb=1.1642, 3 seeds)
technique_details: Custom SentencePiece BPE tokenizer with vocab=4096, MLP expanded to 3x (hidden=1536), Int6 STE fake quantization (quant gap +0.005 BPB), seq_len=4096 training, SWA over 7 checkpoints during warmdown, sliding window eval at stride=256. Muon momentum warmup from 0.92 to 0.99 over 1500 steps.
key_findings: Combining a larger custom BPE vocabulary (4096) with 3x MLP expansion and sliding window eval achieves mean 1.1642 BPB across 3 seeds; the custom tokenizer encodes more bytes per token, directly improving BPB independently of model quality.

title: [Non-record] Meta-Learned TTT + Error-Guided Adaptation Analysis (val_bpb=1.1645)
technique_details: Reptile meta-learning applied to last 20% of training on last 3 blocks' MLPs (1576 meta-steps). Error-guided TTT uses two-pass eval with rank-4 LoRA on top 2% highest-loss windows. 13-layer config outperforms 10-layer (1.1884 vs 1.2090) despite fewer training steps. Artifact is 12.7MB.
key_findings: Reptile meta-learning improves SmearGate models by 0.011 BPB (10x better than naive TTT), partially overcoming SmearGate/TTT redundancy; error-guided TTT concentrating on highest-loss tokens is a negative result, suggesting those tokens are genuinely unpredictable.

title: [Non-record] Meta-Learned TTT + Error-Guided Adaptation Analysis (val_bpb=1.1645)
technique_details: Reptile meta-learning applied to last 20% of training on last 3 blocks' MLPs (1576 meta-steps). Error-guided TTT uses two-pass eval with rank-4 LoRA on top 2% highest-loss windows. 13-layer config. Artifact 12.7MB. Base from PR #198 recipe.
hardware_notes: 8x H100 SXM, 600s training
key_findings: Reptile meta-learning improves SmearGate models by 0.011 BPB over naive TTT; error-guided TTT on highest-loss tokens is a negative result, and per-token analysis reveals the hardest 2.7% of tokens account for ~15% of total loss.

title: Record: Compression-Funded MLP3x (val_bpb=1.1598)
technique_details: Int6 per-row quantization + zlib compression frees artifact budget to expand MLP from 2x to 3x hidden size. FP16 storage for tied embeddings and last two c_k weights. Seq2048 long-context training with tuned LRs (MATRIX_LR=0.02, SCALAR_LR=0.02, TIED_EMBED_LR=0.03). Sliding-window eval at stride=256. Best single seed: 1.15982.
hardware_notes: Seed 1337 had higher ms/step due to CUDA compilation overhead; seeds 1338/1339 reflect steady-state
key_findings: Int6 compression budget is redirected into a 3x MLP expansion rather than a smaller model, yielding a mean BPB of 1.1647 across 3 seeds and beating the prior SOTA (1.1748) by ~0.010 on average and 0.015 at best seed.

title: Int6+zstd MLP1488 + Sliding Window + QAT + Tuned LR (val_bpb=1.1648)
technique_details: Int6 mixed quantization (MLP/Q/V/proj weights) with zstd-22; MLP hidden=1488 (2.91x); sliding window eval stride=64 seq_len=2048; post-training QAT (30s STE); tuned LRs scalar_lr=0.02 tied_embed_lr=0.03 matrix_lr=0.02; FA3 graceful fallback to SDPA; muon_momentum warmup from 0.92 over 1500 steps.
hardware_notes: RunPod 8xH100 SXM
key_findings: Achieves 1.1648 BPB by stacking Int6+zstd, ~3x MLP expansion, sliding window eval, and post-training QAT on top of the baseline, improving -0.0596 BPB vs naive baseline.

title: Non-record: Depth Recurrence in Parameter-Constrained Transformers — What Works, What Doesn't, and Why
technique_details: Key contribution: Noisy QAT for looped transformers — differentiable uniform noise matching int8 quantization error on shared loop-core blocks, reducing recurrence quantization gap from 0.37 to 0.002 bpb. Flat 11L 512d: 1.1648 bpb; Looped 3x3 640d: 1.1894 bpb (+0.025 worse). Loop architecture: Stem→Core(shared blocks × repeats)→Tail with per-repeat embeddings and LoRA adapters. 12 negative results documented including Value Residual (+0.14), factored embeddings, cyclic momentum, full-weight TTT, and others.
hardware_notes: Experiments across 8xH100, 2xH100, and consumer GPUs; ~35 runs total
key_findings: Comprehensive study showing depth recurrence does not improve BPB under competition constraints due to quantization error compounding and step-time overhead; introduces Noisy QAT to collapse the recurrence-quantization gap, and documents 12 negative results with specific numbers.

title: Memory Tokens + Mixed Quantization (val_bpb: 1.1659)
technique_details: Memory Tokens: 64 learnable embedding vectors prepended to every sequence as a global context scratchpad; all real tokens attend to them via causal mask. A/B tested at -0.014 BPB improvement. MTP auxiliary heads (k=2) stripped before export. Eval stride=128.
key_findings: Introduces Memory Tokens — 64 learnable global context vectors prepended to each sequence — with an A/B-confirmed -0.014 BPB improvement. Reproducibility across additional seeds is pending.

title: Memory Tokens + Mixed Quantization (val_bpb: 1.1659)
technique_details: 64 learnable memory token embeddings overwrite first K positions of every input sequence as a global context scratchpad. Mixed int5 MLP / int6 attention quantization with zstd-22. MTP auxiliary heads (k=2, stripped before export). BigramHash(10240), Partial RoPE (16/64 dims), EMA (0.997), Late QAT.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM, 600s
key_findings: Introduces learnable memory tokens that overwrite early sequence positions as a global scratchpad, with A/B test showing -0.014 BPB improvement. Closed to resubmit with a clean diff.

title: Submission: Wider MLP 3x + int6 quant + sliding window eval, val_bpb=1.1659
technique_details: MLP_MULT=3.0 (hidden=1536), int6 per-row quantization on MLP+attention with zstd-22 compression, sliding window eval with stride=256 and batched forward_logits. Two seeds reported: 1.16658 and 1.16591.
key_findings: Three orthogonal improvements over baseline: wider MLP 3x, int6 quantization, and sliding window eval, each individually ablated. Sliding window eval alone contributes ~0.033 BPB improvement at zero artifact cost.

title: Record: Int6 + MLP 3x + STE QAT + NorMuon + sliding window (val_bpb 1.1666)
technique_details: Int6 per-row QAT via STE on MLP and attention weights, 3x MLP expansion (hidden=1536, 21.8M params), NorMuon optimizer with per-neuron row-wise RMS normalization, SWA over 13 checkpoints every 200 steps during warmdown, seq_len=2048, logit softcap=15, zstd-22 compression. Quant penalty reduced to ~0.001 BPB.
hardware_notes: Modal 8xH100 80GB HBM3
key_findings: Combines STE fake-int6 QAT with NorMuon optimizer and SWA, achieving a sliding window BPB of 1.1666 with only ~0.001 BPB quantization penalty; additional seed runs for statistical significance not yet completed.

title: Record: Int6 + MLP 3x + STE QAT + NorMuon + sliding window (val_bpb 1.1666)
technique_details: Int6 per-row on MLP and attention weights with zstd-22; MLP hidden=1536 (3x); STE fake-int6 QAT throughout training (reduces quant penalty ~0.008→0.001 BPB); NorMuon (per-neuron row-wise RMS normalization after Newton-Schulz); SWA checkpoints every 200 steps during warmdown; logit softcap=15; seq_len=2048, batch=786K, grad_clip=0.3.
hardware_notes: Modal 8xH100 80GB HBM3
key_findings: Combines NorMuon optimizer variant and SWA checkpoint averaging with the Int6+QAT+MLP3x+sliding window stack, achieving 1.1666 BPB; sliding window eval contributes ~0.021 BPB improvement over standard eval.

title: Record: Int6 + Canon ACD (K=3) + Muon WD 0.04 + SWA + Sliding Eval (val_bpb=1.1668)
technique_details: CANON ACD placement (before attention, before MLP, in widened MLP hidden stream; excludes B=QKV concat). CANON_KERNEL=3, residual mode. Mixed int6 (mlp+attn) + int8 (other). Muon WD=0.04, Adam WD=0.04. SWA every 200 steps, 8 checkpoints averaged. Bigram hash (vocab=2048, dim=128) + SmearGate. 9L/512d/MLP3x/GQA(8h/4kv).
key_findings: Canon ACD layers (kernel=3) combined with int6 quantization and SWA achieves 1.1668 BPB on a 9-layer model. Demonstrates CANON layer placement tradeoffs: ACD avoids expensive B (QKV) placement while retaining most benefit.

title: Record: Int6 QAT + SmearGate + Muon WD (val_bpb=1.1669)
technique_details: Int6 STE fake quantization (per-row symmetric scaling) stored in int8 containers + zstd-22 (14.77MB from 21.8M params). SmearGate zero-initialized learned gate blending current + previous token embedding (~513 params, very low LR). Decoupled Muon WD=0.01. Sliding window eval stride=64, batch=32. FP16 tied embedding passthrough to avoid compounding int6 errors.
key_findings: Introduces SmearGate (cheap bigram context at embedding layer) combined with Int6 QAT and FP16 tied embeddings to achieve 1.1669 BPB, beating prior SOTA (1.1748) by 0.0079. Earlier version of the approach later extended to 11 layers in PR #192.

title: Record: SwiGLU + MLP 3x + Int6 + LoRA TTT, val_bpb=1.1670 (8xH100)
technique_details: Combines SwiGLU activation, 3x MLP expansion, Int6 quantization, and LoRA-based test-time training
key_findings: Combines SwiGLU, 3x MLP expansion, Int6 quantization, and LoRA TTT to achieve val_bpb=1.1670 on 8xH100; no PR body content was available for further detail.

title: 12L Full-INT4 (MLP + Attn) + BigramHash(4096) — val_bpb 1.1672
technique_details: Group INT4 nibble-packing: 2 values per byte, per-group (gs=64) fp16 scales, symmetric quantization to [-8,7]. Applied to both MLP and attention weights (Q/K/V/O). 10% magnitude pruning before quantization creates zero runs for better zstd compression. U-Net skip connections. 14.4MB final artifact.
key_findings: Full INT4 quantization of both MLP and attention weights achieves 0.5 bytes/param vs 0.75 for INT6, freeing enough budget to run 12 layers instead of 10. The 0.021 bpb quality cost of extending INT4 to attention is offset by the capacity gain from additional layers.

title: Add Nuclear Stack submission: 1.16668 BPB (seed 2884431328)
technique_details: Stacks architectural improvements (Int6+Zstd, 3x MLP, SmearGate, BigramHash, SWA) with test-time training. Claims fixed sliding-window double-counting bug for honest eval. 3-seed mean BPB 1.16759, best seed 1.16516. Artifact 15.8MB. Eventually superseded by PR #254.
key_findings: Combines architectural improvements (Int6, 3x MLP, SmearGate, BigramHash, SWA) with TTT in a single submission, claiming this orthogonal stacking is novel; ultimately superseded by the submitter's own PR #254 with 1.1303 BPB.

title: Non-record: 6-Technique Stack — Catalytic Residuals + Value Residual + Gated Attention + BigramHash(10240) + 12L (val_bpb=1.1690)
technique_details: First submission stacking catalytic residuals, value residual (ResFormer), gated attention, BigramHash(10240 buckets), 12 layers, and 3x MLP together. Also includes OrthoInit, logit softcap 30.0, Muon WD=0.04, SWA (last 20%), Late QAT (threshold 0.25). Individual technique impacts documented from ablations.
key_findings: First submission combining catalytic residuals, value residual, and gated attention together on a 12-layer model; BigramHash scaling to 10240 buckets alone claimed -0.070 BPB vs 2048 buckets. Whole-stack result of 1.1690 is above current SOTA, suggesting not all techniques compose cleanly.

title: [Non-record] LoRA TTT + HParams (val_bpb=1.16973333)
technique_details: Builds on 2026-03-17_LoRA_TTT submission with updated baseline hyperparameters: 8x768 configuration, seq_len=2048, batch=262144 tokens, lower learning rates, lower logit softcap, beta1=0.70.
key_findings: Hyperparameter refinement of an existing LoRA TTT recipe, updating to an 8x768 config with lower LRs and adjusted optimizer betas, achieving val_bpb=1.1697 as a non-record baseline comparison.

title: submission: Int6 MLP3x + QAT + SlidingWindow (val_bpb: 1.1702)
technique_details: Int6 per-row quantization with zstd level-22 compression, MLP_MULT=3 (3x expansion), QAT weight-snapping starting at 70% of training, Muon optimizer with momentum=0.99 and warmup (1500 steps from 0.92), extended warmdown of 3000 iters, sliding window eval with stride=64. Requires zstandard pip package.
key_findings: Stacks int6+zstd22, 3x MLP expansion, QAT, tuned Muon with momentum warmup, extended warmdown, and sliding window eval to achieve 1.1702 BPB — combining multiple complementary techniques for a significant improvement over baseline.

title: non-record: int6 3xMLP + cosine warmdown (1.1704 bpb)
technique_details: 3x MLP expansion enabled by int6 quantization space savings, cosine warmdown schedule, orthogonal init. Built on top of merged SOTA. 7630 steps in 10min wallclock. 13.5MB artifact.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM
key_findings: Single-seed non-record submission combining int6 quantization with 3x MLP expansion and cosine warmdown, achieving 1.1704 BPB at 13.5MB artifact size. Duplicate of PR #246 (resubmitted).

title: non-record: int6 3xMLP + cosine warmdown (1.1704 bpb)
technique_details: 3x MLP expansion enabled by int6 quantization space savings, cosine warmdown schedule, orthogonal init. Built on top of merged SOTA. 7630 steps in 10min wallclock. 13.5MB artifact.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM
key_findings: Single-seed non-record submission (closed, superseded by PR #249) combining int6 quantization with 3x MLP expansion and cosine warmdown, achieving 1.1704 BPB. Author notes willingness to run more seeds if needed.

title: Record: Int6 3xMLP + Cosine Warmdown (val_bpb=1.1704)
technique_details: Cosine warmdown replaces linear warmdown with 0.5*(1+cos(pi*progress)), keeping LR higher during early warmdown then decaying faster near end. Built on 10L+Muon WD+Overtone Init+Sliding Window+FP16 Embed base. Int6 STE quantization with 3x MLP width (24.1M params), RoPE base 50K, 7630 steps in 603s, artifact 13.5MB.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM (standard)
key_findings: Applies cosine warmdown scheduling (standard in LLaMA/GPT-3 but novel to this competition) on top of existing SOTA stack, achieving 1.1704 BPB vs merged SOTA of 1.1748. Reproducibility and additional seeds not yet confirmed.

title: SubSixteen v2: Int6 QAT + MLP 3x + SWA + Sliding Window (val_bpb 1.1708)
technique_details: STE fake-int6 QAT with weights quantized to [-31,31] during forward pass. MLP 3x expansion (hidden=1536). SWA with 16 checkpoints every 200 steps during warmdown. zstd-22 compression. 9L/512d with GQA (8h/4kv). Overtone SVD init, NTK-aware RoPE, logit soft-capping (tanh, cap=30). Muon with momentum warmup 0.92→0.99.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM confirmed
key_findings: Int6 QAT enables MLP 3x expansion within the 16MB budget by saving ~4MB vs int8, achieving 1.1708 BPB with only +0.002 BPB quantization penalty. SWA over 16 checkpoints during warmdown provides further regularization.

title: [Submission] Warmdown Scheduling - 1.2430 BPB on 8×H100 SXM
technique_details: Initial submission focused on WARMDOWN_ITERS=3000 to ensure cosine warmdown fires within wallclock cap. Updated version adds InfiniBand fix (NCCL_IB_DISABLE=0), 10 layers, spectral embedding init, phase-transition resid_mix, Muon weight decay 0.02, FP16 tied embedding export, sliding window eval stride=64, WARMDOWN_ITERS=3200.
key_findings: Initially identified that default WARMDOWN_ITERS=1200 never fires under wallclock cap; maintainer noted it doesn't exceed current SOTA. Updated to 1.1708 BPB with additional techniques but PR was closed.

title: Non-record: PrismLM v3 — DiffTransformer V2 + NorMuon + TrigramHash (val_bpb=1.1715)
technique_details: DiffTransformer V2 attention (differential softmax, last 2 layers), NorMuon optimizer (per-neuron row normalization after Newton-Schulz orthogonalization), TrigramHash(2048) + context-aware sigmoid gating of n-gram signal. 11L dim=512, 8/4 GQA heads, MLP 3x ReLU², XSA last 6 layers, Partial RoPE 16/64, LN depth scaling, U-Net skips, logit softcap, int6+zstd-22. No sliding window eval in this run.
key_findings: First competition submission using Differential Attention (Ye et al. ICLR 2025), NorMuon optimizer, and context-aware trigram n-gram gating; scores 1.1715 BPB without sliding window, ~0.029 behind merged SOTA, with gap largely attributable to missing sliding window eval.

title: Add WaveletWeightedWidenet submission directory with README and metadata
technique_details: 12-layer ternary MLP transformer with vector quantization compression achieving ~1 bit/param; WWW (WaveletWeightedWidenet) architecture
key_findings: Novel WWW architecture using ternary MLP weights with VQ compression at ~1 bit/param achieves val_bpb=1.1719 across 12 layers.

title: Non-record: Value Residual (-0.015 BPB) + Gated Attention (-0.003 BPB) on 11L Production Stack
technique_details: Value Residual (ResFormer arXiv:2410.17897): caches layer-0 V vectors, mixes into subsequent layers via learnable scalars (-0.015 BPB, 22 params). Gated Attention (arXiv:2505.06708): per-head sigmoid gate after SDPA, eliminates attention sinks (-0.003 BPB, ~37K params). Techniques stack additively (-0.0172 combined), validated via controlled 9L ablation. Artifact exceeds 16MB (19.4MB) due to single-GPU exploratory run.
hardware_notes: 1xA6000, 9500 steps, 14.5hr — non-standard hardware, artifact 19.4MB exceeds 16MB limit
key_findings: Provides clean ablation evidence for Value Residual (+ResFormer) and Gated Attention as independently beneficial and additively stackable techniques, both subsequently adopted by multiple community submissions including a reported 1.1101 BPB record-tier entry.

title: Add lzma6 submission (1.172 bpb, 10min_16mb)
technique_details: INT8 quantized model compressed with LZMA-6 instead of zstd. 15.3MB artifact, 12196 steps, seed 1337. Bot comment flags that experiment manifest points to wrong trainer (hardcodes zlib compression), making reproduction metadata incorrect.
key_findings: Explores LZMA-6 as an alternative compression scheme to zstd, achieving 1.172 BPB with INT8 quantization; reproduction metadata is broken per bot review as the manifest references a zlib-based trainer script.

title: The Stinky Frost Recipe — 1.1725 BPB
technique_details: FP16 tied embeddings preserves token distinguishability through int6 quant; MLP hidden=1344 sized to keep FP16 embed under 16MB. Early QAT at 25% of training (~6000 steps). BigramHash 4096×128 buckets.
key_findings: Exploratory submission combining FP16 tied embeddings with custom MLP sizing to fit within artifact budget while maintaining quantization quality. Not a record at time of posting.

title: Add non-record EMA and adaptive export exploration
technique_details: Late-stage EMA (beta=0.9998, start at 80% of training) added to the Int6 MLP3x + SmearGate + BigramHash + Muon baseline. Adaptive export-time pruning search over ratios 0.00–0.05 to meet 16MB target. Artifact is 449,881 bytes over 16MB limit, making this non-leaderboard-valid.
hardware_notes: 2xH100 instead of standard 8xH100
key_findings: Explores late-stage EMA weight smoothing and adaptive export-time pruning for budget-aware artifact compression; achieves competitive 1.1725 BPB but fails the 16MB artifact constraint by ~450KB on constrained 2xH100 hardware.

title: Add submission: 10L Slide64 Mid6, val_bpb=1.1732
technique_details: 10-layer model with mixed export policy: only middle blocks 3,4,5,6 at int6, fp16 tied-embedding export, sliding window eval at 1024/64, stronger Muon crossover schedule, TIED_EMBED_LR reduced from 0.10 to 0.08 vs prior submission yielding measurable improvement.
key_findings: Marginal improvement over prior mid6 submission (1.17334 -> 1.17319) by reducing TIED_EMBED_LR from 0.10 to 0.08; also slightly shrinks compressed artifact size.

title: Add TTT (Test-Time Training) submission: 1.1767 BPB
technique_details: Full-model SGD adaptation during eval phase (epochs=1, causal masking preserved). Claimed 3.0% BPB improvement over static inference with zero architecture changes. Contested by reviewers who argued that adapting model weights on validation data before scoring constitutes information leakage even with causal masking, since the model sees tokens at t>x before being scored at t=x.
key_findings: First TTT submission in the competition; sparked significant debate about whether updating model weights on validation data before scoring constitutes illegitimate look-ahead, even when causal attention masks are maintained during adaptation.

title: Non-record: 10L FP16-Embed + Warmdown20k
technique_details: Built on the merged SlidingWindow_FP16Emb_10L_MuonWD_OvertoneInit baseline; sole change is increasing WARMDOWN_ITERS from 2500 to 20000.
key_findings: Extending warmdown from 2500 to 20000 iterations on the 10L FP16-embed baseline yields post-roundtrip BPB of 1.1739, a modest improvement, with artifact size 14.1MB well under the 16MB limit.

title: Record: Sliding Window + FP16 Embed + 10L + Muon WD + Overtone Init (val_bpb=1.1748)
technique_details: Sliding window eval stride=64 seq_len=1024 (960+ context per token); FP16 tied embedding export skipping int8 for tok_emb; 10 layers enabled by Muon weight decay shrinking artifact size; Muon decoupled weight decay 0.02 via p.mul_(1 - wd * lr); overtone spectral embedding init (SVD power-law spectrum); phase-transition residual mixing (sigmoid-scheduled resid_mix). Mean 1.1748 across 3 seeds, artifact ~14.7MB.
key_findings: Clean 6-technique stack achieving mean 1.1748 BPB across 3 seeds with p<0.001; notably the Muon weight decay serves dual purpose of improving generalization and compressing the artifact enough to accommodate an extra transformer layer, demonstrating a useful interaction between optimizer regularization and model size constraints.

title: Add 10min/16MB record: skinny RLM seq2048 (int8+zlib val_bpb 1.1750)
technique_details: Looped RLM with prefix + 2× weight-tied blocks × 3 iterations + suffix; sequence length 2048; Muon with MATRIX_LR=0.02/SCALAR_LR=0.02/MUON_WD=0.04; EMBED_BITS=8 STE quantization; LOSS_POS_WARMUP=256; ReLU² MLP 3×; tied embeddings; int8+zlib compression
key_findings: Recursive language model with weight-tied blocks iterated 3 times using int8 quantization and zlib compression achieves 1.1750 BPB; demonstrates skinny-width recursive architecture as a viable approach within the 16MB constraint.

title: Non-record subimission: RecurrentTiedDepth_8x2_FiLM records
technique_details: Recurrent tied-depth architecture with 8x2 configuration and FiLM conditioning. Single-seed exploratory result at 13.24MB.
key_findings: Exploratory recurrent tied-depth 8x2 architecture with FiLM conditioning achieves 1.1752 BPB at 13.24MB, submitted as a non-record result.

title: Record: Int5/Int6+Zstd+MLP3x — mean val_bpb=1.1752 (10L, seq4096, sliding window)
technique_details: Int5 for MLP matrices freeing ~1.5MB to enable 3x MLP expansion (hidden=1536); Int6 for attention matrices; Zstd compression; seq4096 training with sliding window eval stride=64; 10L architecture on Modal 8xH100
hardware_notes: Modal 8xH100 SXM
key_findings: Clean 3-seed baseline combining Int5 MLP quantization, Int6 attention quantization, and 3x MLP expansion with sliding window eval, achieving 1.1752 mean BPB — serves as a well-documented reproducible reference point.

title: Int5/Int6+Zstd+MLP3x: mean val_bpb=1.1752 (10L, seq4096, sliding window)
technique_details: Identical to PR #547; closed and resubmitted to remove accidentally included previous submission files. Int5 MLP / Int6 attn / Zstd / 3x MLP expansion / seq4096 + sliding window eval stride=64
hardware_notes: Modal 8xH100 SXM
key_findings: Duplicate of PR #547, closed by author due to accidental inclusion of prior submission files; same 1.1752 mean BPB result across 3 seeds.

title: Record: SP4096 int6+zstd 10L496 overtone+phase sliding (val_bpb=1.1753)
technique_details: SP4096 tokenizer; QUANT_LEVELS=31 (int6-range in int8 container) for improved zstd compressibility; zstd level 22; FP16 tied embeddings passthrough; sliding window eval (stride=64, 960+ context tokens); overtone spectral init; phase-transition residual mixing. Architecture: 10L × 496d × MLP 2×, 8 heads / 4 KV heads.
key_findings: Achieves val_bpb=1.1753 with a 10-layer 496d model using SP4096 tokenizer and int6-range quantization (31 levels in int8 container) to maximize zstd-22 compression. Combines overtone spectral init and phase-transition residual mixing as novel initialization techniques.

title: Sliding Window + Long-Context Training: val_bpb=1.1764
technique_details: Train at seq_len=2048 with Muon momentum=0.99, grad_clip=0.3, eval with overlapping sliding windows (stride=512, context>=1536). QAT warmdown reduces post-quant penalty 3x. Batch=786K tokens optimal for 2048-length training.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM (RunPod, ~47ms/step)
key_findings: Training at 2048 tokens with sliding window eval matches 4096-token training BPB while getting more optimization steps in budget. Gradient clipping at 0.3 identified as sweet spot for long-sequence training stability.

title: Add seq4096 sliding-window fp16 tok coarsen record
technique_details: seq_len=4096, TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS=393216, tuned Muon schedule. Export keeps tok_emb.weight in fp16 and coarsens only blocks.5. Eval with EVAL_STRIDE=64, SW_EVAL_BATCH=32. Canonical size 15.94MB. Seeds 1338 and 1339 provided as reruns.
key_findings: Sliding window evaluation with seq_len=4096 and selective fp16 token embedding export achieves 1.17676 BPB; coarsening only a single middle layer (blocks.5) used to fit within 16MB while preserving model quality.

title: Non-record: BitNet b1.58 - 68M ternary params, val_bpb=1.1770, systematic analysis of ternary limitations
technique_details: 68M ternary {-1,0,1} parameters packed at 1.6 bits/param via base-3 encoding into 15.88MB. Standard techniques (XSA, SmearGate, BigramHash, OrthoInit, WD, EMA/SWA, TTT) found to break or not help ternary models. XSA and weight decay cause training plateaus at val_loss 2.4. Higher LR (0.04), no regularization, and longer warmdown preferred.
key_findings: Systematic analysis showing the standard competition technique stack is incompatible with ternary quantization, with XSA and weight decay causing complete training plateaus. Improves prior ternary record from 1.2029 to 1.1770 BPB using a fundamentally different optimization regime.

title: DenseContextQuantTrim 8xH100: 1.1779 val_bpb
technique_details: Dense baseline-family trainer with clip-search PTQ, hybrid fp16/int8 token embedding export, sliding window validation at VAL_CONTEXT_LEN=2048 stride=512, zlib compression. No int6, QAT, or TTT.
key_findings: Clean dense baseline submission using int8+zlib quantization with sliding window eval, achieving 1.17787 post-quant BPB. Pre-quant BPB was 1.17469, indicating a small quantization gap.

title: Add ContextFuse-2048 submission
technique_details: Submission prepared with OpenAI Codex assistance; uses Modal as GPU provider. No novel techniques described beyond the baseline.
hardware_notes: Modal GPU provider (not standard 8xH100 lab setup)
key_findings: A valid track_10min_16mb submission explicitly not claiming SOTA, scoring 1.17792945 BPB. Notable mainly as a community entry prepared with Codex assistance.

title: Record: 10L Seq2048 TTT LoRA WarmdownQuant (val_bpb=1.1787)
technique_details: TTT with batched LoRA adapters (rank 8) on Q, V projections and LM head. Always-decaying warmdown (WD=15000) for tighter weights reducing int8 quantization penalty. Overtone spectral embedding init and phase-transition residual mixing. Sequence length 2048, Muon momentum 0.98, grad clip norm 1.0.
key_findings: Combines extended warmdown scheduling with TTT LoRA adapters to reduce quantization penalty, achieving 1.1787 BPB at only 13,282 of 200,000 steps; submission is awaiting maintainer review.

title: MetaStack v3: 1.1792 sliding bpb, 10L BigramHash SmearGate OrthoInit SWA
technique_details: Mixed int5 (MLP) / int6 (attn) quantization, 2% magnitude pruning, SWA over 30 checkpoints, Muon WD=0.04, BigramHash embeddings, SmearGate, orthogonal init, sliding window eval stride=64 seq_len=1024, zstd22 compression. Includes ProteinLite search harness and Vast.ai deploy pipeline.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM, 600s training, seed 1337
key_findings: MetaStack v3 achieves 1.1792 sliding BPB combining mixed int5/int6 QAT, SmearGate, BigramHash, OrthoInit, and SWA with an aggressive zstd22 compression scheme fitting within 12.1MB. Submission also bundles a full search harness and remote deployment pipeline.

title: [track_10min_16mb] utkuaydiin 10L Muon — 15.46 MB
technique_details: 10L, d=448, BigramHash(8192), SmearGate, Muon optimizer, SWA, 5.5% global magnitude pruning, mixed Int5/Int6 PTQ, zstd-22 compression to 15.46 MB.
key_findings: Submission claims 1.18 val_bpb but reviewer bot flagged that train_gpt.py is a placeholder, submission.json schema is non-compliant, and no training logs are included, making validation impossible. Likely not a genuine competitive result.

title: Non-record: 11L Partial RoPE + XSA4 + VE128 + Tight SWA + GPTQ-lite (val_bpb=1.1804)
technique_details: MLP hidden=1408 (narrower than standard 1536) enables 33% more training steps (4071 vs 3061) within time budget while staying under 16MB. Mixed int6 (layers 1-9) + int8 (layers 0,10). Shared VE128 on layers 9-10. Tight SWA (scale<0.2). Late QAT. GPTQ-lite per-tensor clip search. Resubmit of PR #534 with clean branch.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM (standard)
key_findings: Demonstrates that narrowing MLP from 1536 to 1408 hidden units reduces artifact size enough to fit within 16MB while enabling ~1000 more training steps, improving BPB from 1.1958 to 1.1804 despite lower per-step capacity.

title: Non-record: 11L Partial RoPE + XSA4 + VE128 + Tight SWA + GPTQ-lite (val_bpb=1.1804)
technique_details: Identical to PR #543 — closed due to unrelated files in branch. MLP hidden=1408 for more training steps within budget. Mixed int6/int8, GPTQ-lite clip search, Tight SWA, Late QAT, VE128 on layers 9-10.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM (standard)
key_findings: Duplicate of PR #543, closed by author because the branch contained unrelated files; identical results and methodology to the resubmission.

title: Submission: Atris Labs v8 (audited seed42, clean branch)
technique_details: Clean replacement for PR #515 with a submission-only branch. Audited single seed (seed=42) run: val_bpb=1.18069496, val_loss=1.99355398, 14.46 MB artifact, 6428 steps in 600s. Same architecture as #515.
key_findings: Administrative replacement for PR #515 providing a clean submission branch with a verified train.log for seed=42; resolves commit-history and co-author concerns from the earlier PR.

title: Non-record: Int6 QAT + MLP1472 + SlidingWindow + TTT (val_bpb=1.1807)
technique_details: 9L 512d GPT, Int6 STE QAT (quant gap 0.0017), MLP hidden=1472, aggressive warmdown, FP16 tied embeddings, sliding window eval stride=64. Full-weight TTT: 3 epochs SGD on validation data, freeze first 2 blocks, yields -0.018 BPB improvement (1.1991 → 1.1807).
key_findings: Full-weight test-time training with SGD (3 epochs, freezing first 2 blocks) yields a meaningful -0.018 BPB improvement on a 9L Int6 QAT model, demonstrating TTT effectiveness even outside the record-attempt tier.

title: Record: Atris Labs — 3-seed mean val_bpb=1.1807, 10L MLP3x Int5/Int6 BigramHash SmearGate SWA
technique_details: 10L transformer, MLP 3x (1536 hidden), d=512, GQA 8/4 heads. BigramHash(10240) with XOR hash → 128-dim embedding. SmearGate per-dim learned gate. Int5 MLP + Int6 attention per-row scale. FP16 tied embeddings. 3% magnitude pruning. SWA over 24 warmdown checkpoints. Superseded by PR #671.
key_findings: Atris Labs submission achieving 1.1807 mean BPB with a combination of Int5/Int6 mixed quantization, BigramHash, and SmearGate; superseded by PR #671 which adds a clean branch with a real train.log.

title: Add 3xMLP + Mixed Quant + Blockade/Sigma submission (val_bpb: 1.1812)
technique_details: 3x MLP expansion with blockade attention diversity (attention head diversity mechanism) and uncertainty-gated sigma residuals. Mixed quantization: INT8 for attention, INT6 for MLP, allowing wider MLP to fit under 16MB at 15.37MB. 7441 steps in 10 min on 8xH100 PCIe.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 PCIe (vs standard SXM)
key_findings: Uses mixed INT8/INT6 quantization to fit a 3x MLP-expanded model under the 16MB budget, pairing it with novel blockade attention diversity and uncertainty-gated sigma residuals, achieving val_bpb 1.1812.

title: Non-record: Soft MoE Exploration — Dense Gating Fixes Sparse Router Collapse Under 16MB (WIP, val_bpb=1.1826)
technique_details: Systematic MoE exploration: sparse MoE fails due to router collapse (98%/2% split) and torch.compile incompatibility (2309ms/step). Soft MoE with learned soft gating over all experts fixes collapse, enables torch.compile (636ms/step), and achieves 1.1826 bpb vs 1.2244 baseline. 11L run is 17.3MB (over 16MB limit); 10L version pending. No 3-seed validation yet.
hardware_notes: Full 8xH100 run for final result; artifact is 17.3MB exceeding 16MB limit
key_findings: Demonstrates that standard sparse MoE fails under 16MB constraints due to router collapse and compile incompatibility, while a dense Soft MoE variant resolves these issues and achieves 1.1826 BPB, though the 11L version exceeds the artifact size limit.

title: [Non-Record] Hymba: Hybrid Attention + Mamba SSM (val_bpb 1.1828)
technique_details: 7-layer hybrid model running GQA (8q/4kv heads) and Mamba SSM in parallel per block, outputs merged via learned sigmoid-weighted average. Fused input projection for K, V, and Mamba inputs. LR=0.02 with aggressive cosine warmdown (3000 steps) for quantization-friendly weights. MLP 4x expansion. ~85ms/step on 8xH100 (~7000 steps in 10 min).
key_findings: First competitive hybrid attention+Mamba SSM architecture in the competition, achieving val_bpb ~1.1828 (3 seeds); shallower depth (7L vs baseline 9-11L) exploits the SSM branch's long-range capacity to enable more training steps within the time budget.

title: 11L + XSA + VRL + SWA + seq4096 + cross-doc TTT - val_bpb 1.1839
technique_details: 11-layer dim=512 model with seq_len=4096 training context (cited as largest contributor), XSA on deepest 4 layers, Value Residual Learning (per-layer learnable residual from layer-0 value vectors), SmearGate at embedding, SWA over 24 checkpoints from last 40% of warmdown, cross-doc rank-8 LoRA TTT per document at eval, warmdown-QAT. Post-quant int8+zlib: 1.2192; with TTT: 1.1839. Model size 15.35MB.
key_findings: Combines 4096-token context, XSA, Value Residual Learning, and per-document rank-8 LoRA TTT to reach 1.1839 BPB, with TTT providing a substantial 0.035 BPB improvement over the post-quant baseline.

title: Non-record: Linearized Neural Memory + TTT (val_bpb=1.1844)
technique_details: Linearized Titans-style neural memory where cumulative gradient update reduces to causal linear attention via cumsum, enabling fullgraph=True compilation. Combined with LoRA TTT, overtone init, FP16 embedding bypass, Int6 quantization, and Muon with weight decay. ~8K params/layer overhead. 14.5MB artifact.
key_findings: Linearized neural memory (Titans-style) compiles with fullgraph=True by reducing to causal linear attention via cumsum, adding only ~8K params/layer. TTT variant scores 1.1844 vs 1.2163 for standard, demonstrating meaningful gain from the memory mechanism.

title: Record: Pre-Enrichment + Encoder Recurrence (val_bpb=1.1855)
technique_details: GELU pre-enrichment via two linear projections before transformer blocks; 2x encoder recurrence with RMS norm between passes yielding 15 effective layers from 10 physical blocks. Overtone embedding init, decoupled Muon weight decay, batched sliding window eval stride=64, MATRIX_LR=0.06, TIED_EMBED_LR=0.1. 512d model, 8004 steps in 600s.
key_findings: Novel encoder recurrence doubles effective depth from 10 to 15 layers at no parameter cost, with ablations showing depth advantage outweighs 50% more training steps. GELU pre-enrichment provides additional input conditioning before transformer blocks.

title: Record: 8192 Vocab Size, NorMuon, Selective Quantization; 1.186 val_bpb
technique_details: Vocab size increased to 8192 with custom sp8192 BPE tokenizer. Selective quantization: weights at int6, embeddings at int8. NorMuon replaces Muon optimizer. Seq len 4096, warmdown 3000 steps.
hardware_notes: Hyperbolic Labs 8xH100 SXM5
key_findings: Increases vocab size from 1024 to 8192 at the cost of one transformer layer, combining NorMuon optimizer with selective precision quantization (int6 weights, int8 embeddings). Labeled 'does not beat SOTA' by maintainers.

title: Add record: Optimizer Tuning + Sliding Window Eval (val_bpb=1.1864)
technique_details: No architectural changes from baseline 9-layer 512dim ReLU² model. Optimizer tuning: warmdown=10000, muon_backend_steps=10, grad_clip=1.0, beta2=0.99, scalar_lr=0.02. Seq2048 training. Sliding window evaluation stride=64. Int8+zlib artifact. Scores reported across 3 seeds (1337/1338/1339) with mean val_bpb 1.18731.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM, standard setup
key_findings: Demonstrates that optimizer hyperparameter tuning alone (no architecture changes) on the baseline 9-layer model achieves statistically significant improvement over prior SOTA (t=8.57, p<0.01), with mean 1.18731 BPB across 3 seeds. Highlights the importance of optimizer configuration as a first-order lever.

title: Record: sliding eval, FP16 tied embeddings, 10 layers, Muon WD 0.02, overtone init, and phase-transition residual mixing. (val_bpb 1.1876)
technique_details: Sliding window eval with stride=64; FP16 tied embedding export bypassing int8 quantization; 10 transformer layers; Muon weight decay 0.02; overtone spectral embedding init with power=0.5 (SVD power-law spectrum shaping); phase-transition residual-mix initialization (sigmoid-scheduled resid_mix). Single seed run; pre-quant BPB was 1.2202, post-quant 1.1876 showing large quantization benefit.
key_findings: Single-seed submission achieving 1.1876 BPB by stacking 6 techniques; the large gap between pre-quant (1.2202) and post-quant (1.1876) BPB suggests the FP16 embedding + int8/zlib pipeline provides substantial compression-time gains, though the single-seed result limits statistical confidence.

title: Add seq4096 fp16 tok coarsen record
technique_details: Seq len 4096, batch tokens 393216, tuned Muon schedule, tok_emb.weight kept in fp16, coarsening applied only to blocks.5. Two rerun seeds (1338, 1339) included for reference alongside canonical run.
key_findings: Combines seq_len=4096 with selective fp16 token embeddings and layer-specific weight coarsening, achieving 1.18838751 BPB. Labeled 'does not beat SOTA' despite providing multi-seed reproducibility data.

title: 1.1888 BPB via SP-4096 compression + stride-64 sliding window
technique_details: SP-4096 tokenizer (0.30 tokens/byte vs 0.41 baseline, 26.6% better compression ratio). Stride-64 sliding window eval giving ~960 tokens of context per token. 8-layer 512-dim GQA encoder-decoder with skip connections. Closed as superseded by PR #251 (val_bpb=1.1596).
key_findings: Uses SP-4096 tokenizer and stride-64 sliding window eval as two independently stacking improvements, achieving 1.1888 BPB. Superseded by a later submission from the same author.

title: Non-record: staging profile (LAWA + slide eval) on 8xH100 (val_bpb=1.18926428)
technique_details: STAGING_PROFILE=1 enables LAWA (Late Weight Averaging) and sliding-window eval at EVAL_STRIDE=512. Also reports TTT LoRA score of 1.1863. Stopped early at step 11436/20000 due to wallclock cap. Artifact 15.29MB (int8+zlib).
key_findings: Staging profile combining LAWA and stride-512 sliding-window eval reaches 1.1892 BPB (int8+zlib) and 1.1863 with TTT LoRA, but does not beat current SOTA of 1.1748. Run stopped early due to wallclock limit.

title: Non-Record: DG Attention, Differential-Gated Attention with Depth-Scheduled Novelty Encoding: (val_bpb=1.1898)
technique_details: Novel DG (Designator/Gradient) attention: asymmetric D_q/D_k projections for matching via SDPA, with value payload as hybrid of raw content and causal differential signal (beta*raw + (1-beta)*diff); beta is learned per layer but collapses in deep layers toward differential mode; depth-scheduled v3 hardcodes the discovered beta pattern to prevent gate collapse. Artifact exceeds 16MB limit by ~638KB. 201ms/step vs ~80ms for standard attention, yielding ~3000 steps in budget vs ~7000.
hardware_notes: Also tested on RTX 5080 laptop for convergence comparison; official run on H100 at 201ms/step
key_findings: DG Attention transmits token value deltas relative to a causal running baseline rather than raw content in deep layers, inspired by video compression frame-differencing; empirical analysis of learned beta values revealed spontaneous depth-dependent specialization (deep layers 99% differential), motivating a hardcoded depth schedule in v3 to prevent gate collapse seen with larger batches.

title: Submission: 10L + Sliding Window eval (mean val_bpb=1.1899)
technique_details: 10-layer GPT trained with train_seq_len=4096 (vs typical 1024) to expose model to longer-range patterns during training; evaluated with sliding window stride=64; Muon optimizer with matrix_lr=0.04 and momentum=0.95; warmdown_iters=3600.
key_findings: Demonstrates that training on longer sequences (4096 tokens) combined with sliding window eval yields consistent results across 3 seeds (std=0.0008); the long-sequence training is the primary distinguishing technique but does not achieve competitive BPB vs current SOTA.

title: Record: CLASE-Quant adaptive layer quantization (val_bpb=1.1914)
technique_details: Non-uniform per-layer quantization: boundary layers (0,1,N-2,N-1) use int8, middle layers use int6, tied embeddings use fp16; ramping weight decay from 0.02 to 0.08 during warmdown; train seq len 2048 with stride-64 sliding window eval; LR 0.03, momentum 0.97
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM on RunPod Secure Cloud
key_findings: Introduces CLASE-inspired adaptive per-layer quantization that saves ~15% model size vs uniform int8 by applying coarser quantization to middle layers, achieving 1.1914 mean BPB across 3 seeds at ~11.5 MB artifact size.

title: Submission/2026 03 22 Sliding Window + WARMDOWN + AttnRes + PhiSimple (mean 1.1925 BPB)
technique_details: WARMDOWN_ITERS=20000 always-decaying LR schedule for better int8 quantization. Sliding window eval with EVAL_STRIDE=64. Block AttnRes: learned attention over previous block outputs at block boundaries. PhiAlpha Simple: per-layer learnable scale on relu-squared activation. TTT LoRA applied at eval.
key_findings: First submission by a high school student combining warmdown scheduling, sliding window eval, block attention residuals, and per-layer activation scaling with TTT LoRA, achieving mean 1.1925 BPB across 3 seeds.

title: Record: Quant Quality: val_bpb=1.1925
technique_details: Int8 quantization with aggressive clipping (99.99995 percentile) and float32 per-row scales.
key_findings: Claims record with val_bpb=1.1925 using int8 quantization with high-percentile clipping and float32 per-row scales. Minimal detail provided in PR body.

title: Record: Sliding Window Eval (stride=64), val_bpb=1.1925
technique_details: Sliding window evaluation with stride=64 on unmodified baseline 9x512 SP-1024 architecture; each token scored with 960+ tokens of context; training identical to baseline; eval takes 70s on 8xH100. Note: a bug was found where the final partial window was silently dropped (fixed in PR #124).
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM (RunPod)
key_findings: Pure eval-time improvement of 0.032 BPB over baseline by using sliding window evaluation with stride=64, requiring no changes to training or the model artifact. A bug causing the final partial window to be dropped was later identified and fixed.

title: Add non-record BigramHash4096 + MLP992 + LR0.08 + Slide64 submission
technique_details: MLP hidden=992, matrix_lr=0.08, BigramHash(4096 buckets, dim=64), sliding window eval stride=64, fp16 tied embedding export, int8+zlib artifact. Exceeded 16MB cap (16,120,324 bytes) so submitted as non-record.
key_findings: A strong run combining BigramHash4096 and MLP992 that narrowly exceeds the 16MB artifact cap at 16,120,324 bytes, disqualifying it from the record track despite a competitive post-quant BPB of 1.1929.

title: Non-record: SWA and doc-isolated eval ablation — two negative findings at stride=64
technique_details: Controlled ablations: SWA with 73 snapshots shows no improvement (+0.0004 BPB) under default warmdown (WD=1200) because model is already in narrow basin; doc-isolated eval at stride=64 hurts by +0.009 BPB because start-of-document tokens lose all context. Crossover point: stride=64 favors context quantity (no doc isolation), stride=256+ favors context quality (doc isolation). SWA bf16 accumulation bug documented (fix: accumulate in fp32, sample every 50 steps).
key_findings: SWA provides no benefit under default warmdown at stride=64, and doc-isolated evaluation actively hurts by +0.009 BPB at stride=64 because removing cross-doc context starves start-of-document tokens; the crossover between these regimes occurs around stride=256.

title: Non-record: BitNet Ternary — 65M params in 15.9MB (1.1932 BPB)
technique_details: Ternary {-1,0,+1} weights at ~1.58 bits/weight via STE with per-row mean-absolute scaling. Full-precision for first 30% of wallclock, ternary STE for remaining 70%. 12L, 768d, 12/6 GQA, MLP 3x (2304 hidden), U-Net skips. Fits 65M params in 15.9MB — 3x more parameters than int6 submissions at similar artifact size.
key_findings: First ternary/BitNet-style submission in this competition: 65M parameters fit in 15.9MB (3x more than typical int6 submissions) with near-zero quantization gap (0.0003 BPB) due to STE training, though BPB of 1.1932 is well above SOTA.

title: Record: 7L MLP3x 4kSeq LR-Tuned (val_bpb=1.1933)
technique_details: 7-layer model with 3x MLP expansion, 4k sequence length, learning rate tuning. Minimal details provided beyond README reference.
key_findings: LR-tuned 7-layer model with 3x MLP expansion and 4k sequence length achieves 1.1933 BPB. Limited technical detail available in the PR body itself.

title: Non-record: 1x RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell 10L Int5-MLP (1.1935 BPB)
technique_details: Ports the merged 2026-03-20_10L_Int5MLP_MuonWD04_SWA50 recipe to a single RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell. Mixed int5/int6 quantization with zstd compression. Pre-quant val_bpb 1.2082, post-quant 1.1935. 14085 steps in 2700s. Artifact size 15,691,796 bytes. Metadata and log labels inconsistently reference int8/zlib vs int6/zstd (noted by reviewer comments).
hardware_notes: Run on a single RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition, not 8xH100. 2700s wallclock (not 10-minute constraint).
key_findings: Demonstrates the 10L Int5-MLP recipe running on a single RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell, achieving competitive post-quantization val_bpb of 1.1935; submission has metadata inconsistencies between int8/zlib labels and actual int6/zstd compression used.

title: Record: 8192 Vocab, Sliding Window Eval, Selective Quantization; 1.194 val_bpb
technique_details: SP-8192 tokenizer (8192 vocab vs baseline 1024), NorMuon optimizer, sliding window evaluation stride=256, selective w6e8 quantization (INT6 weights, INT8 embeddings) + zlib, 8-layer model, TRAIN_SEQ_LEN=4096, WARMDOWN_ITERS=3000. Hardware sourced from Vast.ai.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM via Vast.ai
key_findings: Explores larger 8192-vocab SP tokenizer combined with NorMuon and selective w6e8 quantization, improving over baseline by ~0.031 BPB while reducing artifact size by ~1.2MB; longer seq_len=4096 reduces step count to 5467 due to wallclock cap.

title: [record bpb=1.195] sliding window + LoRA TTT
technique_details: Per-document LoRA TTT at eval time: rank-8 LoRA adapters on lm_head, c_q, c_v in all transformer blocks, trained with Adam (lr=0.01, betas=(0.9, 0.95)) for one gradient step per chunk (chunk_size=256, eval_seq_len=1024). LoRA reset between documents to prevent cross-document leakage. Documents batched (batch_size=64) and sorted by length. Training identical to naive baseline. Sliding window and document isolation contribute more gain than TTT alone.
hardware_notes: train_v0.txt on 2xH100; main runs on 8xH100
key_findings: LoRA TTT at eval enables per-sequence adaptation while batching efficiently; most of the BPB gain comes from document isolation and sliding-window eval rather than TTT itself, with TTT adding only ~0.003 BPB on top.

title: Record:Add TTT-LoRA 512d submission (val_bpb=1.1957)
technique_details: TTT-LoRA with 512d model, 8 heads, 4kv, warmdown-3000. Int8+zlib roundtrip for compression. Self-validated only.
key_findings: Applies LoRA-based test-time training to a 512d model achieving val_bpb=1.1957. Uses int8+zlib for artifact compression with a notable 0.0606 nat gap between raw and roundtrip scores.

title: Record:Add TTT-LoRA 512d submission (val_bpb=1.1957)
technique_details: TTT-LoRA with 512 hidden dim, 8 heads, 4kv heads, warmdown=3000. Best score comes from test-time LoRA adaptation evaluated post-training. Artifact 15,880,385 bytes using int8+zlib compression.
key_findings: Applies test-time training via LoRA adaptation to achieve 1.1957 BPB; the TTT evaluation step occurs after standard training completes within the 10-minute budget, suggesting the LoRA adaptation provides meaningful gains over the static model checkpoint.

title: Add TTT-LoRA 512d submission (val_bpb=1.1957)
technique_details: TTT-LoRA based submission at 512d model dimension. No further details available from PR body.
key_findings: Minimal PR body; claims val_bpb=1.1957 using TTT-LoRA at 512d, but no implementation details or ablations are provided.

title: Sliding Window Eval + Muon6 (val_bpb 1.1973)
technique_details: Sliding window eval with stride=256 using a new forward_logits() method; Muon with 6-step Newton-Schulz orthogonalization (MUON_BACKEND_STEPS=6); extended momentum warmup to 1000 steps; warmdown extended to 1500 iterations. A bot comment flags a potential boundary-token double-counting bug in multi-GPU sliding eval.
key_findings: Combines sliding window evaluation with a more accurate 6-step Muon Newton-Schulz backend, achieving mean 1.1973 BPB across 3 seeds; a bot reviewer identified a potential double-counting bug at shard boundaries in the multi-GPU sliding eval implementation that may affect score validity.

title: Non-record: MUD optimizer — triangular Gram preconditioning (arxiv:2603.17970)
technique_details: Replaces Muon's 5-step Newton-Schulz iteration with MUD's triangular Gram preconditioning (Algorithm 2, arxiv:2603.17970). Only the optimizer is changed; all architecture and quantization settings are identical to thwu1's SOTA (PR #180). torch.linalg.solve_triangular on H100 CUDA is 4.5x slower per step than Muon's GEMM-based Newton-Schulz.
hardware_notes: torch.linalg.solve_triangular is poorly optimized on H100 CUDA vs A100/MI250/GH200 where paper benchmarks were measured; step time 118ms vs ~26ms for Muon
key_findings: MUD optimizer achieves strong per-step convergence but is 4.5x slower than Muon on H100s, resulting in only 5,087 steps vs ~20,000 for Muon in the 10-minute budget and a final BPB of 1.1989 vs 1.1428. Extrapolated convergence suggests ~1.10 BPB if step time matched Muon.

title: Add SmearGate+BigramHash context-repair submission (1.2006 BPB, 15.0MB)
technique_details: 9-layer 512-dim GQA model with SmearGate, BigramHash context features, MLP_MULT=3, int6+zstd export, fp16 embedding passthrough. 12,511 steps in 600s on 8xH100. Developed via AI agent coordination.
key_findings: Non-record 9-layer submission achieving 1.2006 BPB, notable for being developed by a non-ML practitioner (surgeon) through AI agent coordination. Demonstrates accessibility of the competitive stack.

title: Record: SP4096 + Int6 QAT + NorMuon (val_bpb=1.2012)
technique_details: SP4096 tokenizer with 26% better text compression than sp1024; Int6 STE QAT; zstd-22 compression yielding 14.3MB artifact; NorMuon optimizer with tuned learning rates.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM 80GB, 600s training; also tested on RTX 5090 for 16-config comparison
key_findings: Uses a larger SP4096 tokenizer combined with Int6 STE QAT and zstd-22 compression to achieve 1.2012 BPB, beating the baseline (1.2244) by 0.023. This is a duplicate/promoted version of the draft PR #37.

title: Record: SP4096 + Int6 QAT + NorMuon (val_bpb=1.2012)
technique_details: SP4096 tokenizer (26% better compression than sp1024); Int6 STE QAT; zstd-22 compression; NorMuon optimizer. Draft/early version of PR #200; reviewer noted tight size budget and replication concerns.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM 80GB; also tested on RTX 5090
key_findings: Draft predecessor to PR #200 with identical claims. Reviewer flagged that the baseline config leaves little headroom under the 16MB limit, and that the SP4096 tokenizer and dataset should be published for replication.

title: New SOTA attempt (val_bpb=1.2014)
technique_details: Better Muon momentum, improved learning rate, and warmdown schedule tuning. Three seeds: 1.2014, 1.1995, 1.2032.
key_findings: SOTA attempt achieving mean 1.2014 BPB across 3 seeds via tuned Muon momentum, LR, and warmdown schedule; best single seed was 1.1995.

title: Record: 10L Int5-MLP + Mixed Quant + GradClip + Warmdown3k (mean val_bpb=1.20262)
technique_details: Mixed quantization: Int5 for MLP weights, Int6 for attention, FP16 for embeddings. MLP_MULT increased 2→3 (hidden 1024→1536). WARMDOWN_ITERS 1200→3000. GRAD_CLIP_NORM 0.0→0.3. Extra layer funded by Int5 MLP quantization savings.
key_findings: Funds a 10th transformer layer within 16MB by using Int5 quantization for MLP weights, combined with wider MLP (3x) and extended warmdown, improving over the naive baseline from 1.2244 to 1.20262 BPB.

title: Non-record: BitNet b1.58 — 65M ternary params beat 4-hour baseline in 10 minutes (val_bpb=1.2029)
technique_details: BitNet b1.58 ternary {-1,0,1} weight quantization with STE in every forward pass; base-3 packing at 1.6 bits/param (5 trits/byte); FP16 scale simulation during training; 64.5M params in 15.1MB. No sliding window or eval tricks. Near-zero quantization gap (0.002 BPB).
key_findings: Explores BitNet b1.58 ternary quantization to pack 64.5M parameters into 15.1MB, achieving near-lossless quantization roundtrip (0.002 BPB gap); beats 4-hour full-precision baseline in 10 minutes but does not challenge INT6+sliding window SOTA.

title: Non-record: 12L Low-Rank Q + QAT (1xH100, pre-quant 1.2035)
technique_details: Low-Rank Q factorization (r=128) inspired by high condition numbers in Q matrices (~8% faster per step); QAT with STE (int7) reduces quant gap from ~0.016 to ~0.005 BPB; FTLE per-row quantization precision is a clean negative result (uniform int-N strictly better at all bit widths due to entropy/zstd interaction); layer sharing abandoned at 512d scale (costs 0.09 BPB); score is pre-quant on 1xH100.
hardware_notes: 1xH100 only; awaiting 8xH100 compute for full run.
key_findings: Low-Rank Q factorization provides ~8% speed gains enabling a 12th layer; FTLE-guided mixed-precision quantization is definitively worse than uniform per-row quantization because mixing bit widths increases entropy and hurts zstd compression.

title: Record: SEQ_LEN=4096 training
technique_details: TRAIN_SEQ_LEN=4096 with reduced batch tokens (393216) for more optimizer steps; Muon momentum=0.99, extended warmup 1500 steps, LR=0.02; sliding window eval (EVAL_STRIDE=64); FP16 tied embeddings; overtone init; phase-transition resid_mix; NTK-aware RoPE; optional LOWBIT_LAYERS+LOWBIT_STEP for mixed-bit export enabling 11L configs.
hardware_notes: Run cut short at ~570s/600s; full run pending additional compute grant
key_findings: Explores long-context training (seq_len=4096) combined with current SOTA recipe; partial run reached val_bpb=1.2036 (non-sliding) at step 8000 before hitting compute limit. Full run with sliding eval projected at ~1.168.

title: PROTEUS v4 — non-record submission (val_bpb: 1.2037)
technique_details: Mixed INT5 MLP / INT6 attention quantization. SmearGate, BigramHash, OrthoInit from prior PRs. EMA, 3% pruning, RoPE base 50K, zstd-22. Built using the PROTEUS platform by LightSpeedUp.
hardware_notes: Modal 8×H100
key_findings: Non-record submission from the PROTEUS platform, building on established competition techniques. Earlier PR #95 documented negative results on INT4, depth recurrence, and EMA overhead.

title: Non-record: FP16 embed + WD20k + seq2048 + doc-isolated sliding window (val_bpb=1.2045)
technique_details: FP16 tied embedding export, WARMDOWN_ITERS=20000, seq_len=2048 training, sliding window eval stride=64 with doc-isolated scoring; constrained by 70ms/step vs standard 44ms yielding only 8,528 steps
hardware_notes: RunPod node at 70ms/step vs typical 44ms; estimated ~1.185 BPB on standard hardware
key_findings: Composition of community techniques (FP16 embeddings, aggressive warmdown, seq2048, doc-isolated sliding window) beats naive baseline by 0.020 BPB, but hardware bottleneck limited steps to 8,528; superseded by PR #212.

title: Non-record: QAT ablation — int8 QAT overhead exceeds quantization gap recovery
technique_details: Per-row int8 QAT using straight-through estimator matching quantize_float_tensor exactly (INT8_CLIP_Q=0.9999984 percentile); torch.quantile adds ~20% per-step overhead (64ms->77ms), costing ~2000 training steps; QAT activates at step 6000. Key finding: int8 QAT not worthwhile under 10-min cap; aggressive warmdown (WD=20000) achieves similar quant gap reduction at zero cost. torch.compile graph priming pitfall documented.
key_findings: Int8 QAT overhead (~20% per step via torch.quantile) costs more training tokens than it recovers from the quantization gap; aggressive warmdown achieves similar results for free, while int6 QAT likely does pay off given its larger quantization gap.

title: Non-record: Competitive Stack + Phonetic Tokenization Exploration (val_bpb=1.2055, 4xH100)
technique_details: Int6 STE QAT with BigramHash, SmearGate, orthogonal initialization, MLP 3x expansion, sliding window eval (stride=64). IPA phonetic tokenization explored as a negative result. Known compliance issue: 19.6MB with int8 export, needs int6 export.
hardware_notes: 4xH100 SXM (non-standard, half the required 8xH100)
key_findings: Phonetic (IPA) tokenization provides marginal gains in isolation but is subsumed by modern training techniques. Model is non-compliant at 19.6MB due to int8 export; int6 export needed for 16MB limit.

title: SOTA attempt (val_bpb=1.2064)
technique_details: Three seeds reported (1337: 1.20576485, 1338: 1.2061746, 1339: 1.20715923); mean BPB 1.20636623. No architectural details provided in PR body.
key_findings: SOTA attempt reporting mean BPB of 1.2064 across three seeds, acknowledged by maintainer @0hq.

title: Non-record: leader-core valid-eval parity run + 1xH100 proxy screens
technique_details: Validity-safe merge rooted in official 2026-03-19 SlidingWindow_FP16Emb_10L_MuonWD_OvertoneInit training core. Explores stronger int8 export search and temperature-only post-quant search. 1xH100 proxy screens show warmdown800+matrixlr006 as strongest proxy signal (1.4874 BPB). step_avg 49.31ms on 8xH100.
hardware_notes: Main run on 8xH100 (RunPod); proxy screens on 1xH100
key_findings: Non-record ablation run documenting that warmdown length and matrix learning rate are the strongest proxy signals on 1xH100 screens, achieving 1.2064 BPB on the standard leader-core stack with validity-safe eval.

title: Record: 1.2073 bpb • 11L gold6 • 8xH100
technique_details: 11-layer GPT with d=512, 8 attention heads / 4 KV heads (GQA), tied embeddings plus bigram hash embedding, Muon optimizer with tuned momentum warmup, EMA on final weights, mixed int6 quantization with per-row scales and zstd-22 compression; artifact ~15.5MB.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 (standard)
key_findings: Straightforward 11-layer submission combining GQA, BigramHash embeddings, Muon optimizer, EMA, and int6+zstd quantization achieving 1.2073 BPB on 8xH100.

title: Non-record: Systematic Hyperparameter Search (val_bpb=1.2075)
technique_details: Pure hyperparameter search across 33 experiments on three GPU tiers (A40 → 1xH100 → 8xH100); best config: MATRIX_LR=0.02, MUON_MOMENTUM=0.99, WARMDOWN_ITERS=3000, ROPE_BASE=200000, TRAIN_SEQ_LEN=4096. No architectural changes from baseline.
hardware_notes: Multi-tier search: A40, 1xH100, then 8xH100 SXM for final run
key_findings: Systematic 33-experiment hyperparameter study revealing that optimal LR differs 5x across compute scales (A40 vs 8xH100); finds int6 STE + Muon conflicts (+0.007 BPB worse), while seq_len=4096 and Muon tuning yield meaningful gains.

title: Non-record: Int6 QAT + 11L 512d + Sliding Window, val_bpb=1.2089
technique_details: Full-stack int6 QAT with flat tensor storage (int6_mixed_per_row_v2 format); STE fake-int6 activating at step 200; sliding window eval (ctx=4096, chunk=512, stride=64); seq_len=4096; Muon (momentum=0.99, warmdown=3000). 11L × 512d × MLP1024; originally targeted MLP1536 but near-orthogonal Muon weights (~0.91× zlib) forced smaller model.
key_findings: Identifies a fundamental compression tension: Muon-trained weights are near-orthogonal and thus high-entropy, worsening zlib compression and forcing a smaller model to stay under 16MB. Suggests ternary training or structured weight matrices as a path to architectural rather than post-hoc compression.

title: SwiGLU dim=576 + Sliding Window + Muon WD (1.2091 BPB)
technique_details: Model width dim=576 with 7 layers and SwiGLU mult=2; Muon weight decay 0.02; FP16 embedding passthrough; sliding window eval stride=64; warmdown at 60% of wallclock. Architecture found via 111 automated experiments on RTX 3090. Run on 4xH100 with grad_accum=2 rather than standard 8xH100.
hardware_notes: 4xH100 SXM (RunPod) instead of standard 8xH100 due to unavailability; ~10.2K steps vs expected ~20K+ on 8xH100
key_findings: Demonstrates that width scaling (dim=576) outperforms depth scaling for this parameter budget, with architecture discovered through systematic automated search; however, the 4xH100 hardware deviation means results are likely pessimistic relative to the standard 8xH100 leaderboard baseline, limiting direct comparability.

title: Non-record: Add submission track_non_record_16mb/2026-03-23_DepthRecurrent_TTT
technique_details: Depth recurrence runs encoder and decoder blocks multiple times (ENCODER_LOOPS=2, DECODER_LOOPS=2) before propagating activations. Chunk-causal TTT LoRA adapters (rank=8, chunk_size=256) specialize to each document's distribution at eval time. VOCAB_SIZE=1024, dim=512, 8H/4KV GQA.
key_findings: Combines depth recurrence (running transformer blocks multiple times to increase effective depth without extra parameters) with chunk-causal TTT LoRA adaptation at evaluation, achieving 1.2092 BPB post-quant TTT on the non-record track.

title: [WIP] Record: Hybrid architecture 8L 3:1 GDN/Transformer (val_bpb=1.2093)
technique_details: Hybrid 8-layer architecture mixing GDN (Generalized Divisive Normalization) and Transformer blocks in a 3:1 ratio; WIP with size constraint issues needing quantization work and hyperparameter tuning; plans to incorporate TTT and top leaderboard tricks.
key_findings: WIP hybrid GDN/Transformer architecture at 8 layers achieving 1.2093 BPB; currently oversized and needs quantization and HP optimization before being competitive.

title: Record: Seq2048 training + eval (val_bpb=1.2101)
technique_details: Training and evaluating at sequence length 2048 instead of 1024 so RoPE positions seen at eval are interpolation not extrapolation. Same total tokens per step (524K) via 256 sequences of 2048. Post-quant uses int8+zlib roundtrip. 15.87MB artifact, 11,417 steps in 600s on 8xH100.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM
key_findings: Training at seq2048 instead of seq1024 yields -0.0143 BPB improvement by ensuring eval-time RoPE positions are interpolation rather than extrapolation. Across 20+ local ablations, seq2048 was the single largest improvement, surpassing SwiGLU and QAT.

title: Record: 10L Mixed Precision: val_bpb=1.2147 (10 layers + int6 middle layers)
technique_details: 10-layer model with mixed int8/int6 compression: full int8 for layers 0-2 and 7-9, int6 (step=4 rounding) for middle layers 3-6; lower LR MATRIX_LR=0.02 SCALAR_LR=0.02 TIED_EMBED_LR=0.03. Mixed precision allows 10-layer model (18.9M params) to fit within 16MB. Tested on 8xH200 (not H100).
hardware_notes: 8xH200 (not standard 8xH100); H100 verification pending
key_findings: Statistically rigorous 5-seed submission (p<0.001, t=39.29) showing 10-layer mixed int8/int6 precision achieves mean 1.2139 BPB, improving -0.0105 over baseline; hardware deviation to H200 noted as a concern.

title: warmdown-quantization val_bpb = 1.2154
technique_details: Always-decaying LR schedule with WARMDOWN_ITERS=20000 across ~12200 actual steps reduces post-quant penalty from 0.014 to 0.005 BPB. FP16 tied embeddings kept during Int8 export (~500KB cost). NTK-RoPE extrapolation evaluated at seq_len=1408 (1.375x training length). MLP_HIDDEN=992. MUON_BACKEND_STEPS=5 found optimal at high warmdown.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 SXM (RunPod)
