key_findings: Aggressive warmdown (WARMDOWN_ITERS=20000) reduces the Int8 post-quantization BPB penalty by ~3x at zero training-budget cost; NTK-RoPE eval at 1.375x training length outperforms eval at 2048 for well-trained models.

title: NTK Eval + Overtone Init (val_bpb=1.2160)
technique_details: Overtone embedding init uses SVD-based spectral shaping to power-law decay. Phase-transition residual mixing via sigmoid-scheduled resid_mix across layers. NTK-aware RoPE scaling: train at context 1024, eval at 2048 with dynamic base frequency adjustment. AdamW with weight decay 0.01, warmdown 2500 steps, tied embedding LR 0.10. Post-quant int8+zlib artifacts all under 16MB.
hardware_notes: Modal 8xH100 SXM (NVLink) — matches standard spec
key_findings: Combines NTK-aware RoPE context extension at eval time with a novel SVD-based spectral embedding initialization (Overtone init), achieving mean post-quant BPB of 1.2160 across 3 seeds with high statistical significance (p=0.0012, t=20.67).

title: Non-record: ASQU activation, Mixture of Convolutions, BankedLinear
technique_details: ASQU (Asymmetric Squared Unit): learned per-channel generalization of ReLU², ~0.001 bpb improvement over scalar form. MoC (Mixture of Convolutions): token-conditioned dynamic per-token kernel mixture applied to QKV projections. BankedLinear: shared weight bank across layers (small learned + large fixed random) with learned per-layer scalar mixing replacing QKV projections. Ablations at fixed 10k steps with MLP expansion adjusted to match size. Learning exponent instead of fixing square reveals depth-dependent pattern (early ~1.4, middle ~1.8, late ~2.2).
hardware_notes: Single-seed results only; additional runs in progress
key_findings: Cumulative architectural exploration showing ASQU + MoC + BankedLinear improves baseline from 1.2328 to 1.2164 BPB. Dynamic kernel generation via learned projection performs poorly with MoC; constrained basis interpolation is suggested as better approach.

title: Aweb Optimized Baseline — 1.2194 BPB
technique_details: All changes via env vars on unmodified NaiveBaseline train_gpt.py: MUON_MOMENTUM=0.99, MATRIX_LR=0.02, WARMDOWN_ITERS=3000, GRAD_CLIP_NORM=0.3, MLP_MULT=3, TRAIN_SEQ_LEN=2048, TRAIN_ON_VAL=1. 13,442 steps in 600s, 15.88MB artifact.
key_findings: Hyperparameter-only tuning of the NaiveBaseline (9L, 512d) achieves 1.2194 BPB with no code changes, showing that optimizer momentum, MLP 3x expansion, and longer warmdown provide meaningful gains over the default baseline.

title: Depth Recurrence + Cross-Repeat Skip + Sliding Window Eval
technique_details: 3 shared blocks repeated 4 times (12 effective layers), dim=832, with Cross-Repeat Skip (per-repeat learned residuals for stateful recurrence), 2 Value Embedding tables, LR scaled to 0.3x, sliding window eval with stride=256. Int8+zlib roundtrip. 4494 steps in 600s at 133ms/step.
hardware_notes: Development/ablations on RTX 3060 12GB; cloud GPUs used only for validation and final run
key_findings: Depth recurrence with shared blocks and novel Cross-Repeat Skip connections achieves 3.1x fewer training steps than baseline while beating it by 0.005 BPB; sliding window eval contributes significantly (standard roundtrip is 1.2533).

title: fp16 tied embedding + warmdown/LR tuning (val_bpb 1.2197)
technique_details: Keeps tied embedding/output head in FP16 during export to avoid int8 quantization gap (~0.007 → 0.0005 BPB cost). MLP hidden trimmed from 1024 to 992 to fit 16MB. Warmdown extended to 3600 steps; matrix LR set to 0.06.
hardware_notes: Primary results on 8xH100 SXM (RunPod secure); also tested on 8xH200 (1.216–1.218 range).
key_findings: First accepted challenge submission; demonstrates that keeping tied embeddings in FP16 nearly eliminates the quantization penalty at minimal size cost, yielding ~0.013 BPB improvement over baseline.

title: Non-record: 11L PartialRoPE + LNScale + EMA + SWA + TTT (1xH100 107min, val_bpb=1.2207, 15.4MB)
technique_details: Partial RoPE (16/64 dims), LN Scale (RMSNorm damped by 1/sqrt(layer+1)), BigramHash(2048×64), EMA(0.997) + SWA (last 40%), uniform int5 + zstd-22, sliding window eval stride=64, SGD TTT (3 epochs). Trained on 1×H100 PCIe for 107 minutes as non-standard hardware equivalent.
hardware_notes: 1×H100 PCIe, ~107 minutes (not standard 8xH100 SXM 10 min)
key_findings: Non-record exploration combining TTT (test-time training via SGD) with EMA+SWA and Partial RoPE on non-standard single-GPU hardware; BigramHash and quantization were reduced from v1 to bring artifact under 16MB.

title: [10min/16MB] TrigramHash + EMA-SWA + Int4 QAT — val_bpb 1.2219
technique_details: TrigramHash(2048, dim=48) extends BigramHash to 3-token context windows. EMA-SWA with α=0.9 for exponential weighting of late-warmdown checkpoints. Int4 QAT on MLP using STE fake-quantization to free budget for 11th layer.
hardware_notes: 8×A100 SXM 80GB (A100 instead of H100)
key_findings: Combines TrigramHash (3-token context), EMA-SWA, and Int4 QAT on MLP as three orthogonal innovations, achieving 1.2219 BPB. Int4 QAT frees enough budget to add an 11th layer.

title: Modal 8xH100 LowerLR FP16Embed 960 (val_bpb 1.22395)
technique_details: FP16 tied embeddings kept at higher precision in record snapshot. MLP_HIDDEN=960 tuned to stay under 16MB cap. Full 8xH100 timed run on Modal at 599.863s.
hardware_notes: Run on Modal 8xH100 (standard hardware).
key_findings: Clean 8xH100 record attempt achieving 1.22395 BPB with FP16 tied embeddings and MLP hidden dim of 960; labeled 'does not beat SOTA' by maintainers.

title: [WIP] Sparse Attention + Recursive Weight Sharing for 16MB Efficiency
technique_details: Sliding window attention with configurable WINDOW_SIZE; recursive weight tying with NUM_PHYSICAL_LAYERS (e.g. 3 physical blocks acting as 9 logical layers); quantization-ready layers. Seeking development grant for hyperparameter sweeps.
key_findings: WIP skeleton exploring sliding window attention and recursive weight tying to increase effective depth without increasing stored parameter count. Baseline of 1.2244 BPB with target <1.20; labeled not ready for review and appears largely AI-generated.

title: PROTEUS EMA — val_bpb: 1.2253
technique_details: Baseline architecture with EMA weight averaging added (26 lines). EMA reduces INT8 quantization loss from 0.0072 to 0.0048 BPB. Costs ~7% throughput (43.54ms → 46.58ms/step, 13780 → 12881 steps). Artifact 15.89MB, Int8+zlib compression.
hardware_notes: Run on Modal 8xH100 SXM instead of standard RunPod; author notes step time difference is platform overhead
key_findings: EMA reduces INT8 quantization loss by 33% (0.0072 → 0.0048 BPB) but the net effect is slightly negative versus baseline (1.2253 vs 1.2244), labeled 'does not beat SOTA'; a bot comment also flags a potential bug where an unupdated EMA buffer could corrupt the artifact.

title: Add Modal 8xH100 timed validation non-record submission
technique_details: Non-record track submission. Int8+zlib roundtrip val_bpb 1.22964598. Pre-quant BPB at stop was 1.2214. 13262/20000 steps completed in 599.942s on Modal 8xH100.
hardware_notes: Run on Modal 8xH100 (standard hardware).
key_findings: Non-record submission documenting a full timed 8xH100 Modal validation run; serves as a reproducibility baseline with int8+zlib roundtrip BPB of 1.22965.

title: Non-record: LeakyReLU² + LAWA + Ramping WD + Val Training (val_bpb=1.2302, 1xH100)
technique_details: 10-layer model with LeakyReLU(0.5)², LAWA checkpoint averaging over 12-13 warmdown checkpoints, ramping weight decay (0.02→0.08 during warmdown), validation set training, and lzma compression. Explored but rejected: recursive transformers with LoRA deltas, Differential Attention, Value Residual Learning, entropy-weighted loss, QAT.
hardware_notes: 1xH100 SXM, 600s; 8xH100 verification pending
key_findings: Budget-constrained 1xH100 exploration achieving 1.2302 post-quant BPB (1.2012 pre-quant, beating the 8xH100 baseline of 1.2244), with extensive documentation of 19 experiments including notable negative results for recursive transformers and Differential Attention.

title: Add record: INT6 10L SWA NorMuon, val_bpb=1.2320
technique_details: 10L×512 with MLP=1088, GQA 8/4 heads; Int6 per-row amax scale [-32,31] at 3.82x compression; SWA 50 snapshots every 200 steps averaged before quantization; NorMuon (per-row variance reduction after Newton-Schulz); warmdown=20000; NTK RoPE eval at 4096 context caused ~0.04 BPB penalty vs pre-quant 1.1923
key_findings: Pre-quant val_bpb=1.1923 is competitive but post-quant degrades to 1.2320 due to NTK RoPE eval at 4096 context; rerun at standard 2048 context expected to recover ~1.19.

title: Non-record: Hybrid Depth-Recurrent Transformer + Int5 Quantization Studies
technique_details: 8 physical layers × 2 loops = 16 effective depth from 20M stored params; solved quantization compounding for looped layers (0.40 BPB gap reduced to 0.007); novel input features including word-position, copy flags, unigram frequency; content-dependent SmearGate explored as negative result at H100 scale
hardware_notes: Content-dependent SmearGate experiment on RTX4070; main submission on 8xH100
key_findings: Demonstrates that quantization compounding in looped/recurrent architectures can be solved (gap reduced from 0.40 to 0.007 BPB); content-dependent SmearGate is a negative result at scale due to step overhead not compensating for added complexity.

title: Add chasewebb 9x512 sp1024 baseline (val_bpb: 1.2355)
technique_details: Standard 9-layer 512-dim transformer with sp1024 BPE tokenizer, GQA (8 heads / 4 KV heads), trained on ~80 FineWeb shards (~8B tokens). Int8+zlib compression to 15.87MB.
key_findings: Personal baseline submission with no novel techniques; establishes a reference point at 1.2355 BPB using a standard GQA transformer configuration.

title: Non-record: TTT-LoRA Base — HumanAI Convention (val_bpb=1.2364)
technique_details: Per-document TTT via rank-128 LoRA adapters trained with Adam on preceding chunks at eval time; TTT disabled in this submission due to LR miscalibration. Uses SmearGate residual mixing, orthogonal initialization, bigram hash embeddings (2048 buckets), GQA (8q/4kv heads), SWA over final 5065 steps, int6+zstd22 quantization (5.14x compression). Submission size 15,669,326 bytes.
key_findings: Introduces per-document TTT via LoRA as an evaluation-time technique orthogonal to all current leaderboard entries, but TTT is disabled in this submission due to LR miscalibration on the production model; local smoke tests showed -0.136 bpb improvement.

title: Add MaxParams6L_120 submission (1.2374 BPB) to track_non_record_16mb
technique_details: 6-layer encoder-decoder (3+3 with learned skip connections). SwiGLU MLP with 1280 hidden dim. Untied embeddings with embed_lr=0.6 (12x higher than competition default). Full MHA (8 KV heads, no GQA). Per-dimension learned controls: resid_mix, attn_scale, mlp_scale, q_gain (~18K params in fp32). INT8 + zlib, 13.5MB artifact, 19.15M parameters. Only 31% of training schedule completed on 1xH100.
hardware_notes: 1x H100 (non-standard; competition standard is 8xH100)
key_findings: Explores whether fewer layers with faster steps can compensate for lost depth under fixed wallclock: 6 layers complete 4,222 steps vs 3,338 for 9 layers (+26%). Duplicate of closed PR #391, resubmitted open.

title: Add MaxParams6L_120 submission (1.2374 BPB) to track_non_record_16mb
technique_details: 6-layer encoder-decoder (3+3 with learned skip connections). SwiGLU MLP with 1280 hidden dim. Untied embeddings with embed_lr=0.6. Full MHA (8 KV heads). Per-dimension learned controls: resid_mix, attn_scale, mlp_scale, q_gain. INT8 + zlib, 13.5MB artifact, 19.15M parameters. Closed and resubmitted as PR #395.
hardware_notes: 1x H100 (non-standard; competition standard is 8xH100)
key_findings: Duplicate of PR #395, closed in favor of resubmission. Explores 6-layer architecture with faster training steps to compensate for reduced depth under fixed wallclock budget.

title: Non-record: 2026-03-22_SuperchunkBPE_SP1024
technique_details: Superchunk BPE: two-phase tokenization where phase-1 learns intra-chunk merges (standard BPE), phase-2 learns cross-chunk merges on sequences of phase-1 tokens, then interleaves both by frequency into one merge table. Vocab size 1024. Rust BPE implementation. Int8+zlib post-training quantization. Pre-quant val_bpb 1.2294 vs post-quant 1.2389.
key_findings: Exploratory test of superchunk BPE tokenization (cross-chunk merge learning) against stock 1024-vocab recipe; achieves 1.2389 post-quant BPB, significantly above current SOTA, suggesting cross-chunk merges do not provide meaningful gains over standard BPE at this vocabulary size.

title: Add BigramHash: hashed bigram embeddings with optional dim projection
technique_details: Adds BigramHash to train_gpt.py via BIGRAM_BUCKETS and BIGRAM_DIM env vars. Hash: (prev*1000003+cur)%num_buckets. Optional linear projection when BIGRAM_DIM < model_dim saves ~3.7MB artifact space. Learned scale init=0.3. Separate optimizer group at token embedding LR. 129 experiments across RTX 5070 Ti and H100 SXM.
hardware_notes: Tested on RTX 5070 Ti and H100 SXM (non-standard hardware for experiments)
key_findings: Infrastructure PR adding configurable BigramHash embeddings with optional dimensionality projection; 12288 buckets + BIGRAM_DIM=128 achieves 1.2392 BPB on H100 in 10min, with the projection saving 3.7MB at minimal quality cost.

title: Non-Record: 8L + BigramHash(12288) + Systematic HyperOpt (val_bpb=1.2392, 1xH100, 129 experiments)
technique_details: 8L, d=512, 8Q/4KV heads (GQA). BigramHash 12288 buckets with dim=128 projection. Muon LR=0.03, WD=0.048, batch=131K tokens. RoPE base=50000, logit softcap=30. 129 experiments mapping hyperparameter scaling laws. WD used as artifact size control knob. 4x MLP with relu^2.
hardware_notes: 1x H100 SXM 80GB (single GPU, non-standard)
key_findings: Systematic 129-experiment hyperparameter study achieving 1.2392 BPB on 1xH100; documents 17 rejected techniques and quantifies weight decay as a compression knob for artifact size control.

title: Non-record: 7L + BigramHash Projection + Batch Scaling (val_bpb=1.2417, 1xH100)
technique_details: BigramHash 8192 with dim=128 projection saves 3.7MB artifact space with <0.002 BPB loss. Batch size 131K tokens/step (2x default) gives -0.024 BPB. Muon optimizer LR=0.035, WD=0.025. Systematic study across 111 experiments found EMA catastrophic for <5k steps, SWA no benefit <7k steps, SmearGate throughput penalty exceeds quality gain on single GPU. Closed for further experimentation.
hardware_notes: 1x H100 SXM 80GB (non-standard; competition standard is 8xH100)
key_findings: Extensive 111-experiment ablation on single GPU revealing hyperparameter scaling laws: LR, WD, and batch size must co-scale with GPU speed and step count. BigramHash projection at dim=128 is an efficient technique to reduce artifact size with minimal BPB cost.

title: Add submission: Mixed Quantization + BigramHash + SWA (val_bpb 1.2421)
technique_details: 10-layer transformer with ReLU² MLP (3x), BigramHash embeddings, mixed INT6 quantization with STE, SWA, and zstd-22 compression. Minimal description provided.
key_findings: Basic baseline-level submission at 1.2421 BPB combining standard competition techniques (INT6 QAT, BigramHash, SWA) without novel contributions or ablations.

title: Non-record: 10L mixed int5/int6 export reaches ~10.4MB with strong throughput
technique_details: Int5 for MLP matrices, int6 for attention matrices, int8 elsewhere; micro-TTT (eval-time SGD) on q_gain, attn_scale, mlp_scale, resid_mix, skip_weights; zlib compression; artifact ~10.4MB vs 16MB cap. TTT recovers only a small fraction of quantization loss.
key_findings: Mixed int5/int6/int8 quantization compresses the artifact to ~10.4MB (well under 16MB cap), but the aggressive compression is too destructive and micro-TTT only partially recovers the BPB loss, making this a useful direction marker rather than a competitive result.

title: Submission: val_bpb=1.2459 (autoresearch-optimized)
technique_details: 97 autonomous experiments on RTX 4080, validated on H100. 16,562 steps in 600s on 8xH100 SXM. Artifact 15.9MB.
key_findings: Autoresearch-optimized submission achieving 1.2459 val_bpb via 97 autonomous experiments prototyped on RTX 4080 and validated on 8xH100, fitting within the 16MB artifact budget.

title: Non-record: Cache LM + LoRA TTT (negative result on cache, positive on TTT)
technique_details: Per-document rank-8 LoRA on Q/V/lm_head with Adam lr=0.01, 256-token chunks for TTT. Unigram Cache LM (Grave et al. 2017) interpolation with λ=0.02 tested as negative result. 7L GQA + MTP k=2 α=0.3, tied embeddings. Trained 2000 steps on 1xA100.
hardware_notes: 1xA100 (1/10th of full budget, no torch.compile)
key_findings: LoRA TTT gives -0.003 BPB improvement over sliding window, but unigram Cache LM hurts by +0.002 on FineWeb web text due to short, diverse documents that don't accumulate sufficient unigram cache signal before document boundary resets.

title: Non-record unlimited-compute: 1-hour 1xH100 warmdown 9x512
technique_details: Extended 1-hour run on 1xH100 (non-record unlimited-compute track) on baseline 9x512 architecture with warmdown; artifact remains under 16MB.
hardware_notes: 1xH100 (single GPU, not standard 8xH100)
key_findings: A 1-hour 1xH100 run improves BPB to 1.2540 from 1.3486 achieved in the standard 10-minute budget, demonstrating meaningful gains from extended training on the same architecture.

title: WIP: Shared-transformer + warmdown-aligned training (not final submis…
technique_details: Shared-transformer (single block reused) with MLP×5 expansion, BigramHash(4096), SWA, warmdown-aligned schedule. Also experiments with TTT LoRA. Reports ~1.26 bpb Int8 roundtrip and ~1.30 bpb TTT LoRA. WIP, not a final submission.
key_findings: Early WIP exploring shared-weight transformers; key finding is that performance is dominated by a late-stage phase transition driven by LR warmdown, and aligning WARMDOWN_ITERS with the wallclock budget converts wasted steps into usable convergence.

title: Add non-record JEPA byte-level encoder-decoder submission
technique_details: JEPA encoder-decoder: byte-level tokenizer (vocab 260, no BPE), 5-layer depth-recurrent encoder (2 repeats) with patched latent projection, 7-layer causal decoder conditioned on encoder latents. INT6 optimal-clip quantization with STE QAT during warmdown, LZMA preset-9 compression. Sliding window TTT with SGD (lr=0.002), 2 epochs per chunk, stride 256. 24.6M parameters, trained for 10,635/20,000 steps.
key_findings: First JEPA-style encoder-decoder submission to the competition using byte-level tokenization, achieving 1.2622 BPB post-TTT; significantly behind SOTA but demonstrates a fundamentally different architectural approach to the task.

title: [Non-record] Azure 1xH100 frontier-family engineering run (val_bpb=1.2623)
technique_details: Engineering reference run on non-standard hardware (1xH100 NVL) with extended wallclock (1800s). Post-quant roundtrip BPB 1.26233375, artifact 12.7MB. Eval tail interrupted by SIGTERM after metric printed.
hardware_notes: 1x NVIDIA H100 NVL 94GB (not 8xH100 SXM); training wallclock 1800s (not 600s)
key_findings: Non-record engineering reference submission on single H100 with extended training time, bridging proxy-only work toward future 8xH100 submission-grade runs; explicitly not a leaderboard claim.

title: Non-record: Depth-recurrent 5x3 d768, val_bpb=1.2663
technique_details: 5 unique transformer blocks looped 3x for 15 effective layers at dim=768. GQA 12:6 heads. 21.4M params, ~13.9MB compressed. Trained on 4xH100 SXM for 2651 steps.
hardware_notes: 4xH100 SXM (non-standard, baseline uses 8xH100)
key_findings: Depth-recurrent transformer with 5x3 loop achieves val_bpb=1.2663 vs baseline 1.2244, still improving at cutoff. Demonstrates that wider recurrent models (d768) with GQA fit within 16MB budget but underperform baseline in this run.

title: Non-record: Depth-recurrent 5x3 d768, val_bpb=1.2663
technique_details: 5 shared transformer blocks looped 3x for 15 effective layers at d_model=768 (vs baseline 9 layers at d_model=512). GQA with 12 query heads and 6 key-value heads. 21.4M params, ~13.9MB compressed. Run on 4xH100 SXM for 2651 steps; model still improving at cutoff.
hardware_notes: 4xH100 SXM, deviates from standard 8xH100
key_findings: Explores depth-recurrent weight sharing with a wider model (d768 vs d512 baseline) using 5 blocks looped 3 times. Scores 1.2663 BPB, above the baseline of 1.2244, with the loss curve still improving at the time limit.

title: Optimized SOTA Submission: 1.2697 bpb
technique_details: 9x432 model with GQA, int8+zlib compression, systematic hyperparameter tuning over 8 experiments. 10-minute budget fully utilized.
key_findings: Systematic 8-experiment campaign moving from 1.42 to 1.27 BPB, but closed by maintainer as superseded by a newer SOTA before validation.

title: Non-record: Depth Recurrence 5x3 — Weight-Shared Looping Transformer (6xH200, val_bpb=1.2716)
technique_details: 5 unique transformer layers looped 3x for 15 effective depth at ~15M params; per-loop learnable embeddings and scalar gates to differentiate passes; wider model (dim=640 vs baseline 512); key negative finding: loop gates initialized at 1/N over-regularize, collapsing effective compute to ~1.3 loops; removing U-Net skip connections hurt more than recurrence helped.
hardware_notes: 6xH200 (RunPod); standard is 8xH100. Run terminated early at step 4500/~5300 due to compute budget expiry.
key_findings: Depth recurrence with weight-shared looping trails baseline by ~0.02 BPB at comparable FLOPs; the primary failure mode is over-regularizing loop gates that collapse useful recurrence, identified as the single highest-leverage fix for future runs.

title: non-record:10Layer + BigramHash+ SWA + Attention-Residuals
technique_details: Mixed Int5/Int6 quantization: MLP weights at 5-bit, attention weights at 6-bit; uses the space savings to add a 10th transformer layer. BigramHash expanded to 10,240 buckets. Attention-Residuals: removes standard layer residuals, replaces with rolling history of previous layer outputs with learned softmax-weighted query mechanism.
key_findings: Explores mixed Int5/Int6 quantization to fund an extra transformer layer and a novel attention residual mechanism using learned softmax-weighted history, achieving 1.2767 BPB as a non-record exploration.

title: Non-record: 1xH100 warmdown100 30m scaling run
technique_details: Baseline script with WARMDOWN_ITERS=100 run for 30 minutes on 1xH100. Compared against same-session 10-minute control (val_bpb=1.3464) showing 0.069 improvement from additional compute.
hardware_notes: 1xH100 (non-standard), 30-minute run (non-standard)
key_findings: Simple scaling experiment showing 30-minute 1xH100 run with WARMDOWN_ITERS=100 achieves 1.2771 BPB vs 1.3464 for 10-minute control, a 0.069 improvement, providing low-cost evidence of compute scaling.

title: Non-record: trigram phrase-memory ablation on 1×H100: negative result (1.2791 BPB best)
technique_details: Controlled ablation of TrigramHash lookup tables (2048×64 and 4096×96 variants) against matched slim backbone; no-trigram baseline beats both trigram variants on 1×H100; artifact did not fit under 16MB limit
hardware_notes: 1×H100 SXM, 600s wallclock; not run on standard 8×H100
key_findings: Controlled ablation confirms TrigramHash is a negative result at 1×H100 scale: slim backbone without trigrams (1.3007 BPB) outperforms slim+trigram variants (1.3029-1.3040 BPB), suggesting byte budget is better spent on backbone capacity; open question whether this reverses at 8×H100 scale.

title: Non-record: Custom sp4096 BPE Tokenizer (1.2827 BPB on 1×H100)
technique_details: Custom sp4096 BPE tokenizer trained on 2M FineWeb documents achieving 2.75 bytes/token vs baseline 2.00 bytes/token (+37.5%). Tested on 1x H100 rather than the standard 8x H100; author estimates ~1.18-1.20 on 8xH100.
hardware_notes: 1x H100 (non-standard; standard is 8x H100)
key_findings: First custom tokenizer submission to the competition; the 4096-vocab BPE tokenizer provides 37.5% better byte compression than the baseline tokenizer, though the score of 1.2827 is confounded by single-GPU compute vs the standard 8xH100 setup.

title: Non-record: Meta-TTT + Cache/OGD Eval Stacking + Tokenizer Ablation
technique_details: Three ablations: (1) MAML-style Meta-TTT during training hurt BPB (+0.085); (2) unigram cache mixture + online gradient descent on vocab bias stacked on SGD TTT gave -0.003 additive gain at zero artifact cost; (3) BPE tokenizer with split_digits=False and max_len=64 gave -5.7% tokens/byte but no BPB improvement.
hardware_notes: 1xA40 Secure, 2000 steps (not 8xH100)
key_findings: MAML-style Meta-TTT is a negative result; however, stacking unigram cache + OGD on top of TTT yields a small additive BPB gain at zero artifact cost, and a tokenizer ablation explains why the community converged on stock v1024.

title: Non-Record: QAT + NTK-4096 Eval + Cosine Warmdown + Aggressive SWA (val_bpb=1.2890, 1xh100)
technique_details: QAT with STE using mixed int5 (MLPs) / int6 (attention) fake quantization. NTK-aware RoPE rescaling for 4096 eval context. Cosine warmdown schedule. Aggressive SWA from 35% of warmdown every 25 steps. SmearGate token blending + BigramHash 10240 buckets. 5% magnitude pruning. lzma PRESET_EXTREME compression. U-Net skip connections with orthogonal init. 3x MLP expansion.
hardware_notes: Best full run on 1xH100 (872 steps); 8xH100 attempts killed by RunPod pod termination before eval completed.
key_findings: Incomplete submission due to RunPod instability; only 1xH100 roundtrip BPB (1.2890) available. Introduces mixed int5/int6 QAT via STE and NTK-aware RoPE for extended eval context, but could not achieve a valid 8xH100 result.

title: Add CTM tail-QAT proxy non-record snapshot
technique_details: Causal CTM architecture with error-gated skip connections and export-matched tail-QAT. Proxy run on 1xH100 for 20 minutes using only 10 shards. Artifact slightly over limit at 16,009,531 bytes.
hardware_notes: 1xH100, 20-minute proxy run (non-standard: single GPU, longer time, 10 shards only)
key_findings: In-progress non-record snapshot of a causal CTM + error-gated skip + tail-QAT recipe run on 1xH100 as a proxy; artifact is slightly over the 16MB limit and score (1.2917) is well below SOTA, submitted for documentation purposes.

title: feat: Add non-record dense 2048 sliding-window ablation submission
technique_details: Dense 10-layer transformer with 2048-token context, sliding-window eval at stride 64, tied embeddings with spectral init, GQA, Muon with decoupled weight decay, residual mixing, post-training int8 quantization, zlib compression. Structured ablation loop used to identify effective components; negative results documented for 4096 context, lower matrix LR, longer warmdown, recurrent/shared-depth variants, and LoRA TTT.
hardware_notes: 8xH100 run in progress at time of submission
key_findings: Empirical ablation submission documenting which components help in a dense 2048/64 sliding-window setup, reaching 1.2928 BPB, with negative results for several neighboring ideas including 4096 context and LoRA TTT included as useful guidance for future work.

title: Non-record: hybrid spiking Transformer (SNN) with a multi-step spiking MLP
technique_details: Hybrid Transformer + SNN design: standard GQA attention and training/eval/compression pipeline preserved, but feed-forward blocks replaced with multi-step leaky integrate-and-fire (LIF-style) spiking MLP. Embeddings, attention, residuals, and logits remain dense.
key_findings: First spiking neural network hybrid submission in this competition, replacing only the MLP blocks with LIF-style spiking units to isolate the impact of spike-based computation; achieves 1.2982 BPB, well above SOTA but a novel architectural exploration.

title: Non-record: Warmdown-Tuned Training (val_bpb=1.2987) on 1xRTX 5090
technique_details: Key finding: warmdown_iters=3000 is optimal, producing 2.6x per-step BPB improvement. Register tokens tested and ruled out. Layer looping + wider model in progress.
hardware_notes: 1xRTX 5090, 1200s wallclock
key_findings: Extensive warmdown tuning experiments showing warmdown_iters=3000 is optimal with 2.6x per-step efficiency gain. Register tokens tested across 3 approaches and found to not improve performance.

title: Crystal Curriculum — TF-IDF curriculum learning by Bee Bytez
technique_details: Crystallizer module: TF-IDF-based curriculum learning that scores candidate batches by token entropy (unigram statistics) and oversamples high information-density sequences, ~2ms/batch overhead. Curriculum schedule: dense data first, linearly decaying to uniform sampling. 10 transformer layers, Muon WD=0.02. Local results on 1x H100, 10 min: val_bpb 1.2988 (LoRA TTT), 1.3278 (int8+zlib), model 13.8MB.
hardware_notes: Local validation on 1x H100 SXM, 10 min — not full 8xH100 run
key_findings: Novel TF-IDF curriculum learning module oversamples high information-density sequences, achieving marginal improvement over baseline (1.2988 vs 1.3049 BPB with TTT) despite running 48 fewer steps due to scoring overhead.

title: Non-record: HyperparamTuned KV2 + FP16 Embed
technique_details: Systematic hyperparameter tuning of baseline 9x512 architecture: NUM_KV_HEADS reduced 4→2, MATRIX_LR 0.04→0.048, WARMDOWN_ITERS 1200→600, SCALAR_LR 0.04→0.03, MUON_MOMENTUM 0.95→0.97, QK_GAIN_INIT 1.5→1.35, tok_emb export changed from int8 to fp16. 1xH100 preliminary results only; 8xH100 validation pending.
hardware_notes: 1xH100 preliminary run only; 8xH100 SXM validation pending
key_findings: Seven throughput-neutral hyperparameter changes yield -0.8% BPB improvement on 1xH100 (1.3003 vs 1.3106 baseline). Key insight: all techniques that slowed per-step time (SwiGLU, depth recurrence, etc.) were net negative under the wallclock cap, emphasizing throughput as the primary constraint.

title: Non-record: Wider-shallower 4x768 + QAT (1xH100, 1.3043 bpb)
technique_details: Wider-shallower architecture: 4 layers x 768 dim with GQA (12h/4kv). QK gain 2.5 for sharper attention. Muon lr=0.06, grad clip 0.5, beta2=0.99. QAT via STE after 0.5% warmup, reducing int8 gap from ~0.03 to 0.0016 BPB. 51 hyperparameter experiments on A40, 4 validation runs on 1xH100.
hardware_notes: 1xH100 SXM 80GB, not 8xH100
key_findings: Wider-shallower 4L/768d architecture beats the baseline by 0.032 BPB at matched wallclock on 1xH100. Extrapolated 8xH100 performance estimated at ~1.19 BPB at 20K steps, pending official run.

title: Non-record: LoRA TTT exploration on SOTA base (negative result)
technique_details: LoRA rank-8 adapters on Q/V projections for test-time training; chunk=256, evaluated at seq_len=1024 and 2048; per-document LoRA reset; built on thwu1 Int5-MLP + BigramHash + SWA base.
hardware_notes: 1xH100 only; full 8xH100 run pending
key_findings: TTT with LoRA rank-8 adapters on Q/V projections degrades BPB by +0.138 to +0.163 on a SOTA base that already uses SmearGate + BigramHash, demonstrating TTT provides no benefit when long-range context and strong local embeddings are already present.

title: Add non-record 1xH200 fp16-embed baseline sweep submission
technique_details: SP-1024 baseline with tok_emb.weight preserved in FP16 during int8 export (instead of quantizing embeddings). Sweep also tested Sink4, MTP1, MTP2 variants on 1xH200.
hardware_notes: 1xH200, not verified on 8xH100
key_findings: FP16 embedding passthrough during int8 export improved BPB from 1.32172 to 1.32078 on 1xH200, outperforming more complex objective-side changes like multi-token prediction variants.

title: Record: 11L Int6 QAT + Warmdown (val_bpb=1.3267, 1xH100)
technique_details: Int6 grouped quantization (group_rows=8). QAT with STE fake-quantization in last 15% of wallclock. Wallclock-fraction warmdown (last 15%) fixing buggy iter-based formula. SWA with 7 checkpoints during warmdown guarded against premature collection. Sliding window eval stride=64. Muon WD=0.04, momentum warmup 0.92→0.99. 6-variant ablation study on 1xH100.
hardware_notes: 1xH100 (non-standard; competition standard is 8×H100); 8xH100 verification pending
key_findings: Systematic 6-variant ablation shows wallclock-fraction warmdown + SWA is the dominant improvement (1.3761→1.3267), outperforming BigramHash+SmearGate and mixed Int5/Int6 additions; fixes a wallclock warmdown bug from prior iter-based approach.

title: Add baseline H100 training report and process docs
technique_details: Baseline 1xH100 run using int8+zlib roundtrip; 1404 steps in 600s; documentation PR with training report, process guide, and proposed next experiments (QAT int6/int5, 3x MLP, SwiGLU, BigramHash)
hardware_notes: 1x NVIDIA H100 80GB SXM (non-standard, single GPU)
key_findings: Documentation/baseline PR closed for resubmission with proper folder structure; establishes 1.3274 BPB as a single-GPU baseline starting point for the author's subsequent experiments.

title: [codex] Validate sliding-window post-quant evaluation on 1xH100 proxy
technique_details: Adds sliding-window evaluation mode (stride and batch size configurable) so each scored token sees near-max context, improving post-quantization score. Used as a proxy pipeline for ranking architecture ideas on 1xH100 before committing multi-GPU budget. Reports final int8+zlib roundtrip val_bpb of 1.32756718.
hardware_notes: 1xH100 proxy run on RunPod — not standard 8xH100
key_findings: Draft PR demonstrating sliding-window post-quant evaluation as a cost-efficient proxy for ranking architecture ideas; current best proxy result is 1.32757 BPB on 1xH100, not intended as a final leaderboard submission.

title: Non-record: SwiGLU + warmdown fix + quarter batch (1x5090, 1.3281 bpb)
technique_details: Discovered warmdown_iters=1200 bug causing LR decay from step 1 on 600s wallclock; fixed with warmdown_frac=0.2 (-0.006 bpb). SwiGLU replaces ReLU² (-0.004 bpb). Quarter batch size 131K tokens (-0.016 bpb). Gradient accumulation x2 (-0.002 bpb). Negative results for weight decay and layer recurrence.
hardware_notes: 1x RTX 5090 (vs standard 8xH100); ~3,773 steps vs ~13,780 on reference hardware
key_findings: Identifies and fixes a warmdown schedule bug in the stock baseline worth ~0.006 BPB, and documents hardware-agnostic improvements from SwiGLU and smaller batch sizes totaling -0.035 BPB over baseline. Performance gap vs leaderboard is attributed to single-GPU throughput.

title: Add Hybrid Depth-Recurrent Transformer submission
technique_details: Hybrid depth-recurrent architecture: 1 unique entry layer + 4 shared blocks × 5 loops + 1 unique exit layer = 22 effective layers from 6 weight blocks; U-Net skip connections; per-virtual-layer scalars (attn_scale, mlp_scale, resid_mix, q_gain); FP16 tied embedding passthrough; reduces quantization gap from 0.40 to near-zero (-0.004 BPB); Overtone spectral embedding init; phase-transition resid_mix init. Only 2xH100 used.
hardware_notes: 2xH100 only; 8xH100 run pending
key_findings: Hybrid depth-recurrent architecture with unique entry/exit layers and shared looped middle blocks reduces the int8 quantization compounding gap from 0.40 to near-zero BPB, though the 1.3323 BPB result uses only 2xH100 and is not yet competitive.

title: Non-record: Stacked hyperparameter tuning + eval2048 (RTX 5090, val_bpb 1.336)
technique_details: 40+ automated hyperparameter experiments. Key fix: WARMDOWN_ITERS=3000 (not 1200, which fires from step 1 at ~968 total steps). Stacked: MATRIX_LR=0.06, LOGIT_SOFTCAP=15, MUON_MOMENTUM=0.99, TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS=131072, EVAL_SEQ_LEN=2048. Also implemented alias-aware serialization, mixed int6/int8 (INT6_ALL_BLOCK_MATRICES), sliding-window eval, depth recurrence support.
hardware_notes: RTX 5090 on RunPod, not 8xH100
key_findings: Identifies critical WARMDOWN_ITERS bug (1200 > total steps at ~968 steps/run, causing warmdown from step 1) and documents 5 stacked config fixes improving BPB by 0.027. Negative results include Butterfly/Monarch MLP (1.46 BPB) and depth recurrence (1.50 BPB).

title: EBLS Learned Sharing (10min/16MB)
technique_details: EBLS: 3 shared transformer blocks × 3 virtual layers = 9 effective layers, with per-virtual-layer rank-8 LoRA deviations gated by learned shrinkage factors gamma_i = sigmoid(logit_i). Model discovers optimal sharing from data: MLP gammas converge to 0 (fully shared), attention shows minimal specialization only in early layers. 1024-dim, 16Q/4KV GQA, 3x MLP with ReLU². Artifact slightly over 16MB limit (16,224,826 bytes).
key_findings: EBLS empirically demonstrates that MLP weights benefit from full sharing across virtual layers (gamma→0) while attention requires minimal specialization only in early layers, providing data-driven evidence for architectural sharing decisions; artifact exceeded 16MB limit and submission was superseded.

title: Submission: Low-Rank All-Attention (1.3446 bpb)
technique_details: Replaces feed-forward NN with persistent memory (augmented self-attention). Int8 quantization for tensors >16384 elements, FP16 for smaller tensors. LoRA factorization of weight matrices (W = W_d * W_u) to reduce parameter count.
key_findings: First submission exploring an all-attention architecture replacing FFN with persistent memory, combined with Int8 quantization and LoRA weight factorization. Author acknowledges it needs more tuning for size/quantization trade-off.

title: Non-Record: Replace Muon optimizer with NorMuon for baseline (1xH100)
technique_details: Replaces Muon with NorMuon (neuron-wise normalized Muon). NorMuon adds per-neuron update normalization via EMA of mean-squared updates (beta2=0.95), dividing each neuron's update by sqrt of second-order momentum, then renormalizing to original norm. Achieves -0.003 BPB improvement over Muon baseline at 1xH100 scale.
hardware_notes: Single H100 NVL (non-standard); full 8xH100 run pending compute credits
key_findings: NorMuon (neuron-wise normalized Muon) improves over Muon baseline by 0.003 BPB on 1xH100, addressing non-uniform per-neuron update norms from Newton-Schulz approximation. Full 8xH100 validation still pending.

title: Add Parameter Golf submission: Depth12 Dim416 KV4
technique_details: 12L×416 with MLP_MULT=2, GQA 8/4 heads, tied embeddings, VOCAB_SIZE=1024, batch=524288 tokens; int8+zlib export at 14.3MB; stopped at 9067/20000 steps due to wallclock cap
key_findings: Baseline-style 12-layer dim=416 submission achieves val_bpb=1.3509 with standard int8+zlib; relatively weak score compared to later submissions, stopped at fewer than half the planned steps due to wallclock cap.

title: Add non-record local A100 TTT eval-stride0 submission
technique_details: Non-record local run focused on TTT metric; int8+zlib compression; TTT LoRA eval; eval stride=0 (no sliding window); 856 steps completed in 600s wallclock cap.
hardware_notes: Run on local 1xA100 (non-standard hardware, vs 8xH100)
key_findings: Local single-GPU reproduction exploring TTT LoRA evaluation; significantly lower step count (856) due to single A100 vs 8xH100, resulting in high BPB of 1.3510; submitted as non-record hardware baseline.

title: Attention Warm-Start: Initializing Q/K from Bigram Co-occurrence SVD
technique_details: Initializes W_Q and W_K in layer 0 from bigram co-occurrence matrix via SVD: build co-occurrence from 2M tokens, log-transform + double-center for PMI-like matrix, project to model_dim via fixed random projection, SVD factorize and assign components to heads for diversity. Zero extra parameters, <3s overhead. Built on PR #623. Slightly worse than baseline (1.3525 vs 1.3345) due to noise from random projection P.
hardware_notes: 1×H100 only due to compute constraints
key_findings: Warm-starting Q/K from bigram co-occurrence SVD slightly hurts performance (1.3525 vs 1.3345 baseline) due to noise introduced by random projection; using the actual embedding matrix as projection basis is suggested as improvement.

title: Add local baseline reproduction record
technique_details: Local single-GPU baseline reproduction with train_shards=1, seq_len=1024, grad_accum_steps=8.
hardware_notes: Local single-GPU (non-standard)
key_findings: Non-SOTA baseline reproduction run on local single GPU; performance plateaued at step 4200 with val_bpb=1.3529.

title: [Non-record] MLA + SmearGate + BigramHash + SWA — pre-quant 1.2838 bpb
technique_details: Multi-Head Latent Attention (MLA) with kv_rank=128, SmearGate MLP (relu^2 gated, mlp_mult=3), BigramHash(10240 buckets, dim=128), SWA (start_frac=0.4, every=50 steps), mixed int5 MLP / int6 attention / fp16 embeddings quantization, zstd-22. 13 layers, 512-dim, 8 heads, 4 KV heads. MLA roughly doubles step time (~83ms vs ~43ms baseline), halving tokens seen.
key_findings: Demonstrates that MLA's compute overhead (~83ms/step vs ~43ms baseline) halves token throughput to ~3.7B tokens in the 10-minute budget, bottlenecking training despite competitive architecture; pre-quant BPB of 1.2838 suggests architecture capacity is not the limiting factor.

title: Non-record: LapushBaby stock baseline 1xGPU RunPod
technique_details: Stock root train_gpt.py with no modifications. Single GPU run on RunPod, 10 training shards of FineWeb sp1024 tokenization. Reports int8+zlib roundtrip exact BPB.
hardware_notes: 1x GPU on RunPod (non-standard, deviates from 8xH100 requirement)
key_findings: Baseline reproducibility submission using unmodified stock train_gpt.py on a single RunPod GPU, achieving 1.363 BPB; useful as a reference point for single-GPU performance.

title: Non-record: 1.366 BPB Baseline (SmearGate + Muon, int6, zstd)
technique_details: 14 layers, 512 dimensions, 1.5x MLP expansion (float), SmearGate for local context, Muon with 0.02 weight decay, zstd level 22. Submitted primarily as reference for a compute credit request; model fits 20.7M parameters to ~12MB but lacks GPU resources to increase parameters.
hardware_notes: Submitted without full 8xH100 run due to lack of GPU resources
key_findings: Baseline reference submission showing SmearGate + Muon + int6 + zstd stack at 14 layers achieving 1.366 BPB; primarily submitted to support a compute credit request rather than as a competitive record.

title: Add non-record 16MB layers7 submission
technique_details: Shallower 7-layer model (vs 9-layer baseline) with VOCAB_SIZE=1024, MODEL_DIM=512, NUM_HEADS=8, NUM_KV_HEADS=4, MLP_MULT=2; int8+zlib export; motivated by wallclock-constrained speed-capacity tradeoff on 1xA100.
hardware_notes: Developed on 1xA100 with 600s wallclock cap
key_findings: Under a hard 600s wallclock budget, reducing depth to 7 layers outperforms the 9-layer baseline by reaching a better speed-capacity tradeoff. Submitted as a clean non-record documenting this architectural observation.

title: Add non-record submission: 8xH100 FineWeb baseline + TTT eval (val_bpb 1.3825)
technique_details: Int8 quantization with zlib compression, QAT starting at 10% of training, TTT LoRA eval, Muon optimizer with momentum warmup, GQA (12 query heads / 4 KV heads), MLP_MULT=3, selective FP32 retention for embedding/scale layers
hardware_notes: RunPod remote machine with NCCL P2P/IB/NVLS/CUMEM disabled for compatibility
key_findings: Non-record baseline documenting TTT LoRA eval at 1.3825 BPB; the post-quant roundtrip score (1.4225) is significantly worse than the TTT-boosted score, suggesting TTT provides a notable eval-time gain over the raw quantized model.

title: Non-record: Mixture of Softmax K=2 R=64 (1xH100, 10min, 1.3932 bpb)
technique_details: Replaces standard tied-embedding softmax with K=2 Mixture of Softmax using rank-64 low-rank factorization (~99K extra params, ~97KB overhead) on baseline 9x512 architecture. Int8+zlib compression. Only 1113 steps completed on 1xH100.
hardware_notes: 1x H100 SXM — significantly underpowered vs standard 8xH100; only 1113/20000 steps completed
key_findings: Explores Mixture of Softmax (Yang et al., 2018) to address softmax bottleneck in the 9x512 baseline; achieves 1.3932 post-quant BPB but is heavily undertrained due to single-GPU constraint. Low-rank factorization keeps artifact overhead to ~97KB with only +0.0011 BPB quantization degradation.

title: Depth-recurrent transformer: shared block × 12 passes, val_bpb 1.4061, 4.39MB artifact
technique_details: Single shared transformer block applied 12 times (depth recurrence); U-Net skip connections between encoder and decoder passes; x0 residual mix injects original embedding at every pass for stability; wider model dim=768 enabled by parameter sharing. Int8 + zlib roundtrip.
hardware_notes: RTX 4090, ~45s training time (not 8xH100)
key_findings: Depth recurrence with one shared block × 12 passes reduces unique params to 4.7M and fits in 4.39MB, but achieves only 1.4061 BPB—well below SOTA—suggesting parameter sharing at this scale is insufficient without additional techniques.

title: Hybrid INL + Sort-Split MoE (1.41/1.46 bpb TTT, 15.5MB, 1xH100)
technique_details: Hybrid architecture: layers 0-4 use GQA+RoPE attention, layers 5-8 use INL BetaMu O(n) cumsum attention. Sort-and-Split MoE with 4 experts and relu² activation. ALiBi with learned slopes in INL layers. Int8+zlib compression.
hardware_notes: 1xH100 (non-standard)
key_findings: Introduces INL BetaMu, an error-driven O(n) attention alternative using causal cumsum instead of QKV projections, combined with a fullgraph-compatible Sort-and-Split MoE router. Achieves 1.41 bpb on 1xH100 in 10 minutes but is significantly slower than 8xH100 baselines.

title: Submit 1x A100 QAT Fix - 1.4078 BPB (Non-Record)
technique_details: Single-device A100 run with hyperparameter tuning for single-device scale; swaps torch.quantile for w.amax().clamp_min to avoid 30x Triton compiler performance penalty. Has unused numba/njit dependency and unimplemented FP8 path flagged by reviewer.
hardware_notes: Single A100 (non-standard; standard is 8xH100)
key_findings: Adapts multi-device hyperparameters to single-A100 scale with a notable compiler optimization fix for QAT quantile computation. Several code quality issues flagged including unused numba dependency and no-op FP8 branch.

title: Add non-record local A100 PR60-stack reproduction
technique_details: Local reproduction of PR60-style stack: 10L + FP16 tied embedding export + Muon WD + overtone init + residual mix + sliding eval; int8+zlib compression; 716 steps in 600s on single A100.
hardware_notes: Run on local 1xA100 (non-standard hardware, vs 8xH100)
key_findings: Single-GPU local reproduction of an established PR60-style technique stack; low step count (716) due to single A100 results in 1.4106 BPB, confirming that hardware parallelism is critical to competitive scores; submitted as non-record baseline.

title: [WIP][non-record] 8L/448 width branch local results
technique_details: WIP exploration of 8L/448-width vs 9L/384-depth architecture tradeoff on local 1×GPU compute. TTT noted to help modestly. Not a SOTA claim; submitted as public WIP and compute-grant reference.
hardware_notes: Local 1×GPU (not standard 8×H100)
key_findings: Width (8L/448) outperforms depth (9L/384) in local experiments, achieving 1.412 BPB (seed 42); results are preliminary single-GPU local runs not directly comparable to 8×H100 competition results.

title: Non-record: QAT + Neural Cache + LoRA TTT
technique_details: QAT with STE fake-quantization (bugged: symmetric STE vs percentile-based export causes +0.25 BPB penalty); Neural Cache with hidden-state similarity lookup and logaddexp interpolation during sliding window eval; rank-8 LoRA TTT with entropy-gated updates; 24-checkpoint SWA; built on thwu1 #1 recipe
hardware_notes: 8xH100, 5109 steps in 600s
key_findings: Documents a QAT export mismatch bug causing 0.25 BPB quantization penalty; Neural Cache and LoRA TTT implementations are validated and working, serving as a codebase for future use once the QAT bug is fixed.

title: Record: 10-Layer 4xMLP (val_bpb: 1.4444)
technique_details: 10-layer model with 4× MLP expansion. No further details provided in the PR body.
key_findings: Minimal PR testing a 4× MLP expansion on a 10-layer model, achieving val_bpb=1.4444 — well above current SOTA, suggesting the expanded MLP alone without complementary techniques (quantization, larger vocab, sliding eval) is insufficient.

title: Non-record: Value Residual (-0.015 BPB) + Gated Attention (-0.003 BPB) with ablations
technique_details: Value Residual (ResFormer, arXiv:2410.17897): caches V vectors from layer 0, mixes into all subsequent layers via 18 learnable scalar params, -0.015 BPB. Gated Attention (arXiv:2505.06708): per-head sigmoid gate after SDPA output to eliminate attention sinks, ~37K params, -0.003 BPB. Techniques stack additively for -0.017 BPB combined. PPM-C context mixer tested as negative result (+0.0018 BPB). Tested on 9L, 2x MLP, 1000 steps on 1x RTX3090.
hardware_notes: 1x RTX3090, 1000 steps, 131K batch — not a full competition run
key_findings: Controlled ablation study showing Value Residual and Gated Attention stack additively for -0.017 BPB combined improvement; PPM-C classical compression mixing is a negative result on models already using SmearGate+BigramHash.

title: [Non-Record] MLP3x + WD0.04 + OrthoInit + Sliding Eval — 1.4536 BPB
technique_details: 38+ controlled experiments on RTX 3060 at small scale. MLP 3x, weight decay 0.04 in Muon, orthogonal init, sliding window eval (stride=64). Int6 QAT, BigramHash, and zstd-22 implemented but not used in final run. SWA proven quantization-hostile (0.12 BPB degradation). Projected ~1.18-1.21 BPB at H100 scale.
hardware_notes: RTX 3060 (local, non-standard); 1000 steps × 65K batch tokens only
key_findings: Systematic ablation study documenting SWA as quantization-hostile at this scale (0.12 BPB degradation across 4 experiments), and SmearGate with dim=12 bottleneck as catastrophic. Establishes projected H100-scale BPB from local experiments.

title: [Non-record] MHALM v1 (1.4574 bpb)
technique_details: Multi-kernel language model with 5 geometric readout heads (Spherical, Gabor, Laplacian, Tucker GL, Linear) operating in 128-dim Stäckel coordinate space; ComplexSSM; 13.6M params; 10.8MB artifact; trained 594s on 8xH100.
key_findings: Exploratory architectural experiment using geometric/exotic readout heads in Stäckel coordinate space; BPB of 1.4574 is well above competitive range but represents a novel non-transformer approach.

title: Non-record: 11L + XSA4 H100 frontier (1.4612 BPB legal)
technique_details: XSA applied in final 4 layers of 11L decoder; legal checkpoint uses packed_zstd artifact; over-cap result (1.412 BPB, 17.6MB) included as supporting evidence only; checkpoint frontier selected every 25 steps
hardware_notes: Single H100, not 8xH100
key_findings: Explores XSA on final 4 layers of an 11L model on a single H100; legal result is bottlenecked by artifact size at 1.4612 BPB, while the over-cap result shows 1.4121 BPB potential.

title: experiments: MODEL_DIM=256, MLP_MULT=3, WARMDOWN fix - best bpb 1.4702
technique_details: Systematic ablation of model width (DIM=256 vs 512), depth (18 layers), and MLP multiplier (2x vs 3x). Best result: DIM=256, MLP_MULT=3, WARMDOWN=400 steps at 6.4MB. Key finding: step speed matters more than model size; MLP_MULT=3 beats default 2.
key_findings: Exploration showing MLP_MULT=3 outperforms MLP_MULT=2 and that narrower models (DIM=256) beat wider ones (DIM=512) under time budget constraints. Notes 9.6MB headroom remaining for layer tying experiments.

title: 11L AttnRes + Gated Attention + Looped Blocks + EMA + Cosine + QAT
technique_details: Block Attention Residuals (arXiv 2603.15031) replacing fixed skip_weights with learned depth routing. Per-head gated attention to prevent attention-sink pathology. Looped middle blocks (layers 4-7 repeated x2) for zero-param compute depth increase. EMA decay=0.995, cosine LR decay, QAT last 15%. 11L, 3x MLP, 26.5M params, ~13.7MB artifact. Only local M4 Max validation at 500 steps.
hardware_notes: H100 run pending; only local M4 Max validation completed (500 steps)
key_findings: Draft submission exploring learned depth routing via Block Attention Residuals and looped middle blocks for parameter-free compute scaling. BPB of 1.475 is from incomplete local validation only; full H100 results pending compute credits.

title: Non-record: BigramHash(4096) + Cosine EMA + LZMA-9
technique_details: Built on PR #549 stack. BigramHash expanded 2048→4096. Cosine EMA schedule (0.99→0.999) replacing fixed 0.997 — creates quantization gap (1.37→2.21 roundtrip) that TTT partially recovers. Earlier late QAT activation threshold 0.15→0.10. LZMA preset 6→9. Developed using ShinkaEvolve evolutionary code optimization with GPT-5.4 + Gemini 3 Pro as mutation operators.
hardware_notes: 1×H100 only (940 steps); 8×H100 evaluation pending due to budget constraints
key_findings: Cosine EMA schedule introduces a quantization gap that TTT partially recovers, suggesting fixed EMA may be preferable. Artifact is very compact at 7.9MB (49% of budget), leaving significant room for model expansion.

title: First submission
technique_details: MLP_MULT increased from 2 to 4, ROPE_BASE raised from 10k to 100k, reduced batch tokens from 196608 to 98304, lowered LR from 0.04 to 0.035, shortened warmdown from 800 to 600 iters.
hardware_notes: Single GPU with 20GB VRAM, not 8xH100
key_findings: Baseline exploration combining MLP 4x expansion and extended RoPE base on a small single GPU, achieving 1.4784 BPB. Not competitive with leaderboard but documents parameter interactions at constrained compute.

title: Add TRN hybrid non-record submission (1.4942 bpb, 1x RTX 5090)
technique_details: 10-layer hybrid: 7 TRN (oscillatory recurrence via Kogge-Stone parallel prefix scan over complex-valued oscillators) + 3 attention layers. Int5 QAT, zstd-22. Pure PyTorch, no Triton/custom CUDA. Oscillator projection weights (d_model→6K encoding frequency/phase) suffer O(t) phase drift under int5 quantization, causing catastrophic degradation from 1.26 fp32 BPB to 1.93 post-quant BPB at 20K steps.
hardware_notes: 1x RTX 5090, 636 steps in 600s; no 8xH100 results
key_findings: Novel oscillatory recurrence hybrid architecture (TRN layers using Kogge-Stone prefix scan over complex oscillators) reaches 1.26 BPB in fp32 but collapses to 1.49 BPB post-int5 quantization due to phase drift in oscillator weights, a failure mode absent in standard transformers.

title: Non-record: Basis Block Interpolation (novel negative result) + Hyperparameter Sweep (MATRIX_LR=0.03 improves SOTA by 0.059 bpb)
technique_details: 5 basis blocks × 3 unrolls = 15 effective layers at dim=576; block reuse bottlenecked by torch.compile(fullgraph=False) speed penalty, documented as negative result. Systematic hyperparameter sweep of 15+ experiments identifying MATRIX_LR=0.03 as +0.059 BPB improvement over default 0.02.
hardware_notes: 1xH100 SXM
key_findings: Basis Block Interpolation (block reuse as depth expansion) is a documented negative result due to compile overhead; the hyperparameter sweep finding that MATRIX_LR=0.03 beats 0.02 by 0.059 BPB is the more actionable contribution.

title: Non-record: Family 1A tied blocks (1xH100 dev snapshot)
technique_details: Tied transformer block weights with grad clip 1.0, 30-step data warmup, Muon + AdamW optimizer combo; int8+zlib compression to 2.03MB; stopped early at step 1258/20000 due to wallclock cap.
hardware_notes: 1xH100, 600s wallclock cap; not the official 8xH100 record track
key_findings: Reproducible snapshot of tied transformer block weights on 1xH100; non-record submission documenting a stable training recipe that reportedly hits ~1.507 BPB on another seed locally.

title: Non-record: 1xH100 auto-research int6 policy sweep
technique_details: Export-time mixed quantization policy sweep via INT6_CATS knob on a single 1xH100 under 10-minute budget. Structured negative-result submission showing aggressive int6 export policies on short 1x runs create large quantization gaps.
hardware_notes: 1xH100 (non-standard)
key_findings: Negative result showing that aggressive int6 export policies on short 1xH100 runs preserve size but create a large quantization gap (val_bpb=1.5248), useful for pruning dead-end search directions before expensive 8x attempts.

title: Non-record: TrigramHash — iso-parametric bigram(96)+trigram(32), val_bpb=1.5275 (1xH100)
technique_details: Iso-parametric split of BigramHash(10240, dim=128) into BigramHash(10240, dim=96) + TrigramHash(10240, dim=32) with identical total params. Hash function uses three prime multipliers (36313, 27191, 18731) for orthogonal bit patterns per token position. Tested on 1xH100 with ~8x fewer steps than standard run.
hardware_notes: 1xH100 (non-standard; competition standard is 8xH100)
key_findings: Explores whether 3-token co-occurrence (TrigramHash) adds signal beyond BigramHash in an iso-parametric budget split; result of 1.5275 is not directly comparable to SOTA due to reduced compute on 1xH100.

title: RQZ-Golf v1: Depth recurrence for parameter efficiency
technique_details: 7 unique layers plus 1 recurrent layer applied K=3 times with learned per-pass iteration embeddings (psi_k) and residual scaling by 1/sqrt(K). Preliminary result from 1 shard on 1xA100; full benchmark pending compute.
hardware_notes: Preliminary run on 1xA100 (single shard); full 8xH100 evaluation not yet done.
key_findings: Explores depth recurrence inspired by Universal Transformers, saving ~20% parameters while increasing effective depth; also proposes increasing K at inference for test-time compute scaling without changing model size.

title: Add non-record 1x5090 autoresearch submission with two-campaign analysis
technique_details: Git-native autoresearch loop on single RTX 5090. 188 measured runs across two campaigns moving from 1.734 to 1.535 BPB. Key winning techniques: low-rank q_proj on most blocks, short-to-full context warmup, disabling attention fake quant during training, MLP-only int6 export, wider tail MLPs, fp16 tied embeddings on final block. Recurring losers: blunt d_model increases, removing recurrence, global mlp_mult=3, seq_len changes.
hardware_notes: Single RTX 5090 (non-standard); wallclock 427.9s
key_findings: Automated git-loop architecture search across 188 runs on RTX 5090 reduced BPB by 0.205 (1.734→1.529). Key insight: under tight wallclock budget, targeted compute allocation (low-rank q_proj, delayed fake quant, narrow precision spends) outperforms making every component richer.

title: Non-record: TernaryRecurrentGPT - ternary 1.58-bit MLP + depth recurrence (1xL4 val_bpb=1.5348)
technique_details: 7 unique layers looped twice for 14 effective depth with learnable loop gates initialized at 1.0. MLP weights use 1.58-bit ternary STE QAT enabled at 85% of training. FP16 tied embeddings. Validated only on 1×L4 at reduced batch (32k tokens, seq=512).
hardware_notes: Validated on 1×L4 GPU with reduced batch size (32k tokens, seq=512); full 8×H100 run pending compute grant.
key_findings: Explores depth recurrence with ternary (1.58-bit) MLP weights for extreme compression, achieving 12.4MB artifact but only validated locally at reduced scale with a val_bpb of 1.5348 that is not competition-comparable.

title: Non-record: TTT + QAT on Consumer GPU (val_bpb=1.5382)
technique_details: QAT uses fake int8 quantization with straight-through estimator in CastedLinear.forward(); TTT performs 1 SGD step (LR=1e-4) on both attn.proj.weight and mlp.proj.weight per eval batch. Developed via AI-assisted autonomous experimentation (Claude agent, 15 experiments). Architecture: 9L, dim=512, 8 heads, 4 KV heads, MLP 2x, tied embeddings.
hardware_notes: 1x RTX 5070 Ti (12GB VRAM) — consumer GPU, not 8xH100
key_findings: Demonstrates that QAT (fake int8 noise during training) can speed up training (362ms vs 399ms/step) while improving post-quantization quality; combined with TTT achieves 1.5382 BPB on a consumer GPU, developed entirely via AI-assisted autonomous experimentation.

title: Non-record: Mac mini M4 16GB, no H100s, still golfing (val_bpb=1.5672)
technique_details: Apple Silicon MLX submission titled 'Late Projection EMA Finalizer'. Int8+zlib compression (15,888,695 bytes), total artifact 15,962,372 bytes.
hardware_notes: Mac mini M4 16GB — Apple Silicon, not 8xH100
key_findings: Hardware-specific Apple Silicon non-record entry demonstrating competition-faithful submission pipeline on consumer hardware using MLX framework, achieving 1.5672 BPB.

title: Non-record: 1x RTX 3090 baseline run (sp1024, 1 shard)
technique_details: Baseline SP-1024 run on single RTX 3090, 1 training shard. Final int8+zlib roundtrip BPB reported.
hardware_notes: 1x RTX 3090 on RunPod, not 8xH100
key_findings: Documented baseline run on consumer hardware (RTX 3090) with 1 shard, achieving 1.5729 BPB post-quantization. Serves as a hardware portability reference, not a leaderboard attempt.

title: submission: QK Gain Init 1.2 + Sliding Window Eval (stride=64)
technique_details: QK_GAIN_INIT lowered from 1.5 to 1.2 for better attention stability; sliding window eval with EVAL_STRIDE=64, EVAL_BATCH_SEQS=32 giving ~0.03 BPB free gain. Local results on RTX 4090 at ~340 steps; awaiting H100 CI numbers.
hardware_notes: Local results on RTX 4090 (~340 steps); H100 CI results pending
key_findings: Combines QK gain initialization tuning with sliding-window evaluation for a claimed ~0.03 BPB free gain; local RTX 4090 results show sliding window alone improves from 1.6133 to 1.5879 BPB with no training changes.

title: Depth recurrence: 3 unique layers x 3 loops, 1.589 BPB
technique_details: 3 unique transformer blocks looped 3 times (effective depth 9). Freed parameter budget invested in wider layers (d1024). Sweeps across widths 512-1152, layers 2-6, recurrence counts 2-6, head counts 4-16. QAT planned to resolve 16MB compression constraint. Run on MLX.
hardware_notes: Apple Silicon MLX (local runs, not 8xH100)
key_findings: 3 unique layers x 3 recurrences is optimal shape for depth recurrence; best config (d1024, h8) achieves 1.589 BPB but currently exceeds 16MB limit at 20.4MB compressed — QAT needed to fit within budget.

title: Non-record submission: recurrent 512 L3 6k (8x H100, 224s)
technique_details: Recurrent shared-loop transformer with MODEL_DIM=512, 3 layers, 6000 iterations. Post-quant BPB 1.6004, pre-quant 1.5890. Runtime ~224s on 8xH100.
hardware_notes: 8xH100, only used 224s of 600s budget
key_findings: Simple non-record baseline using a recurrent shared-loop transformer (weight-tied layers) achieving 1.6004 BPB post-int8-quantization; demonstrates stable convergence in a compact 3-layer recurrent architecture well within time budget.

title: Non-record: local RTX 4070 SP1024 8x512 KV4 seq768 500-step run
technique_details: 8-layer 512-dim model with 4 KV heads (GQA), seq len 768, 500 steps, SP1024 tokenizer. Throughput-oriented search path. Local workstation run using first published train shard and full validation split.
hardware_notes: 1x RTX 4070 Laptop GPU (vs standard 8xH100); only 500 steps completed
key_findings: Local single-GPU non-record submission using seq_len=768 on an RTX 4070 laptop, achieving 1.611 BPB after 500 steps via a throughput-oriented configuration search. Companion to PR #248 exploring different sequence length.

title: Radial bitnet submission
technique_details: FRO (Fractal Resonant Optimization) custom optimizer; Radial Token Branch for analytic token-level geometric features; 1024-bucket bigram hash branch; BitNet-style ternary-weight behavior in major projections; mixed int8/int6 post-training export with light export-time pruning; 18.47M params, seq_len=1024, batch=4.
hardware_notes: Validated on Kaggle Dual T4 only; not reproduced on official 8xH100 SXM
key_findings: Experimental submission combining a custom FRO optimizer, radial token geometric features, bigram hash branch, and BitNet ternary weights, achieving 1.613 BPB on Kaggle Dual T4; significantly above current SOTA and not yet validated on official 8xH100 hardware.

title: Non-record: local RTX 4070 SP1024 8x512 KV4 500-step run
technique_details: 8-layer 512-dim model with 4 KV heads (GQA), 500 steps, SP1024 tokenizer. Local workstation run using first published train shard and full validation split.
hardware_notes: 1x RTX 4070 Laptop GPU (vs standard 8xH100); only 500 steps completed
key_findings: Local single-GPU non-record submission documenting a compact 8-layer 512-dim run on an RTX 4070 laptop, achieving 1.623 BPB after 500 steps. Intended as a reference for local development rather than a competitive score.

title: 11L INT6 + Legal Score-First LoRA TTT (rank 8, Adam, 3ep per 32K chunk)
technique_details: Score-first LoRA TTT: chunk is scored in inference_mode before any weight update. LoRA rank 8 on Q+V projections with Adam lr=0.01, 3 epochs per 32K chunk. Claims ~24x improvement over full-param SGD TTT. 11L 512d, 8H/4KV, LeakyReLU(0.5)^2 MLP 3x, BigramHash(4096), EMA(0.997), INT6 QAT + zlib. BPB estimated from RTX 5090 run, not official H100.
hardware_notes: Preliminary results on RTX 5090; official 8xH100 evaluation pending
key_findings: Replaces full-parameter SGD TTT with LoRA (rank 8) on Q+V projections, showing ~24x greater BPB improvement over SGD in a score-first legal TTT framework. Estimated ~1.63 BPB after TTT vs 1.714 base, but unvalidated on official hardware.

title: Non-record: local RTX 4070 SP1024 7x512 KV4 500-step run
technique_details: 7-layer, dim=512, 4 KV heads configuration run for ~500 steps on a local laptop GPU. Non-record track submission under 16MB artifact cap. Training on first published train shard, eval on full published validation split.
hardware_notes: 1x RTX 4070 Laptop GPU — not 8xH100
key_findings: Non-record local workstation submission documenting a compact 7-layer KV4 configuration found through local search, achieving 1.65718 BPB in 500 steps on a consumer laptop GPU.

title: Non-record: local RTX 4070 shared-depth RMS interface v0
technique_details: Shared-depth RMS-interface variant with 8 logical layers mapped to 4 physical blocks on a single RTX 4070. Explicitly not intended for the official 8xH100 record track. Post-quant val_bpb=1.65765109 on full published validation split.
hardware_notes: 1x RTX 4070 (local workstation); explicitly not run on 8xH100
key_findings: Local workstation run demonstrating shared-depth RMS-interface architecture (8 logical layers, 4 physical blocks) under the 16MB artifact cap on consumer hardware. Scores 1.658 BPB, expected given single-GPU local constraint rather than 8xH100.

title: Non-record: local RTX 4070 SP1024 7x512 KV2 500-step run
technique_details: 7x512 architecture with KV2 (2 KV heads), sequence length 1024, run for 500 steps on a local RTX 4070 Laptop GPU. Non-standard hardware and configuration; stored under non-record track.
hardware_notes: 1x RTX 4070 Laptop GPU (non-standard)
key_findings: Local workstation non-record submission demonstrating a compact 7-layer 512-dim model on consumer hardware, achieving 1.666 BPB post-quant under the 16MB artifact cap on a single RTX 4070 Laptop.

title: non-record: LR warmdown on 1x A40 (1.723 bpb, 8.40MB)
technique_details: Baseline architecture with schedule tuning only: WARMDOWN_ITERS=3600, MATRIX_LR=0.06; int8+zlib compression; no architectural changes.
hardware_notes: 1x A40; standard is 8xH100.
key_findings: Demonstrates that schedule tuning alone (warmdown and LR adjustment) improves over a local MLX baseline by ~0.15 BPB; well below competitive range but useful as a reproducible minimal baseline on consumer hardware.

title: Non-record: BitNet b1.58 + depth recurrence + NorMuon (1.7510 BPB, 3.78 MB)
technique_details: Ternary (2-bit) weight packing {-1,0,+1} 4-per-byte + zlib yielding 3.74MB; 4 unique transformer blocks run 3x each = 12 effective layers with U-Net skip connections; learnable per-block resid_mix for depth specialization; NorMuon (per-neuron row-wise RMS normalization post Newton-Schulz); sequence length warmup 128→1024 over 2000 steps with YaRN NTK-aware RoPE base scaling. Pre-quant: 1.4866, post-quant: 1.7510 (Δ+0.264 BPB gap).
hardware_notes: 1x RTX 3060, ~3 hours training (unlimited compute track)
key_findings: Explores BitNet b1.58 ternary quantization combined with depth recurrence and U-Net skips to fit ~60M parameter models in 3.78MB; large quantization gap (+0.264 BPB) indicates QAT insufficient to push weights toward ternary values.

title: Non-record: PR315 repro on 1xH100 PCIe, int6+zstd (val_bpb=1.8338)
technique_details: Reproduction of PR #315 recipe (XSA+EMA+PartialRoPE+LateQAT) adapted for 1xH100 PCIe with Flash Attention 2 instead of FA3. Training schedule adjusted: WARMDOWN_ITERS=400, MOMENTUM_WARMUP=200. Only ~492 steps possible vs ~6200 on 8xH100; QAT had ~40 steps of adaptation.
hardware_notes: 1xH100 PCIe instead of 8xH100; Flash Attention 2 instead of FA3
key_findings: Demonstrates that the PR #315 technique stack can be reproduced on a single H100 PCIe within 10 minutes, though severe compute reduction (~492 vs ~6200 steps) results in much weaker BPB of 1.8338; useful as a single-GPU reproducibility reference.

title: Add 10L 4K long-context negative-result submission
technique_details: 10-layer model with 4K context window. Post-quant TTT val_bpb=1.8389; post-quant sliding val_bpb=2.50893917. 4K context reduced training token coverage to ~4.01B tokens within the 10-minute budget, substantially worse than public baselines.
hardware_notes: Single 8xH100 attempt due to credit constraints
key_findings: Negative result showing that a 4K context window reduces training token coverage too severely under the 10-minute budget, yielding substantially worse BPB than public baselines; documents the coverage-context tradeoff.

title: Add Deep14x416 KV2 non-record MLX submission (val_bpb=1.8440)
technique_details: 14-layer transformer at width 416 with 2 KV heads (aggressive KV sharing); int8+zlib quantization yielding 12.3MB artifact; trained for 750 steps on 10-shard FineWeb subset; uses larger validation batch and logit chunking for efficiency on Apple Silicon.
hardware_notes: Apple M5 Max (MLX), not 8xH100; 750 steps on 10-shard FineWeb subset
key_findings: Explores depth-vs-width trade-off on Apple Silicon: 14 layers at width 416 with aggressive KV sharing. Non-record submission documenting MLX reproducibility with a straightforward parameter-budget reallocation.

title: [WIP] SSM LRU Baseline — First State Space Model Submission
technique_details: Linear Recurrent Unit (LRU) state space model with complex diagonal recurrence and parallel scan (cumsum trick), gated projection, ReLU^2 MLP. MuonAdamW optimizer with SSM-aware parameter groups. Plans for int8+zlib quantization pipeline.
hardware_notes: Current results on RTX 3090 (5 min budget); requesting H100 compute credits for validation
key_findings: First non-transformer (SSM/LRU) submission to the competition; SSM blocks are 36% smaller than attention blocks enabling more layers, but current BPB of 1.848 is bottlenecked by pure PyTorch scan speed at 2.8% MFU.

title: Non-record: DART - Differential Attention Recurrent Transformer (Student submission, Kerala)
technique_details: Shared-weight recurrent transformer combining Differential Attention V2 with per-loop specialisation via low-rank Q deltas, memory tokens, deep supervision, and U-Net skip connections. 3.92M params compressed to 3.55MB with int8+zlib.
hardware_notes: Google Colab T4 free tier — not 8xH100
key_findings: Student submission exploring a recurrent shared-weight architecture with Differential Attention V2 and low-rank per-loop specialization; uses only 22.5% of the 16MB budget at 3.55MB.

title: Non-record: Skill Forge — Autonomous ML Experimentation System (Local RTX 4070)
technique_details: Infrastructure/methodology submission: autonomous skill-based experimentation loop inspired by Karpathy autoresearch. Local validation only on RTX 4070 Laptop with 7L/512d model at seq_len=512. Compute grant pending for 8xH100 runs.
hardware_notes: RTX 4070 Laptop (8GB VRAM), local validation only — not competition-comparable
key_findings: Autonomous ML experimentation system where domain skills encode knowledge from 21 leaderboard submissions and 13+ arXiv papers; each experiment proposes hypotheses and tests them within the 10-minute competition budget.

title: Non-record: MLX-Optimized 12L 416d with SmearGate + BigramHash (val_bpb=1.9011, Mac)
technique_details: 6-encoder + 6-decoder U-Net architecture with SmearGate, BigramHash(4096), FP16 embeddings, MLP 3x with relu^2 activation, GQA (8H/4KV). Trained on Apple Silicon M4 Pro using MLX framework for only 500 iterations (~16M tokens), severely undertrained.
hardware_notes: Apple Silicon M4 Pro (24GB unified memory), MLX framework — not 8xH100
key_findings: Non-record exploration of MLX-based training on Apple Silicon; model is severely undertrained at 500 iterations and the author estimates 1.5-1.6 BPB is achievable with full H100 training.

title: Add Aria local sweep tooling and non-record 16MB runs
technique_details: 8 layers, 576 dim, 8 heads, 4 KV heads (GQA), ROPE_BASE=50000. Int8+zlib export at 14.2MB. Local single-GPU sweep tooling added. Two non-record submissions: base config and rope50k variant.
hardware_notes: Single GPU local run (nproc_per_node=1), non-standard hardware
key_findings: Local sweep tooling for fast iteration on 8x576 KV4 architecture; rope50k variant achieves 1.904 BPB at 14.2MB compressed on single-GPU non-record run.

title: Add 1.9105 BPB submission (M3 Pro Optimized)
technique_details: Submission optimized for Apple M3 Pro hardware rather than standard 8xH100.
hardware_notes: M3 Pro (non-standard hardware, deviates from 8xH100 requirement)
key_findings: Non-competitive submission trained on Apple M3 Pro rather than the standard 8xH100 setup, achieving 1.9105 BPB; included for reproducibility on consumer hardware.

title: Autoresearch over a Collective + Distributed Candidate Pool — M4 Air, val_bpb=1.9263 (non-record)
technique_details: 8 logical layers with 4 unique blocks via mirrored recurrence (U-Net skip connections, encoder forward + decoder reverse), 512d, SwiGLU MLP. Muon optimizer with momentum=0.90. Reduced batch size (8K tokens) for more steps on Apple Silicon. Evolutionary bandit autoresearch loop with periodic candidate pool updates from other PRs. Run on MLX (Apple Silicon), not H100.
hardware_notes: Run on M4 Mac Air (16GB), not 8xH100. Single-process MLX training to fit 16GB memory constraint. ~14 hours of autoresearch over 84 experiments.
key_findings: Proposes a distributed autoresearch paradigm using an evolutionary bandit over a collective candidate pool, achieving 1.9263 bpb on M4 Air (16GB) — the best reported score on that hardware — via 84 experiments combining SwiGLU, mirrored recurrence, and Mac-specific batch size tuning.

title: Non-record: MLX prototyping harness with validated technique stack (val_bpb=1.9588, Mac)
technique_details: 14L×416d with KV2 (GQA), MLP 3x, int6 per-row quantization + FP16 tied embedding passthrough achieving near-zero quant gap (0.001 BPB), sliding window eval stride-64, Muon decoupled WD=0.02, Overtone spectral embedding init (SVD power-law), phase-transition resid_mix init. DenseFormer DWA tested and showed +0.003 regression. Apple Silicon only, H100 validation pending.
hardware_notes: Apple Silicon (Mac MLX) only; H100 validation pending compute grant
key_findings: FP16 tied embedding passthrough combined with Muon weight decay achieves near-zero quantization gap (0.001 BPB post vs pre-quant) on Apple Silicon; depth recurrence with int6 caused a +0.61 BPB quantization gap due to shared weight error compounding.

title: Add test submission: 2026-03-23_naive_solution
technique_details: Unmodified baseline train_gpt.py run for only 100 steps on a single GPU with int8+zlib compression. Explicitly a format/process test, not a competitive entry.
hardware_notes: Single GPU (non-standard; standard is 8xH100)
key_findings: Non-competitive test submission verifying the submission format and process using the unmodified baseline for 100 steps; BPB of 1.9672 reflects minimal training.

title: [WIP] Compression-aware fixed-step research
technique_details: Compression-aware training with multiple regularization knobs (COMPRESSION_REG_WEIGHT, COMPRESSION_GRID_REG_WEIGHT, COMPRESSION_SCALE_REG_WEIGHT, COMPRESSION_RANK1_REG_WEIGHT, TERNARY_REG_WEIGHT, OUTLIER_REG_WEIGHT). Fixed-step exact roundtrip evaluation methodology. Best local result: 14 layers, 576 dim, 8 heads, 4 KV heads, with COMPRESSION_REG_WEIGHT=0.005 and COMPRESSION_GRID_REG_WEIGHT=0.10 at 15,222,128 bytes. Iso-byte dense frontier sweep shows near-cap dense models dominate micro-optimizations.
hardware_notes: All experiments on 1x RTX 3090 under Windows; not directly comparable to 8xH100 challenge runs.
key_findings: Systematic local research showing compression-aware training and near-cap dense scaling (14 layers, 576 dim) yields best local BPB of 1.998; key finding is that optimizing for post-roundtrip artifact quality and spending more of the byte budget on a dense model outperforms most architectural micro-optimizations.

title: Non-record: Shared-Core 8/12 @ 896 on 1xH100 SXM (1.9998 val_bpb)
technique_details: Shared-core recurrent transformer architecture at dim 896; int8+zlib compression; 12,960,324 bytes artifact; two runs showing highly consistent results (1.99976 and 1.99971 BPB)
hardware_notes: 1x H100 SXM (non-standard, single GPU instead of 8xH100)
key_findings: Non-record submission using a shared-core recurrent transformer validated only on 1xH100, achieving ~2.0 BPB; demonstrates high reproducibility across two runs but is far from SOTA, likely due to single-GPU training constraints.

title: [WIP] Depth-recurrent QAT: 3x4 loops, 768d, 15.6MB artifact
technique_details: 3 shared transformer blocks looped 4x (12 effective layers), dim 768, 12 heads, 6 KV heads. Per-loop rank-4 LoRA adapters on Q/V for specialization. QAT via STE fake-quantize in CastedLinear. LAWA weight averaging during warmdown. fp16 tied embedding export. Validated only on A10G for 200 steps/1 shard; post-quant BPB 2.0485.
hardware_notes: Validated on A10G (200 iterations, 1 shard); full 8xH100 run pending compute grant.
key_findings: Depth-recurrent QAT model achieving 2.0485 post-quant BPB on limited A10G validation; notably QAT yields better post-quant score than pre-quant (2.0799 vs 2.0485), but full H100 multi-seed validation is pending.

title: Add mirror recurrence non-record submission
technique_details: Mirrored recurrent depth: 9 unique transformer blocks reused across 18 logical layers; encoder uses blocks 0..8, decoder reuses them in reverse (8..0). Int8+zlib compression to stay under 16MB. A bot flagged a bug where blocks beyond UNIQUE_LAYERS//2 could be allocated but never used.
hardware_notes: Apple M5 Max, deviates from standard 8xH100
key_findings: Explores mirrored depth recurrence with shared weights across 18 layers using only 9 unique blocks on Apple Silicon. Superseded by PR #56 which achieved 1.8440 BPB on the same hardware.

title: Experiment#1 : Deeper compact
technique_details: Local Apple Silicon MLX experiments with capped validation (VAL_MAX_BATCHES). Best result: MLP_MULT=1, NUM_LAYERS=11, 300 iterations — deeper compact model beats baseline (MLP_MULT=2, NUM_LAYERS=9) in BPB while being smaller. Embedding factorization and magnitude pruning explored but both found ineffective.
hardware_notes: Apple Silicon (local MLX); capped validation used, not full leaderboard evaluation.
