key_findings: Finds that reducing MLP multiplier to 1 and increasing depth to 11 layers beats the MLP_MULT=2 9-layer baseline in BPB while using fewer parameters; 12 layers was worse than 11, and reducing KV heads hurt quality.

title: Non-record: 10L MLP3x int6 baseline (MLX smoke test)
technique_details: 10L, 512 dim, 8 heads (4 KV GQA), 3x MLP (hidden=1536), relu² activation, U-Net skip connections, tied embeddings, int6 per-row quantization, fp16 tied embeddings, zlib-9. Only 200 iterations on Apple Silicon MLX — pipeline validation only. Planned: zstd-22, sliding window eval, Muon WD=0.04, SmearGate, BigramHash, SWA, 11th layer.
hardware_notes: Apple Silicon MLX, 200 iterations only — not a competition run; full H100 runs pending compute grant
key_findings: Pipeline smoke test on Apple Silicon MLX confirming end-to-end int6 training works; BPB of 2.3517 at 200 steps is not competitive but validates the full compression and eval pipeline before H100 runs.

title: WIP: Add adaptive eval-time context non-record MLX submission
technique_details: 9x512 model with KV4 (4 KV heads) and tied embeddings. Adaptive eval does a coarse pass (stride=256) then rescores harder windows with finer stride (stride=64, hard_fraction=0.25). Local Apple Silicon run, not a leaderboard claim.
hardware_notes: Apple M4 Pro, 48 GB unified memory; local MLX run, not 8xH100.
key_findings: WIP non-record submission demonstrating adaptive eval-time context rescoring improves BPB by ~0.010 on a local proxy, at the cost of slower evaluation; submitted for exploration rather than leaderboard competition.

title: Minimal recurrent motif (sb1 rs2 g0.18) – non-record submission
technique_details: Single shared block (shared_block_size=1) with 2 recurrence steps and soft gating (gate_init=0.18). Emphasizes structural reuse over explicit depth to achieve compact parameter footprint (~8.47MB raw, ~1.92MB compressed).
hardware_notes: Validated on local hardware
key_findings: Explores minimal recurrent motif with a single shared block and 2 recurrence steps, achieving 2.7873 BPB — well above competitive scores but notable for extreme parameter efficiency (~1.92MB artifact). Submitted as a duplicate of PR #320 (non-draft version).

title: Minimal recurrent motif (sb1 rs2 g0.18) – non-record submission
technique_details: Single shared block (shared_block_size=1) with 2 recurrence steps and soft gating (gate_init=0.18). Emphasizes structural reuse over explicit depth. Draft version of PR #323.
hardware_notes: Validated on local hardware
key_findings: Draft version of PR #323; same minimal recurrent motif architecture achieving 2.7873 BPB with ~1.92MB compressed artifact. Represents an early structural exploration of weight-shared recurrence under Parameter Golf constraints.

title: [wip] depth-recurrent QAT: 3x3 shared blocks + LoRA
technique_details: 3 shared transformer blocks looped 3x with per-iteration rank-4 LoRA deltas on Q/V (9 effective layers). QAT via STE fake-quantize in CastedLinear, reducing post-quant degradation 18x (0.00012 vs 0.00217). Byte-grouped export separating int8 weights from fp16 scales for ~2x better compression. Model widened from 512d to 768d using freed parameter budget. LAWA during warmdown. RoPE NTK scaling for 2048-token eval context (trained at 1024). Local MLX validation only; H100 runs pending.
hardware_notes: Local MLX validation only (200 iterations, 1 shard); full 8xH100 multi-seed runs pending compute grant.
key_findings: Depth-recurrent QAT with LoRA specialization achieves 2.962 BPB in local MLX validation at 4.12MB artifact; QAT dramatically reduces post-quantization degradation and byte-grouped export improves compression ratio by ~2x.

title: Add non-record 1xH100 auto precision budget experiment
technique_details: Calibration-driven precision allocator: after training, SWA, and pruning, greedily promotes individual tensors to higher precision where quantization appears most harmful, subject to a 16MB byte budget. Run was only 150 iterations on 1xH100 so score is not meaningful.
hardware_notes: 1xH100 (non-standard), only 150 iterations with 60s wallclock — exploratory only
key_findings: Exploratory submission proposing a sensitivity-driven automatic precision allocator as an alternative to hand-tuned mixed precision. The calibration run is too short to yield competitive results (3.08 BPB) but documents the direction.

title: PhiDelay Shared-Recurrent LM (3.23 bpb, 6.84MB)
technique_details: Replaces self-attention with a lightweight multi-head delay mixer (PhiDelayLayer) inspired by PhiDelayNet for time-series forecasting. 3 physical blocks repeated 4x (12 effective layers), dim 1024, learned per-block recurrence gates, relu-squared MLP with mult=2, 1024-token SentencePiece vocab. Artifact only 6.84MB (43% of budget). Int8+zlib roundtrip is lossless (0.0000 BPB degradation).
hardware_notes: Training hardware not specified; likely not standard 8xH100.
key_findings: Novel PhiDelayLayer replaces self-attention with a gated multi-head delay mixer adapted from time-series forecasting; achieves 3.2306 BPB at only 6.84MB, with recurrent weight sharing outperforming BitLinear quantization (3.23 vs 6.09 BPB).

title: Add RIOM v1 shared-depth recurrence non-record submission
technique_details: Replaces 9 distinct transformer blocks with 3 shared blocks repeated 3 times, with lightweight learned recurrence gates. Run on Apple Silicon MLX for only 50 steps; not an 8xH100 submission. Post-quant val_bpb=3.24862, total artifact 2.32MB.
hardware_notes: Apple Silicon MLX development run, not 8xH100. Only 50 training steps.
key_findings: Explores shared-depth recurrence (parameter sharing across repeated blocks) as a way to improve effective depth per byte; results are very preliminary (50 steps, local hardware) but artifact size is dramatically smaller than standard submissions.

title: [codex] add gw kv2 int7 non-record submission
technique_details: 9 layers, 512 dim, 8 query heads with 2 KV heads (GQA), MLP_HIDDEN=1248. Int8+zlib export with INT8_KEEP_FLOAT_MAX_NUMEL=65535 to force quantization of smaller KV matrices. Post-export INT4 coarsening on middle layers 4,5 with INT4_STEP=2. Run on Apple Silicon MLX proxy.
hardware_notes: Apple Silicon (MLX proxy run, no valid 8xH100 timed run)
key_findings: Reducing KV heads to 2 and reinvesting budget into wider MLP (1248) beats KV4/MLP_HIDDEN=1120 on local proxy; lowering float-passthrough threshold was key to making smaller KV matrices actually quantize rather than serialize as fp16.

title: Non-record: 1x RTX 4090 compat smoke (50 steps)
technique_details: 50-step smoke run on RTX 4090 via Vast.ai; manual KV head expansion before SDPA to work around PyTorch build lacking `enable_gqa=` parameter; int8+zlib artifact of 5.17MB.
hardware_notes: 1x RTX 4090 on Vast.ai; PyTorch build without enable_gqa= support required manual KV head expansion
key_findings: Hardware compatibility smoke test (50 steps only) documenting a workaround for a PyTorch build lacking GQA support on RTX 4090; not intended for main leaderboard.

title: Non-record: O(n) field LM, 2L FP16 (no attention)
technique_details: Field-based language model with linear-time O(n) scoring, no attention mechanism and no transformer weights. FP16. Exploration of alternative architectures rather than benchmark performance.
key_findings: Purely exploratory submission investigating a field-based LM without attention; achieves val_bpb ~21.42 which is far from competitive, but demonstrates a structurally novel O(n) approach.

title: GolfStudent v2 14L: d=352, Value Residuals, GPTQ-lite, Schedule-Free, Muon+EMA
technique_details: d=352, 14-layer hybrid (10x GatedMLP + 4x Attention every 3rd layer), vocab=1024, weight-tied embeddings. Value Residuals use learned scalar skip gates (tanh-gated, init=0) every 3 blocks. GPTQ-lite uses 5 clip percentile candidates per row minimizing reconstruction MSE. Schedule-free final 120s with constant LR floor (10%) + faster EMA (decay=0.97). Muon with momentum warmup 0.85→0.99 over 1500 steps. Per-row INT8 + zlib level=9 compression to ~15.06MB.
key_findings: Hybrid GatedMLP/Attention architecture with Value Residuals and GPTQ-lite quantization achieving ~94% of the 16MB budget. No BPB score reported; submission appears to be in-progress without final evaluation results.

title: Record: 11L VR + GA + LeakyReLU² + Legal Score-First TTT (val_bpb=pending)
technique_details: Value Residual (learned lambda blending first-block V into subsequent blocks), Gated Attention (per-head sigmoid gate on attention output), LeakyReLU(0.5)² replacing relu² in MLP. Legal score-first TTT: AdamW lr=1e-4, 3 epochs, cosine LR across 131K-token chunks, last 2 blocks + norms unfrozen. Initial submission used non-compliant pre-eval TTT; rerun pending.
key_findings: Combines Value Residual, Gated Attention, and LeakyReLU² on the standard 11L stack with legal score-first TTT; BPB is pending rerun after correcting non-compliant pre-eval TTT from initial submission.

title: [track_10min_16mb] 50-Epoch Cosine LoRA TTT + SOTA (10L Int5/Int6 BigramHash SWA) — Atharva Date (ADIITJ)
technique_details: 50-epoch cosine LoRA TTT at evaluation time: lr = 0.001 × 0.5 × (1 + cos(π × ep / 50)). Rank-8 LoRA adapters on Q and V projections in all 10 attention layers. Score-first per chunk within each epoch. NLL accumulated from final epoch only. LoRA + optimizer reset between documents. Bottom layers not frozen (unlike some other TTT submissions). Reviewer flagged potential BOS token scoring inconsistency that may artificially improve reported bpb.
hardware_notes: Training on 8xH100; evaluation TTT not yet run on H100.

title: Non-record: Phase 1 Legal Score-First TTT + Meta-TTT (FOMAML) — awaiting compute

Legal score-first TTT protocol. Phase 2 plans Meta-TTT via FOMAML inner loops during training. U-Net gated skips, Star-ReLU activation, BigramHash(8192, 128d), Partial RoPE (16/64 dims), Int6 QAT, zstd-22.

title: Non-record: AETHER block-sparse attention with Lyapunov-stable governor (12L)
technique_details: Block-sparse SDPA with Cauchy-Schwarz upper bound pruning of KV blocks, top-k selection, and dense fallback for short sequences. Lyapunov-stable PD controller (geometric governor) adaptively adjusts sparsity. Chebyshev GC guard bounds false reclamation. 12 layers, MLP 2.5x, 4% magnitude pruning. Preserves SOTA stack: int5/int6 QAT, BigramHash(10240), SmearGate, SWA, Muon WD=0.04. Lean 4 verified proofs included.

title: Value Residual + DenseFormer DWA + TTT

DenseFormer DWA replaces U-Net skip connections with full depth-weighted averaging over all previous layer outputs (~65 scalar params for 10 layers). TTT uses AdamW fine-tuning on already-scored validation tokens during sliding window eval (score-then-train). Built on top of thwu1's 10L_Int5MLP_MuonWD04_SWA50 baseline.
hardware_notes: Pending 8xH100 run; no results yet
key_findings: Combines Value Residual Learning, DenseFormer depth-weighted averaging, and AdamW TTT on top of the current #1 submission baseline, but lacks run logs and results pending compute availability.

Muon with weight decay for matrix params, AdamW for embeddings. Pre-quant val_bpb=1.2156 on 8xH100; post-quant artifact pending due to budget/pipeline issues.

title: Complexity MoE + PID Dynamics (Token-Routed I64)
technique_details: Token-routed MoE with 4 experts, deterministic routing (token_id % 4) plus learned hybrid router (LearnedHashRouter: Linear(H,E) micro-router). PID dynamics traversing all layers with tight clamping. SwiGLU activation. Cosine warm restarts (SGDR) LR schedule. Updated to 11 layers, 2x MLP expansion, 26.5M params estimated ~14.2MB with int6+zstd.
hardware_notes: No training run completed; compute credits pending
key_findings: Draft submission proposing a novel combination of deterministic token-routed MoE with PID-based layer dynamics and a hybrid learned/modulo router, but no val_bpb reported as training has not been completed due to lack of compute credits. Generated with Claude Code.

title: [Non-record] Golomb-Rice Optimal Encoding for Error Correction Tables
technique_details: Replaces varint-delta encoding with Golomb-Rice coding (optimal for geometric distributions), achieving 8-9% savings in bytes-per-entry for error correction tables. Near-Shannon-optimal compression. Merged as a Future Optimization section into PR #108/#232.
hardware_notes: No GPU needed for benchmarking
key_findings: Golomb-Rice coding achieves near-Shannon-optimal compression for error correction tables, saving ~8% over varint-delta encoding, but was folded into the main error correction table PR rather than submitted independently.

title: [Non-record] Eval-time Adaptation: Stride-OGD + Two-Pass + NTK-RoPE
technique_details: Stride-OGD: online gradient descent on 1024-dim vocab bias updated every 64-token stride, exact gradient without backprop, 16x faster than TTT LoRA. Two-Pass Eval: pass 1 collects per-token gradients, pass 2 re-scores with accumulated bias correction within 600s eval budget. NTK-RoPE 4096: eval at 4x context via RoPE base rescaling without retraining.
key_findings: Proposes three eval-time adaptation techniques including a lightweight Stride-OGD vocab bias update that claims 16x faster feedback than TTT LoRA, with working code provided but no competition-standard BPB scores reported.

title: [Non-record] Quantization Findings: SWA Reversal + Int5 Failure
technique_details: Finding 1: SWA over 84 checkpoints reverses quantization gap — int6+zstd roundtrip BPB (1.5164) is lower than pre-quant BPB (1.5536), gap = -0.037, because SWA smoothing eliminates quantization-sensitive outliers. Finding 2: Mixed int5/int6 quant gap explodes from 0.3 to 1.4 BPB (4.5x increase), making int5 catastrophic for undertrained models.
key_findings: Documents two important quantization findings: SWA can eliminate the quantization penalty entirely by smoothing outlier weights, while int5 quantization is catastrophic for undertrained models with a 4.5x larger quant gap than int6.

title: Draft: 6-bit QAT + SP4096 + Layer Recurrence 5×2, requesting compute
technique_details: 6-bit QAT with STE fake-quantization of 2D weight matrices; 5 unique layers shared across 2 passes (U-Net style) for 10 effective layers; SP4096 BPE tokenizer; Muon for 2D matrices + Adam for embeddings/scalars; 1200-step warmdown.
hardware_notes: Awaiting 8×H100 compute — no run completed yet
key_findings: Proposes layer recurrence (weight sharing across 5×2 passes) combined with 6-bit QAT and a 4096-vocab SentencePiece tokenizer to double effective depth without doubling parameters. No BPB results yet; awaiting compute grant.

title: Add sliding-window eval to MLX trainer
technique_details: Adds EVAL_STRIDE and EVAL_BATCH_SEQS parameters to the MLX trainer; adds GPT.forward_logits for per-token NLL scoring over overlapping windows; includes focused unit tests for sliding window coverage.
hardware_notes: Apple Silicon (MLX trainer); smoke test run locally
key_findings: Infrastructure PR adding sliding-window evaluation support to the MLX (Apple Silicon) trainer, mirroring the evaluation strategy already used in the main competition trainer. No model submission or BPB score.

title: Draft: SOTA+ TTT + RoPE50K + EMA + Curriculum (pending H100 run)
technique_details: Built on PR #198 stack (11L 512d, SmearGate, BigramHash, OrthoInit, mixed int6/int8, zstd-22); adds RoPE base 50K, LAWA-EMA (decay=0.995) replacing SWA, context-length curriculum (seq1024 for 60% then seq2048), and full-model SGD TTT (1 epoch, lr=3e-4) on val data before scoring.
hardware_notes: 8×H100 SXM run pending; only CPU smoke test completed
key_findings: Draft targeting sub-1.13 BPB by layering RoPE base 50K, EMA, context-length curriculum, and test-time training (SGD on val data) on top of the current #1 stack. No actual run completed; expected BPB range 1.125–<1.10 depending on TTT effectiveness.

title: Record: WD + FP16 Embed + Warmdown (val_bpb=TBD)

hardware_notes: 8xH100 (RunPod)
key_findings: Incremental record attempt combining three low-risk improvements (weight decay, FP16 embedding, extended warmdown) with estimated combined BPB improvement of 0.008-0.015; no actual BPB score reported yet as training was planned.

Architecture candidates include cross-layer weight sharing, GQA, SwiGLU+RoPE, hybrid transformer+linear attention, int8 quantization+magnitude pruning. Training runs described as in progress.

title: Depth Recurrence: 3x3x1024 (non-record, pending H100)

hardware_notes: Only local Apple Silicon results available; H100 run pending

title: [WIP] wallclock-aware context curriculum

hardware_notes: Waiting on compute credits; no H100 result yet

Expected ~1.14 val_bpb. PR flagged for exceeding 1500-line script limit.

title: Experiment and [WIP] record attempt: Weight decay reduces quantization gap and artifact size
technique_details: Experimental investigation showing weight decay statistically significantly reduces pre-vs-post quantization BPB gap and compressed artifact size (6-seed paired one-sided t-tests). Mechanism: Muon steepest descent under spectral norm + weight decay constrains singular values, making weights more compressible. WIP record attempt with deeper model pending H100 access.
hardware_notes: Preliminary results on A100s; no 8xH100 access at time of submission
key_findings: Provides experimental and theoretical evidence that weight decay improves compressibility of quantized weights by constraining singular values, enabling larger models within the 16MB artifact budget; full record attempt pending H100 access.

title: Refactor model with BioDNA and FractalLinear classes :))
technique_details: Replaces all Linear layers with a central BioDNA hypernetwork that procedurally generates weights per layer based on depth and function. Exploits matrix associativity (X @ c) @ r.T to avoid materializing full weight matrices. Claims <10MB genotype that can grow arbitrary width/depth models.
key_findings: Experimental hypernetwork approach where a central BioDNA module generates all layer weights on the fly; no validated BPB score and labeled not ready for review, with significant theoretical claims unverified.

U-Net skip connections at loop level. MTP auxiliary heads excluded from artifact. Int8 per-row quantization + zlib compression.

title: Fix: score final partial window in sliding window eval
technique_details: Bug fix: changes `>= stride` to `>= 1` in window filter and uses `max(wlen - stride, 0)` to prevent negative indexing, ensuring the final partial window is scored rather than silently dropped.
key_findings: Infrastructure bug fix for sliding window evaluation that was silently dropping up to stride-1 tokens in the final window; maintainer confirmed addition to leaderboard.

title: Non-record: Muon-Aware QAT + LAWA + Adaptive LR Scheduling (7 toggleable improvements)
technique_details: 7 toggleable improvements: (1) Muon-aware QAT with STE and Gaussian noise modes, late activation at 75% training, 50% LR reduction on activation, targets matrices >65K params only; (2) LAWA (Latest Weight Averaging) of late-stage checkpoints; (3) LR floor at 10%; (4) wall-clock-aware warmdown fraction; (5) sequence length warmup 256→1024; (6) adaptive zstd/Brotli compression; (7) higher LRs for matrix/scalar/embed. Int6 + MLP 3x expansion planned.
hardware_notes: Pending 8xH100 verification
key_findings: Novel Muon-aware QAT design addressing how Muon's orthogonalization amplifies discrete rounding noise compared to Adam; combines with LAWA for complementary quant-robustness. No BPB result yet as 8xH100 verification is pending.

title: Add Psionic non-record runtime replay submission

hardware_notes: Not 8xH100; local verification only
key_findings: Non-record submission using a prebuilt Rust runtime; author subsequently indicated it was an accidental automated submission and asked maintainers to ignore it.

title: Fix: score final partial window in sliding window eval
technique_details: Bug fix for sliding window eval from PR #50: changed window filter from >= stride to >= 1, and clamped wlen - stride to max(wlen - stride, 0) to avoid negative indexing on short final windows.
key_findings: Fixes a silent bug in sliding window evaluation where the final partial window (up to stride-1 tokens) was dropped, potentially biasing the BPB metric.

title: felix_v1: int6+zstd, fp16 embed, MLP 3x, seq4096, sliding window eval
technique_details: Int6 quantization (QUANT_MAX=31) in int8 containers with zstd level-22 compression; fp16 embedding passthrough skipping quantization; MLP_MULT=3; TRAIN_SEQ_LEN=4096; sliding window eval with stride=512. Estimates body ~14.1MB and embedding ~0.5MB for ~14.6MB total artifact.
hardware_notes: Draft — awaiting H100 training run; local validation on Apple Silicon M4 Pro
key_findings: Draft submission stacking int6+zstd, fp16 embeddings, 3x MLP, and seq4096 training to maximize parameter efficiency within the 16MB limit; no final BPB reported yet as H100 run is pending.

title: Non-record: Looped Transformer + LoRA + Skip Connections + NorMuon + SWA + Int6 + Sliding Window
technique_details: 5 unique transformer blocks looped 30x (6x depth multiplier) with per-virtual-layer rank-4 LoRA on Q,V projections; encoder-decoder skip connections (15 encoder + 15 decoder layers); learned residual mixing (resid_mix); NorMuon optimizer; wallclock-aware warmdown; SWA over 7 checkpoints; seq_len=4096; int6+fp16 embedding export (~14.9MB). Official 8xH100 run pending compute grant. Bot flagged bug: lm_head.weight not included in optimizer when TIE_EMBEDDINGS=0.
hardware_notes: Prior runs on 1xH100; 8xH100 official run pending compute grant
key_findings: Novel looped transformer with per-virtual-layer LoRA and encoder-decoder skip connections to address the failure of naive weight sharing; projects ~1.07-1.12 BPB on 8xH100 but no validated score yet.

title: Depth Recurrence 5x3 d672 SwiGLU
technique_details: 5 unique transformer blocks reused 3x for 15 effective layers at width 672; SwiGLU MLP replacing relu-squared at matched parameter count (~16.5M); eval at seqlen 2048; warmdown, momentum warmup, and grad clip tuned for shorter step budget. H100 run pending.
hardware_notes: Pending 8xH100 run
key_findings: Depth recurrence trades training steps for effective compute per token; with the baseline massively over-trained relative to Chinchilla, 15 effective layers at d=672 via 5x3 recurrence is hypothesized to be more efficient, but no validated BPB reported.

title: Submission: Top-Heavy FFN Allocation + Packed Int6 Export | pending eval

Eval pending compute access.
hardware_notes: No GPU credits; pending 8xH100 run; dry-run verified on CPU
key_findings: Proposes top-heavy FFN allocation (later layers get larger FFNs) motivated by proximity to next-token loss, implemented as a controlled variation on the proven 9x512 backbone; no validated BPB yet as compute is pending.

title: Keep tied embeddings in fp32
technique_details: Keeps tok_emb.weight as fp32 master parameter in CUDA and MLX trainers, casting to bf16 only at compute time, aligning tied embeddings with existing fp32-master treatment for linear weights. Smoke test shows marginal pre- and post-quant BPB improvement.
hardware_notes: Tested locally on Apple Silicon (MLX path), not on 8xH100.
key_findings: Aligning tied embedding training to use fp32 master weights (like linear layers) yields a small but consistent improvement in both pre-quant and post-quant BPB in local smoke tests. PR was opened by an automated agent without author confirmation.

title: fix(data): normalize cached FineWeb paths

title: Value Residual + Gated Attention + XSA + EMA + AdamW TTT — val_bpb pending H100
technique_details: SwiGLU replaces ReLU² with 2/3 hidden scaling (hidden=938). Value Residual connects raw token embeddings (v0) to all layers via learned scalar λ init=0. Gated Attention uses per-layer learned scalar gate init=1. XSA on last 4 layers. EMA decay=0.9999 updated every 10 steps. AdamW TTT: 3 epochs over validation tokens, bottom 6 layers frozen, weights restored after eval. Smoke run confirmed 11.9MB artifact.
hardware_notes: 1xH100 smoke run only; awaiting 8xH100 credits for full validation.
key_findings: Stacks SwiGLU, Value Residual, Gated Attention, XSA, EMA, and AdamW TTT on a competitive base; notable for using AdamW (vs SGD) for TTT with bottom-layer freezing, but no H100 results yet.

title: Record: 10L QAT + SwiGLU + BigramHash(10240) (val_bpb=TBD)
technique_details: STE-based QAT during training with int5 clip for MLP, int6 clip for attention to eliminate quantization gap. SwiGLU replaces ReLU², parameter-matched to 2/3 × mlp_mult × model_dim. BigramHash(10240). SmearGate. SWA start_frac=0.4. OrthoInit + muP-scaled projections. Pending 3-seed validation on 8xH100.
key_findings: Combines QAT (eliminating quantization gap at inference) with SwiGLU activation on a 10-layer stack; BPB score not yet available as 3-seed validation was still pending at submission time.

title: [Non-record] ParGolf-Zero v2 — 6-layer joint optimization + BigramHash + SWA + zstd
technique_details: Joint compression-aware training with per-row weight range penalty to minimize Int8 quantization error during every gradient step. BigramHash(10240), SWA, zstd-22, TTT combined. Tested on Kaggle T4; awaiting H100 for scored run.
hardware_notes: Confirmed on Kaggle T4; awaiting H100 compute grant for final scored run
key_findings: Proposes joint compression-aware training where quantization error is minimized during gradient steps rather than post-hoc. No final BPB score yet as H100 compute is pending.

title: [WIP] Cross-Layer Reciprocal Basis Sharing (CRBS)
technique_details: Documentation-only WIP PR describing Cross-Layer Reciprocal Basis Sharing (CRBS) concept including core ideas, compression strategy, and expected gains. No implementation or results provided.
key_findings: Draft documentation submission introducing CRBS, a proposed cross-layer weight sharing/compression concept; no code or empirical results are included.

title: (Nonrecord) Async Prefetching
technique_details: Infrastructure improvement: asynchronous prefetching of training and validation batches using background threads and dedicated CUDA copy streams to overlap CPU data prep and host-to-device transfers with GPU compute. Adds timing diagnostics via TRAINING_TIMING_BREAKDOWN env var.
key_findings: Async prefetching yields modest step-count gains (1191 vs 1137 steps in 600s locally), but timing analysis shows CPU+H2D is only ~5ms per optimizer step vs ~800ms compute, so the optimization has minimal impact when compute is the bottleneck on large GPU configurations.

title: Modified

title: Hybrid Hypergraph + Transformer approach
technique_details: Two-phase training: Phase 1 builds a multi-resolution hypergraph pattern store (bigrams, trigrams, 5-grams) using binding energy filtering; Phase 2 trains a residual transformer. Final prediction interpolates hypergraph and neural outputs via confidence-weighted lambda.
key_findings: Proposes generalizing BigramHash to a principled multi-resolution pattern store using binding energy to filter noise, combined with a residual transformer. No GPU validation on real FineWeb data was completed before submission.

title: 5 novel architecture ablations on SOTA baseline

key_findings: Proposes five targeted ablations on the SOTA baseline covering techniques not yet explored in the competition, but no experimental results are included — this is a draft ablation plan only.

title: submission: XSA-all + Soft-Round QAT + Full GPTQ + MLP 3.5x + AdamW TTT
technique_details: XSA on all 11 layers (vs 4 in base). Soft-Round QAT with tanh-based differentiable rounding (alpha annealed 1→16). Full GPTQ with Hessian-aware column-reordered Cholesky error compensation. MHA 8/8 replacing GQA 8/4. MLP 3.5x expansion (1792 hidden). BigramHash 8192 buckets. AdamW TTT with grouped LR and cosine schedule. Early QAT threshold 0.5. Selective ±1 magnitude pruning.
key_findings: Combines multiple aggressive improvements over PR #549 SOTA including full-layer XSA, Soft-Round QAT, and AdamW TTT, but was flagged by maintainer as missing submission.json/val_bpb and warned about GPTQ calibration potentially accessing training data at eval time.

title: Add BigramHash module: lightweight n-gram logit bias via hash embedding
technique_details: Standalone BigramHash module using Knuth multiplicative hash to map bigram pairs (t_{i-1}, t_i) into a learned logit bias table (vocab_size × table_size). Zero-initialized to be a no-op at training start. Proposes int4 quantization of the table to fit within artifact budget. No training results reported; presented as exploratory/infrastructure contribution.
key_findings: Proposes a standalone BigramHash module as a logit bias (not embedding input), with plans to ablate table sizes and integrate with the current SOTA stack; no empirical results provided yet.

title: Non-record: Hybrid SSM Scene Overview

key_findings: Negative result: SSM layers are not competitive under the 10-minute training budget due to higher time complexity compared to optimized attention kernels, making them impractical for this competition format.

title: feat: depth recurrence + cosine recovery TTT
technique_details: Depth recurrence repeats layers 4-5 to create 13 virtual layers from 11 physical layers, with per-repetition learnable scale parameters and adapted U-Net skip connections. Enhanced TTT adds a cosine recovery phase post-scoring: N additional cosine-LR epochs on all scored data to repair int6 quantization damage, then re-scores. FlashAttention 3 with SDPA fallback for non-Hopper GPUs.
hardware_notes: Smoke-tested on 1xH100 SXM 80GB only; no 8xH100 results.
key_findings: Introduces depth recurrence (virtual layer repetition) and a novel post-TTT cosine recovery phase to repair int6 quantization damage; smoke-tested only with no BPB results reported.

GTX 1650 compatibility patches via env-var-gated NO_COMPILE and math SDP fallback.
hardware_notes: NVIDIA GTX 1650 (4 GB VRAM, SM 7.5, Turing, Windows 11) — non-competition hardware, 200-step ablation runs only

title: SwiGLU MLP: parameter-neutral gated activation over LeakyReLU^2
technique_details: Parameter-neutral swap of LeakyReLU(0.5)² for SwiGLU by reshaping mlp_up_bank from (1536,512) to (2048,512) and mlp_down_bank from (512,1536) to (512,1024), preserving total parameter count. Built on PR #549 SOTA stack.
hardware_notes: RunPod 8×H100 (training logs pending)
key_findings: Proposes a parameter-neutral activation swap from LeakyReLU(0.5)² to SwiGLU on the PR #549 SOTA stack; results are pending as training is still in progress.

key_findings: Draft placeholder PR containing code snapshots for three technique combinations, submitted to obtain a reviewable link for a compute credit application; final metrics and H100 logs are not yet included.

title: Non-record: Negative results — hardware alignment & quantization on 8xH100
technique_details: 30+ experiments on 11L d=512 stack (PR #593 baseline, 1.1171 BPB). Tested: CUTLASS SM90 kernels, fused Triton GEMM, FP8, QKV fusion, SpinQuant/Hadamard rotation, mixed Int5/Int8, Soft-Round QAT, selective pruning, XSA-all-layers, Value Residual Learning, Gated Attention, larger d=576+Int5, shard ordering, Legal TTT (22 experiments). All negative or marginal.
hardware_notes: All experiments on 8×H100 SXM, PyTorch 2.9.1+cu128, CUDA 12.8
key_findings: Comprehensive negative-results report covering kernel engineering (CUTLASS, Triton, FP8, QKV fusion all failed), quantization variants, and architecture changes; key insight is that torch.compile already optimizes the training step to 95%+ efficiency and the competition margin lies in quantization quality, not kernel speed.
